source.subject: MAP: last-mile commitment & signature timing for evaluation -> production
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Julie Park <julie@acuityhealth.com>, Matt Li <matt.li@acuityhealth.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: MAP & timeline for evaluation -> production (Acuity)

Hi Julie / Matt,

Attaching the updated mutual action plan (MAP_v3_Acuity.pdf) and the SOW (SOW_Acuity_v1.docx). The goal here is to pin the final approval path and signature timing so we can lock a start date for the pilot. High-level ask: can Acuity commit to a signature date no later than 2026-09-15? That gives us the security and procurement lead time to meet an early-October start.

Key items in this MAP:
- Evaluation plan: 2-week technical evaluation (canary) followed by a 4-week scoped pilot.
- Success criteria: latency P95 < 350ms on 8k token workload; cost targets scoped in SOW section 3.
- Approvals: Procurement (Matt Li) -> Legal -> Executive sign-off (Acuity)
- Redwood deliverables: environment onboarding, test dataset ingestion, 24/7 on-call during canary.

Please review and tell us:
1) Does the approval chain above match your internal flow?
2) Any hard blockers on the 2026-09-15 signature target?
3) Preferred signer (name & email) for DocuSign?

If we get confirmation this week we’ll send a DocuSign packet and schedule the onboarding kickoff for the week of 2026-10-04.

Attachments: MAP_v3_Acuity.pdf (pdf), SOW_Acuity_v1.docx (docx)

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference for LLMs
From: Julie Park <julie@acuityhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Cc: Acuity Health Procurement <procurement@acuityhealth.com>, Matt Li <matt.li@acuityhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 14:03:12 -04:00
Subject: Re: MAP & timeline for evaluation -> production (Acuity)

Hi Ava — thanks for sending. The MAP looks mostly aligned; a few procurement/legal questions before we can commit to a signature date:

- Procurement needs a sample invoice and our typical payment terms (Net30 vs Net45) clarified.
- Security wants to confirm data retention and audit log retention in the SOW (can you point to the clause?).
- We have a scheduled Executive review on 2026-09-13; if we need signatures by 09-15 that is doable but tight for any redlines coming out of that meeting.

I’m looping in procurement (procurement@acuityhealth.com) who will share a requirements matrix. If Redwood can confirm the following today it will help:
1) Where in the SOW is the retention/audit clause (section #?).
2) Can you provide a sample invoice and a statement on whether Net30 is the default?
3) Do you have a preferred signer email for DocuSign?

We attached our procurement checklist for reference (proc_requirements.xlsx).

Best,
Julie Park
Director, Data Strategy — Acuity Health
julie@acuityhealth.com
From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.ai>
To: Julie Park <julie@acuityhealth.com>, procurement@acuityhealth.com, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Matt Li <matt.li@acuityhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 27 Aug 2026 11:20:45 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP & timeline for evaluation -> production (Acuity)

Julie — quick technical clarifications to help the procurement/security review:

- Retention & audit logs: see SOW section 5.2 (Data retention & auditability). We commit to configurable retention windows; default is 90 days for KV/cache and 365 days for audit logs. We can update to match Acuity policy as needed — just indicate desired retention in the procurement matrix.
- Invoice / billing: Redwood issues monthly invoices based on usage + fixed reserved capacity line items. Net30 is standard unless otherwise negotiated. We can provide a sample invoice PDF; attaching a response matrix that maps your procurement checklist to SOW clauses (MAP_response_matrix.xlsx).
- Evaluation cadence: Our recommended approach for a clean go/no-go:
  1) 2-week canary (non-prod traffic, production routing with throttles) — technical acceptance tests (latency, correctness) run daily.
  2) 4-week pilot scoped to X product calls/day with agreed QoE prompts and monitoring.
  3) Post-pilot exec review for sign-off -> roll to production.

If you can target 2026-09-15 for signature, we will prioritize the canary kickoff for the week of 2026-10-04. If approvals slip beyond 09-22, we should expect a 2–3 week slip in the start date.

Attachments: MAP_response_matrix.xlsx (xlsx)

Happy to hop on a 30-min call if that helps accelerate procurement questions.

Thanks,
Dev Patel
Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
dev@redwood.ai
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Julie Park <julie@acuityhealth.com>, procurement@acuityhealth.com, Matt Li <matt.li@acuityhealth.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 2 Sep 2026 16:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Final go/no-go & signature flow — next steps (DocuSign enclosed)

Julie / Matt / Procurement team — following up to close the loop and lock the signature cadence. Summary and explicit requests below so nothing gets lost between teams: 

Summary:
- Target signature date: 2026-09-15 (Acuity Exec review on 2026-09-13 makes this feasible).
- Target onboarding/kickoff: week of 2026-10-04 (2-week canary -> 4-week pilot).
- Approval chain: Procurement (Matt Li) -> Acuity Legal (legal@acuityhealth.com) -> Exec sign (name TBD).

Requested items from Acuity (please reply inline):
1) Confirmation of signature authority & preferred signer email for DocuSign (we will route to that address).
2) Confirmation that Net30 billing terms are acceptable or provide preferred terms.
3) Any required changes to SOW section 5.2 (data retention) — otherwise we will proceed with the defaults (90/365).

I’ve uploaded the final MAP for signature: final_map_for_signature.pdf and created a DocuSign packet here: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=abcd-ef01-2345

If procurement confirms the matrix by 2026-09-10 we’ll send the DocuSign packet the same day and target the 2026-09-15 signature window. If you prefer, we can schedule a 20-minute call Friday to run through the checklist live.

Thanks again — we’re excited to get Acuity into a production-safe canary and prove the latency/cost economics quickly.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: final_map_for_signature.pdf (pdf)

--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference for LLMs

source.subject: Controls handoff and FedRAMP scope questions
messages:
From: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>
Date: 2026-08-30T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the intake call this morning. Two quick items we need to lock before the evidence sprint:

1) FedRAMP scope — you mentioned earlier that logging for Dedicated customers is retained in a customer-controlled bucket. Can you confirm whether Redwood will include audit logs for Dedicated vs Hosted in the same evidence set, or should we treat them separately for control sampling?

2) ISO control owner list — could you provide a single spreadsheet with control owners, evidence paths, and expected retention windows (especially for SOC 2 / SIG / CAIQ crosswalks)? If you have a draft, a shared Drive link is fine.

3) Timing — BoxCorp is targeting a production cutoff for new integrations 10/15; we need to know when the control evidence will be available for sampling so we can schedule auditors.

If it's easier, a short mapping (owner, location, link) is perfect.

Best,
Kelly
Kelly Tran
Sr. Auditor, SecureAudit
[Attachment: standard-intake-checklist.pdf]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-08-31T10:07:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Team — forwarding Kelly's asks (see below). Quick proposed division of labor so we can reply to them tomorrow AM:

- Marcus: confirm how we want to present Dedicated vs Hosted logs (single evidence set with annotations vs separate sampling). Note: Dedicated customers have customer-controlled buckets with KMS; Hosted logs live in our central logging cluster.
- Rachel: assemble the control-owner spreadsheet (owner, slack/email, evidence path, retention) and mark any controls with 3P attestations (SaaS providers we rely on). Start with SOC 2 + ISO crosswalk and then SIG/CAIQ columns.
- Laura: estimate earliest deliverable date for the mapping and a realistic window for auditors to sample (consider our internal blackout windows). Also flag controls that would require engineering time to pull snapshots.

I will draft the reply to Kelly and attach the spreadsheet (we can iterate). Please add comments to the Drive file by EOD so we can send consolidated.

Thanks — Ava
Ava Chen
Head of GTM Security, Redwood Inference
[Internal note: Drive draft created: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2bControlsMapping]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-01T08:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Hi Kelly / Sam —

Thanks for the clear asks. Summary below and attached is a first-pass mapping (controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx) and a draft FedRAMP scope PDF. Highlights:

1) Logging evidence sets
- Dedicated (customer-managed buckets): we'll provide a consolidated manifest that shows the bucket name, KMS key id, and a pointer to the stored audit logs. For sampling, please treat Dedicated customers separately — we will label evidence as "Dedicated" vs "Hosted" in the mapping so you can select the sample accordingly.

2) Control owner spreadsheet
- Attached spreadsheet includes control owners, primary contact, evidence path (Drive links or S3 manifest), expected retention (months), and whether there is a 3P attestation. Columns include SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / SIG / CAIQ crosswalks. Rachel created comments on rows where we anticipate engineering extraction.

3) Timing for BoxCorp sampling
- Earliest complete evidence handoff: 2026-09-10 for scoped controls that are already instrumented.
- Controls requiring engineering snapshots: target handoff 2026-09-24.
- If you need auditors on site / synchronous sampling windows, propose dates 2026-09-28 to 2026-10-06.

4) Outstanding items for us to confirm
- Laura will confirm any internal blackout windows during the proposed sampling window.
- We will provide a short README in the Drive link explaining the "Hosted" vs "Dedicated" labeling.

Attachments: controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx, fedramp-scope-draft.pdf

Let us know if this approach works or if you want the Dedicated/Hosted evidence combined into one manifest (we can do that, but for audit sampling we find separate labeling reduces ambiguity).

Best,
Ava Chen
Head of GTM Security, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx]
[Attachment: fedramp-scope-draft.pdf]

From: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>
Date: 2026-09-02T15:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Ava —

Thanks, this is exactly what we needed. Quick clarifications after I scanned the spreadsheet:

- On control AC-6 (access reviews) the evidence path points to a shared Drive folder but I couldn't find the last quarterly review report. Can you point me to the file name?
- For retention windows, there are a few items with "TBD" — can Rachel flag whether those are policy gaps or simply missing metadata so we can annotate the sample plan.

If you can drop the small updates by 9/07 we can lock the audit schedule for the 9/28 start.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
Kelly
Sr. Auditor, SecureAudit
notes: Thread contains mixed external + internal coordination around evidence handoff for FedRAMP and ISO mapping.
All internal participants are employees listed under sources/gmail/.
Attachments are referenced as stubs per content rules.
Message ordering is oldest -> newest and includes realistic quoting and short internal action items.

- Rachel: assemble the control-owner spreadsheet (owner, slack/email, evidence path, retention) and mark any controls with 3P attestations (SaaS providers we rely on). Start with SOC 2 + ISO crosswalk and then SIG/CAIQ columns.
- Laura: estimate earliest deliverable date for the mapping and a realistic window for auditors to sample (consider our internal blackout windows). Also flag controls that would require engineering time to pull snapshots.

I will draft the reply to Kelly and attach the spreadsheet (we can iterate). Please add comments to the Drive file by EOD so we can send consolidated.

Thanks — Ava

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>

Date: 2026-09-01T08:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Hi Kelly / Sam —

Thanks for the clear asks. Summary below and attached is a first-pass mapping (controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx) and a draft FedRAMP scope PDF. Highlights:

1) Logging evidence sets
- Dedicated (customer-managed buckets): we'll provide a consolidated manifest that shows the bucket name, KMS key id, and a pointer to the stored audit logs. For sampling, please treat Dedicated customers separately — we will label evidence as \"Dedicated\" vs \"Hosted\" in the mapping so you can select the sample accordingly.

2) Control owner spreadsheet
- Attached spreadsheet includes control owners, primary contact, evidence path (Drive links or S3 manifest), expected retention (months), and whether there is a 3P attestation. Columns include SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / SIG / CAIQ crosswalks. Rachel created comments on rows where we anticipate engineering extraction.

3) Timing for BoxCorp sampling
- Earliest complete evidence handoff: 2026-09-10 for scoped controls that are already instrumented.
- Controls requiring engineering snapshots: target handoff 2026-09-24.
- If you need auditors on site / synchronous sampling windows, propose dates 2026-09-28 to 2026-10-06.

4) Outstanding items for us to confirm
- Laura will confirm any internal blackout windows during the proposed sampling window.
- We will provide a short README in the Drive link explaining the \"Hosted\" vs \"Dedicated\" labeling.

Attachments: controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx, fedramp-scope-draft.pdf

Let us know if this approach works or if you want the Dedicated/Hosted evidence combined into one manifest (we can do that, but for audit sampling we find separate labeling reduces ambiguity).

Best,
Ava Chen

Redwood Inference

- For retention windows, there are a few items with "TBD" — can Rachel flag whether those are policy gaps or simply missing metadata so we can annotate the sample plan.

Kelly

source.subject: Quick demo & intake questions — Marketly signup
messages:
From: Timothy Park <tim@marketly.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T15:22:00Z
Subject: Interested in Redwood — quick demo?

Hi Ava,

Thanks — we just signed up for a trial via the self-serve flow (Marketly account: ops@marketly.ai). I'd love to get a short walkthrough of the product and see whether Redwood is a fit for our embedding + assistant use case.

Super-brief background:
- Use case: customer support knowledge base search + assistant (embeddings + reranking).
- Team: AI platform + product (2 engineers, 1 ML infra).
- Timeline: hoping to kick a pilot in early September and evaluate for a Q4 production push.

Can someone on your team send available times next week? Also curious whether you provide a sandbox with a small sample dataset so our engineers can validate latency and output quality.

Thanks — Tim

--
Timothy Park
Head of AI, Marketly
ops@marketly.ai
https://marketly.ai
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Timothy Park <tim@marketly.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T17:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — quick demo?

Hi Tim — great to meet you and glad you signed up. I'm Ava on the solutions team; I'll run point for the demo and intake. A couple quick questions so we tailor the session and make the time valuable:

1) Primary objective: is the priority retrieval relevance (search) or multi-turn assistant behavior? If both, which is higher priority?
2) Estimated monthly volume: approximate API requests or token counts you expect during a pilot and at production scale?
3) Peak concurrency: any SLA/latency targets we should know about (e.g., sub-100ms p50 for short queries)?
4) Timeline: you mentioned early September pilot — is that a two-week pilot or longer evaluation window?
5) Security/compliance: do you need SSO (Okta/SAML), specific data residency, or KMS/HSM integrations for keys?
6) Sandbox: what data sample would be most useful (KB dump, CSV of tickets, vector index snapshot)?

Proposed slots for a 30-minute demo + intake call (PT):
- Thu Sep 2 — 11:00 AM PT
- Thu Sep 2 — 2:00 PM PT
- Fri Sep 3 — 10:00 AM PT

You can also pick a time directly from my calendar: https://cal.redwoodinference.com/ava/book

I've attached a short one-pager (redwood-one-pager.pdf) and a tiny intake form link (demo-intake-form-link.txt) to accelerate the technical prep — if you can fill the form ahead of the call we'll be able to surface a tailored demo.

If you prefer, I can loop in Ben (cc'd) for any deeper infra / throughput questions.

Best,
Ava
SDR, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
--
redwood-one-pager.pdf (attachment stub)
demo-intake-form-link.txt (attachment stub)
From: Timothy Park <tim@marketly.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-08-30T09:12:00Z
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — quick demo?

On Sun, Aug 29, 2026 at 17:05, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Tim — great to meet you and glad you signed up. I'm Ava on the solutions team; I'll run point for the demo and intake. A couple quick questions so we tailor the session and make the time valuable:
> 
> 1) Primary objective: is the priority retrieval relevance (search) or multi-turn assistant behavior? If both, which is higher priority?
> 2) Estimated monthly volume: approximate API requests or token counts you expect during a pilot and at production scale?
> 3) Peak concurrency: any SLA/latency targets we should know about (e.g., sub-100ms p50 for short queries)?
> 4) Timeline: you mentioned early September pilot — is that a two-week pilot or longer evaluation window?
> 5) Security/compliance: do you need SSO (Okta/SAML), specific data residency, or KMS/HSM integrations for keys?
> 6) Sandbox: what data sample would be most useful (KB dump, CSV of tickets, vector index snapshot)?
> 
> Proposed slots for a 30-minute demo + intake call (PT):
> - Thu Sep 2 — 11:00 AM PT
> - Thu Sep 2 — 2:00 PM PT
> - Fri Sep 3 — 10:00 AM PT

Thanks, Ava — answers below and happy to take the 11am PT slot on Sep 2.

- Primary objective: search relevance first (we want help surfacing exact KB sections), secondarily a short assistant for triage.
- Pilot volume: roughly 40k requests / month in pilot, ~1.2M requests / month at scale (ballpark).
- Tokens: pilot ~120k tokens/month, production ~3.5M tokens/month.
- Peak concurrency: expect short bursts up to ~250 concurrent requests during business hours.
- Timeline: target pilot kickoff the week of Sep 6 (2–4 week pilot), production evaluation through Q4.
- Security: Okta SSO required for console access; would like KMS support for customer-managed keys long term.
- Sandbox: a 10–15k ticket KB dump (CSV) would be ideal so our engineers can run latency checks; sample vector index also helpful.

Please go ahead and book Sep 2 at 11:00 AM PT. Also, Ben — can you confirm if a small sandbox index is something we can provision for Marketly ahead of the meeting?

—Tim
Timothy Park
Head of AI, Marketly
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Timothy Park <tim@marketly.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-01T10:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — quick demo?

Thanks Tim — that's really helpful detail. I've booked Thu Sep 2, 11:00 AM PT on my calendar and sent a calendar invite (calendar-invite.ics attached).

Next steps / pre-meeting checklist:
- Please complete the short intake form (linked in my previous email) if possible — it will save us 10–15 minutes during the call.
- Ben (cc) will review sandbox feasibility; if we can provision a small vector index we'll share a temporary API key and a short README with sample curl calls.
- We'll plan a 30-minute session: 10 min intake (confirm objectives + data), 15 min tailored demo, 5 min next steps (pilot plan + success metrics).

If you have any conflicts, let me know and I can swap to the 2:00 PM slot. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Ava
SDR, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
--
calendar-invite.ics (attachment stub)

If you have any conflicts, let me know and I can swap to the 2:00 PM slot. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Ava
SDR, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
--

source.subject: Re: Credit corridor and EU withholding clarity
messages:
From: Trevor Hayes <trevor@mediform.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: payments@mediform.com
Date: Thu, 20 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request to move to Net60 for Q3 invoices + VAT/withholding note

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the helpful demo last week. We wanted to circle back on payment terms for our pilot period. Our CFO is asking if Mediform can move to Net60 for the initial three invoices (Aug–Oct) while we smooth cashflow ahead of our Medicare billing cycle.

A few constraints from our side:
- We need a credit buffer (small reserve) while on extended terms.
- EU VAT handling: our AP team may withhold VAT on cross-border invoices unless we provide explicit VAT registration evidence.
- We can provide an upfront deposit to offset risk if that helps.

I've attached a proposed payment timeline (mediform_proposed_payment_timeline.xlsx). Can Redwood confirm whether Net60 is acceptable, and what documentation you need for VAT / withholding?

Best,
Trevor Hayes
VP Finance, Mediform Health

--- Attachment: mediform_proposed_payment_timeline.xlsx (spreadsheet) ---

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Trevor Hayes <trevor@mediform.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request to move to Net60 for Q3 invoices + VAT/withholding note

Trevor,

Appreciate the clear ask and the timeline. We're open to accommodating a temporary Net60 for the first three invoices with a couple of guardrails so both sides are covered. High level proposal:

- Net45 by default, but Net60 for Aug–Oct as a time-limited concession (three invoices).
- Mediform provides either a one-time deposit equal to 30% of the first invoice or a rolling credit buffer (we can size this after reviewing usage estimates).
- Redwood requests VAT registration evidence or a UK/EU VAT exemption certificate to avoid local withholding; if not available, we may need to gross-up invoice amounts to be VAT-neutral.
- We will place a conditional credit hold trigger at 45 days past due if the rolling buffer drops below threshold; automatic notification to your AP and our finance ops.

Can Marcus / Laura confirm internal approvals for the temporary Net60 + deposit option? Also attaching Redwood's short credit summary for context (redwood_credit_summary.pdf) and a VAT evidence checklist (vat_evidence_request.docx).

If this looks acceptable, we can draft an amendment to the MSA and a short mutual action plan to capture milestones and the deposit flow.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0142

--- Attachments: redwood_credit_summary.pdf, vat_evidence_request.docx ---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request to move to Net60 for Q3 invoices + VAT/withholding note

Ava / Laura,

I'm OK with the temporary Net60 concession under these conditions:
- Deposit equal to 25–30% of month-one invoice (we should ask for 30% given usage variability).
- A schedule for actual usage reconciliation at the end of the three-month window; any over/under will be credited or billed on the next invoice.
- VAT evidence required within 10 business days of signing the amendment. If not provided, we'll default to net amounts that assume potential withholding.
- Finance to enable a one-click payment link on our invoices and set up an early-pay discount (0.5%) if they pay within 10 days to encourage earlier settlement.

I'll green-light legal to prepare a short amendment with those points and add a clause that allows reverting to Net45 after the pilot unless both parties agree to continue Net60. Laura, please check SLA implications for the credit buffer.

Marcus Lin
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

--- Note: internal approval, no attachments ---

From: Trevor Hayes <trevor@mediform.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: payments@mediform.com, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Credit corridor and EU withholding clarity

Ava / Marcus,

Thanks — this is helpful and fair. Mediform agrees to:
- Provide a 30% deposit on the first invoice.
- Submit VAT registration evidence by 2026-09-10 (we'll upload to the Drive link you shared).
- Accept the reconciliation at the end of the three-month window; please include the early-pay link on invoices.

We'll sign the amendment once Laura's team confirms the SLA note in writing. I've attached a signed copy of the proposed timeline for reference.

Trevor Hayes
VP Finance, Mediform Health

--- Attachment: mediform_signed_timeline.pdf (PDF) ---

Ava / Laura,

I'm OK with the temporary Net60 concession under these conditions:
- Deposit equal to 25–30% of month-one invoice (we should ask for 30% given usage variability).
- A schedule for actual usage reconciliation at the end of the three-month window; any over/under will be credited or billed on the next invoice.

- Finance to enable a one-click payment link on our invoices and set up an early-pay discount (0.5%) if they pay within 10 days to encourage earlier settlement.

I'll green-light legal to prepare a short amendment with those points and add a clause that allows reverting to Net45 after the pilot unless both parties agree to continue Net60. Laura, please check SLA implications for the credit buffer.

Marcus Lin

source.subject: Atlas eval: prioritized throughput & p95/p99 action plan
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya@atlascloud.com>, Rahul Sharma <rahul@atlascloud.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Atlas eval: follow-up from today's call & suggested throughput tests

Hi Priya / Rahul,

Thanks for the time today — helpful to hear your SLOs and the key customer scenarios. Quick recap + a narrow set of tests we recommend to make the hyperscaler comparison defensible: 

Priority summary (from our call):
- Target latency SLO: p95 < 120ms for short chat turns (~64 tokens)
- Long-form background generation: p95 < 800ms for 512-token responses
- Cost sensitivity: prefer tests that reflect tokens/sec and per-token cost at 95th percentile

Proposed focused A/B tests (we’ll run Redwood hosted vs Hyperscaler A API using the same model family):
1) Short-seq single-client: 64-token prompts, 1000 warmup requests, 10k measured requests; measure p50/p95/p99, tokens/sec, tail latency under steady-state.
2) Long-seq burst: 512-token outputs with 20 concurrent connections to emulate parallel UX flows; measure throughput (tokens/sec) and p99 under bursts.
3) Batching sensitivity: run same short-seq at batch sizes [1, 4, 8] to show latency vs cost tradeoffs.
4) KV cache probe: for chat-like flows, measure p95 with and without prefix/KV caching to show savings in tokens/sec and latency.

Deliverables we’ll provide: test harness config, raw CSVs, per-route p50/p95/p99 plots, and a concise recommendation slide deck. Ben will own the lab run; Karthik will validate kernel/quantization parity vs Hyperscaler profiles.

Can you confirm the most realistic production request mix (ratio short:long:multi-turn) and any constraints on example prompts/PII so we can use representative prompts? Also confirm whether you want us to run tests in us-west or your eu region.

Attaching a short eval proposal workbook and the test instructions we plan to execute.

Next steps (proposed):
- Priya: confirm mix and region by EOD Wednesday 8/25
- Redwood (Ben/Karthik): lock test infra and start dry runs on 8/26
- Full A/B runs target week of 8/31

Thanks — happy to tighten scope if you prefer a smaller starter test.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: atlas-eval-proposal.xlsx, throughput-test-instructions.md
From: Priya Menon <priya@atlascloud.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Rahul Sharma <rahul@atlascloud.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Atlas eval: follow-up from today's call & suggested throughput tests

Thanks Ava — that plan looks good and nicely scoped. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Request mix: short:long:multi-turn = 60:20:20 (short = < 128 tokens, long = 256-512, multi-turn = 4–6 messages with short turn lengths).
2) Region: run tests in us-west first (we'll consider eu later).
3) Prompts: we will provide a sanitized set of 30 representative prompts (no PII) by end of day Tuesday 8/25.
4) Hyperscaler target: please include Hyperscaler A and Hyperscaler B (their managed LLM endpoints) in the comparison — both are what we currently evaluate.

Important callout: our procurement team will want clear p95/p99 snapshots and cost-per-1000-tokens for each run — the more reproducible the harness the better. Also, if you can include a short section that explains where Redwood's batching + KV cache impacts unit cost versus the Hyperscalers, that will be useful for our CFO.

We’re OK with the proposed schedule. Confirm if Redwood needs an IP or security checklist completed before we hand you the prompts.

Regards,
Priya Menon
Head of Platform
Atlas Cloud
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-23T08:42:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Atlas eval - infra & test notes (internal)

Looping internal notes here so we have an execution checklist. Priya asked for Hyperscaler A & B; we should be explicit about run parity. 

Execution checklist (Ben owner):
- Provision identical VM/GPU shapes in us-west for fairness (instances + network egress as close as possible) — target done by 8/25.
- Use same model weights (open/checksum) and tokenization to avoid model mismatch noise. Where Hyperscaler provides only API, instrument at request/response level to capture latency and token counts.
- Measurement: p50/p95/p99 (latency), tokens/sec, CPU/GPU util, and cost-per-1000-tokens estimated from run usage.
- Batching: implement batch plumbing with sizes [1,4,8] and fixed timeouts to reflect production routing.
- KV cache: two test modes (cold start vs warmed prefix cache). We'll include scripts to preload cache.

Karthik — can you validate that our quantized runtime path produces comparable output to the float baseline for the short test prompts? If the outputs diverge substantively we need to flag quality/regression risk to Priya.

Also: record the exact SDK versions, driver/kernel versions and attach logs so procurement has an audit trail.

Ben
Principal Performance Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya@atlascloud.com>, Rahul Sharma <rahul@atlascloud.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Atlas eval: prioritized throughput & p95/p99 action plan

Great — thanks Priya & team, we’ll proceed as discussed. Quick schedule + owners summarised below (final):

Confirmed scope:
- Regions: us-west only (initial)
- Competitors: Hyperscaler A & Hyperscaler B
- Test set: Redwood harness vs Hyperscaler APIs using the same sanitized prompt set you’ll provide

Schedule & owners:
- 8/25 EOD: Atlas shares sanitized prompts (Priya)
- 8/26: Infra provisioned & dry runs (Ben)
- 8/27–8/29: Full A/B runs + KV cache permutations (Ben / Redwood lab)
- 9/01: Initial results slide + raw CSVs delivered to Atlas; cost-per-1k token summary included (Ava)

Deliverables:
- CSVs with request-level latencies, token counts, and resource utilization
- p50/p95/p99 plots and tokens/sec charts for each route
- Short recommendation deck summarizing where Redwood’s batching & KV cache reduce unit cost/tail latency compared to Hyperscaler A/B

Security / procurement: please point us to the datasheet or checklist your procurement needs — we can complete the Redwood security questionnaire if required.

If anything changes to the schedule, we’ll flag it immediately. Looking forward to getting the prompts and running the tests.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Quoted: 
> On Aug 22, 2026, at 09:15, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Thanks for the time today — helpful to hear your SLOs...

Attachments: atlas-eval-proposal.xlsx, throughput-test-instructions.md

source.subject: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency
messages:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: timothy.holt@acmehealth.com
Date: 2026-08-20 09:12 -0700
Subject: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Team —

Monitoring alerted at 03:08 UTC to a sustained p95 tail latency increase for model alpha-9 serving on our hosted fleet. Summary: p95 has drifted from ~420ms baseline to 1.05s over the last 4 hours (traffic-weighted), with increased variance on longer prompts. The spike correlates with the alpha-9 rollout 02:45 UTC and a kernel change in the optimized attention path.

Quick findings (attachments included):
- 03:00–06:00 UTC: gradual ramp, 06:00–09:00 UTC: step increase coincident with a subset of requests hitting the new kv-cache path.
- Affected tenants: 12 customers in US region; Acme Health shows elevated client-side timeouts on 0.7% of requests (attached sample traces).
- Reproduction locally on a sample workload shows p95 ~1.1s vs alpha-8 p95 ~430ms.

Mitigation options (tradeoffs):
1) Immediate: "pin" affected tenants to alpha-8 variant (no model code change) and apply a temporary traffic cap for alpha-9 for those tenants (recommended cap: 60% routing to alpha-9). Low-risk for correctness, moderate customer-visible behavior change.
2) Rollback alpha-9 globally to alpha-8 (broad rollback). Fast but disruptive to rollout metrics and to other tenants already benefiting from alpha-9 improvements.
3) Kernel patch hotfix (engineering) — likely takes 6+ hours to validate and deploy.

Request: I need authorization from sales/ops leadership to enact option #1 for Acme Health and the most impacted tenants as an emergency mitigation while we investigate. This will temporarily alter routing (affects latency SLOs coverage and may trigger an SLA exception for impacted time window).

Attachments: alpha9-tail-traces.tar.gz, alpha9-profiles.csv

If approved, I will: (a) pin Acme Health to alpha-8, (b) set traffic cap to 60% for alpha-9 for the next 12 hours, (c) continue trace collection and produce a short hotfix plan.

Rishi
Rishi Malhotra, Senior SRE
Redwood Inference

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-20 09:34 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Thanks Rishi — this looks urgent. A couple quick Qs before I loop in legal/finance if needed:
- Can you confirm whether pinning to alpha-8 will change model outputs for any of Acme Health's anchored prompts (we need to avoid regressions for their clinical scoring flows)?
- Do we have a precise list of the 12 tenants (I only want to authorize for the small set that are showing client-side timeouts) — please paste IDs in a follow-up.
- I prefer option #1 if delta in outputs is minimal. Ben — can you pre-approve an emergency routing policy for Acme Health for a 2-hour mitigation window while we triage? We can extend if necessary but want an initial guardrail.

Ava Chen
Account Executive — Enterprise
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-20 10:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Approve option #1 for Acme Health and the other tenants with confirmed client-side timeouts. Conditions:
- Keep the initial window to 2 hours and notify the customer immediately (Lauren, please send a short note).
- If the mitigation is still required after 2 hours, escalate back to Ben/Finance for a formal SLA exception.

Ava — you have my sign-off for the emergency routing policy for ACME-2345 for the 2-hour mitigation window. Rishi — proceed with pin + 60% cap for those tenant IDs once you paste them.

Ben Carter
Sales — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: timothy.holt@acmehealth.com
Cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-20 10:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Timothy —

We're aware of increased tail latency impacting a small percentage of inference calls made by Acme Health this morning. Our engineering team has identified the likely cause and proposed a short mitigation that reduces exposure while they finish a fix. With your permission we'll:
- Temporarily route Acme Health traffic to the previous model variant for a short window, and
- Apply a conservative traffic cap to the new variant to reduce the chance of timeouts on your production calls.

We expect this to be a brief measure (initially 2 hours). Will this window work for your on-call/team? If you'd prefer we can schedule a similar maintenance window instead.

Apologies for the disruption — we'll follow up with a post-mortem and traces.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
lauren_bishop@redwood.com

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>

title: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency
preview:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: timothy.holt@acmehealth.com
Date: 2026-08-20 09:12 -0700
Subject: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Team —

Monitoring alerted at 03:08 UTC to a sustained p95 tail latency increase for model alpha-9 serving on our hosted fleet. Summary: p95 has drifted from ~420ms baseline to 1.05s over the last 4 hours (traffic-weighted), with increased variance on longer prompts. The spike correlates with the alpha-9 rollout 02:45 UTC and a kernel change in the optimized attention path.

Quick findings (attachments included):
- 03:00–06:00 UTC: gradual ramp, 06:00–09:00 UTC: step increase coincident with a subset of requests hitting the new kv-cache path.
- Affected tenants: 12 customers in US region; Acme Health shows elevated client-side timeouts on 0.7% of requests (attached sample traces).
- Reproduction locally on a sample workload shows p95 ~1.1s vs alpha-8 p95 ~430ms.

Mitigation options (tradeoffs):
1) Immediate: \"pin\" affected tenants to alpha-8 variant (no model code change) and apply a temporary traffic cap for alpha-9 for those tenants (recommended cap: 60% routing to alpha-9). Low-risk for correctness, moderate customer-visible behavior change.
2) Rollback alpha-9 globally to alpha-8 (broad rollback). Fast but disruptive to rollout metrics and to other tenants already benefiting from alpha-9 improvements.
3) Kernel patch hotfix (engineering) — likely takes 6+ hours to validate and deploy.

Request: I need authorization from sales/ops leadership to enact option #1 for Acme Health and the most impacted tenants as an emergency mitigation while we investigate. This will temporarily alter routing (affects latency SLOs coverage and may trigger an SLA exception for impacted time window).

Attachments: alpha9-tail-traces.tar.gz, alpha9-profiles.csv

If approved, I will: (a) pin Acme Health to alpha-8, (b) set traffic cap to 60% for alpha-9 for the next 12 hours, (c) continue trace collection and produce a short hotfix plan.

Rishi
Rishi Malhotra, Senior SRE
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-20 09:34 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Thanks Rishi — this looks urgent. A couple quick Qs before I loop in legal/finance if needed:
- Can you confirm whether pinning to alpha-8 will change model outputs for any of Acme Health's anchored prompts (we need to avoid regressions for their clinical scoring flows)?
- Do we have a precise list of the 12 tenants (I only want to authorize for the small set that are showing client-side timeouts) — please paste IDs in a follow-up.
- I prefer option #1 if delta in outputs is minimal. Ben — can you pre-approve an emergency routing policy for Acme Health for a 2-hour mitigation window while we triage? We can extend if necessary but want an initial guardrail.

Ava Chen
Account Executive — Enterprise
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0182


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-20 10:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Approve option #1 for Acme Health and the other tenants with confirmed client-side timeouts. Conditions:
- Keep the initial window to 2 hours and notify the customer immediately (Lauren, please send a short note).
- If the mitigation is still required after 2 hours, escalate back to Ben/Finance for a formal SLA exception.

Ava — you have my sign-off for the emergency routing policy for ACME-2345 for the 2-hour mitigation window. Rishi — proceed with pin + 60% cap for those tenant IDs once you paste them.

Ben Carter
Sales — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: timothy.holt@acmehealth.com
Cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-20 10:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: authorize emergency model pin & traffic cap for alpha-9 tail latency

Timothy —

We're aware of increased tail latency impacting a small percentage of inference calls made by Acme Health this morning. Our engineering team has identified the likely cause and proposed a short mitigation that reduces exposure while they finish a fix. With your permission we'll:
- Temporarily route Acme Health traffic to the previous model variant for a short window, and
- Apply a conservative traffic cap to the new variant to reduce the chance of timeouts on your production calls.

We expect this to be a brief measure (initially 2 hours). Will this window work for your on-call/team? If you'd prefer we can schedule a similar maintenance window instead.

Apologies for the disruption — we'll follow up with a post-mortem and traces.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
lauren_bishop@redwood.com

source.subject: Polaris POC: scheduling hands-on throughput dry run and prompt/routing review
source.thread:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Murphy <sarah.murphy@polarisanalytics.com>, Tim Cooke <tim.cooke@polarisanalytics.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Polaris POC: scheduling hands-on throughput dry run and prompt/routing review

Hi Sarah / Tim —

Thanks again for the time today. To keep momentum, we'd like to lock in a short POC kickoff that includes:
- 30m prompt + routing review (prompt sets, token budgets, expected truncation cases)
- 60m hands-on session (model routing rules + simple fallbacks)
- 60-90m load test / throughput dry run (sustained and burst checks, autoscaler behavior)

Proposed windows (all PT):
- Tue Aug 20: 09:00–12:00 or 13:00–16:00
- Wed Aug 21: 09:00–12:00 (preferred by our ops team)

We have a couple of artifacts attached: test-harness-specs.md (test scenarios + metrics collection) and polaris_poc_mpa.xlsx (mutual action plan). Can you confirm which date/time works for your team and whether you want us to run the load step on Hosted or Dedicated for the dry run?

Notes / goals: verify sustained throughput target, validate p95 latency under SLO at realistic token lengths, and exercise routing fallbacks.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai

----
From: Sarah Murphy <sarah.murphy@polarisanalytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tim Cooke <tim.cooke@polarisanalytics.com>, poc@polarisanalytics.com
Date: Sat, 15 Aug 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Polaris POC: scheduling hands-on throughput dry run and prompt/routing review

Hi Ava —

Appreciate the clear agenda. Wed Aug 21 09:00–12:00 PT works best for us. Please prioritize:
1) prompt review (we have a couple of long-system messages that might push tokens)
2) routing (we want to validate routing to a cheaper quantized variant when latency allows)
3) a focused throughput run to confirm our expected peak of ~300 req/s (burst to 450) with p95 latency goal <= 250ms for the 1k-token median case.

Also, could you attach your security checklist for the POC? Our compliance team will want to walk through data retention and audit logging during the session.

Thanks — Sarah
Sarah Murphy
Head of ML Ops | Polaris Analytics

----
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2026 11:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Polaris POC: scheduling hands-on throughput dry run and prompt/routing review

Looping in ops/infra for the load plan. High-level runbook notes we should cover during the prep:
- Reserve a 2-hour throughput window on Aug 21 starting 09:00 PT 
- Test harness: synthetic traffic (mix of short/plain and 1k-token median requests), warm prefixes to exercise KV cache
- Throughput targets: sustained 250 req/s with p95 < 250ms; burst to 450 req/s to see autoscaler behavior
- Failure modes: model fallback latency regressions, token truncation edge cases, and routing fallback correctness

Rishi — can you confirm available capacity (Dedicated vs Hosted) and whether we need to schedule an extra GPU pool reservation? Tessa — I'll need you to hold two hands-on slots (09:00 prompt review + 09:30 hands-on walk-through) and then hand off to ops for the 10:15 load window.

Connor
Sr. Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

----
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Polaris POC: scheduling hands-on throughput dry run and prompt/routing review

We can reserve capacity on Dedicated for the dry run. Quick specifics: 
- Reserved pool: 4 x A10g-equivalent (config for deterministic p95 behavior)
- Expected throughput: baseline sustained 260 req/s (typical 16–32 token responses), burst-tested to 480 req/s for short responses
- Observability: token-level metrics, per-route latency, and kv-cache hit rates will be live in the console
- Action: I'll create the run reservation and attach gcp_instance_list.csv with the nodes we've pinned.

If Polaris wants Hosted instead, we can run but the variance will be higher; Dedicated gives repeatable p95 figures which is what they asked for.

Rishi
Manager, Inference Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachment: gcp_instance_list.csv (instances pinned for Aug 21 window)

----
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Murphy <sarah.murphy@polarisanalytics.com>, Tim Cooke <tim.cooke@polarisanalytics.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, poc@polarisanalytics.com
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Polaris POC: scheduling hands-on throughput dry run and prompt/routing review

Sarah / Tim —

Confirmed: Wed Aug 21 09:00–12:00 PT. Agenda cadence:
- 09:00–09:30 prompt review + token budgeting
- 09:30–10:15 hands-on routing configuration and fallbacks
- 10:15–11:45 load test (sustained then burst; we'll step through scenarios in test-harness-specs.md)
- 11:45–12:00 quick next-steps and action items (rollout recommendations / quality checks)

Resources attached and links: polaris_poc_mpa.xlsx (mutual action plan), test-harness-specs.md (scenarios), runbook link: https://redwood.app/runbooks/polaris-poc-dryrun

Rishi has reserved a 4-node Dedicated pool for the window. We'll also share a short security checklist in a follow-up email (per Sarah's request) and drop a calendar invite in a few minutes.

See you Wed —
Ava
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Attachment stubs: test-harness-specs.md, polaris_poc_mpa.xlsx

source.subject: Next steps: integration & prompt safety for NexView POC
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@nexview.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 14:12:00 -0400
Subject: Next steps: integration & prompt safety for NexView POC

Hi Daniel / Elena —

Thanks again for the call today. Quick recap and proposed owners so we have a single source of truth for the POC plan. High level we heard:

- Goal: Validate real-time summarization + ranked insights for clinical notes (latency target < 200ms for tail, throughput ~120 req/s during peak).
- Primary constraints: PHI-handling requirements, VPC-only traffic, and a desire to run embeddings & reranker as part of the pipeline.

Proposed technical checklist and owners (please confirm):

1) SDK & auth setup (owner: Jordan)
   - Setup service account with scoped API keys and VPC egress rules. Evaluate short-lived tokens vs. static key + KMS rotation.
2) Routing & model variant policy (owner: Rishi)
   - Route high-priority traffic to a low-latency variant; failover to cost-optimized model on saturation.
3) Embeddings + reranker (owner: NexView + Redwood eng)
   - Store embeddings in your existing vector DB (we’ll provide embedding profiles).
4) Structured output / tool-calling (owner: Ava)
   - Define tool schema for lookup calls (patient lookup, medication mapping). We recommend explicit schema + strict type checking to reduce hallucination.
5) Prompt governance & safety (owner: Marissa + NexView Sec)
   - Versioned system prompts, injection mitigations (context window filters, whitelist/blacklist of tools), and a red-team plan.

Attachments: I’ve attached a one-page SOW and a first-pass architecture surface for discussion. Also included a small set of starter prompt templates (prompt-templates-v0.2.md).

Next steps / proposed timeline:
- Jordan: send SDK onboarding steps and credential template by EOD Thu (20 Aug).
- Rishi: share model routing policy examples and perf targets by Fri (21 Aug).
- NexView: provide sample anonymized notes + expected QPS by Mon (24 Aug).
- Marissa & Ava: organize a red-team kickoff for prompt injection tests (week of 8/31).

If anything above is off, flag it here. Otherwise I’ll update the HubSpot record and drive folder to reflect these owners.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: nexview-integration-sow-v1.pdf (PDF, 1 page); arch-surface-plan.drawio.png (PNG); prompt-templates-v0.2.md (MD)

From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@nexview.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 16:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Next steps: integration & prompt safety for NexView POC

Thanks Ava — this is excellent and aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- For PHI, our security team requires token rotation and HSM-backed keys for any persistent secrets. Short-lived tokens with a refresh flow are preferred.
- Vector DB: we plan to use our existing FAISS cluster behind the VPN. Can Redwood’s embeddings profile be normalized to match our F32 layout (we currently store 1536-d floats)?
- Tooling: we like the explicit schema approach. For the POC we only need patient lookup and a simple ICD mapping tool.

We’ll upload a small anonymized dataset by Monday (24 Aug). I attached a quick doc that lists the PHI handling checklist from our security review.

Two quick asks:
1) Can Jordan provide the SDK quickstart that shows service-account creation and least-privilege roles?
2) Can Rishi share any example routing JSON for low-latency vs. cost-fallback so our infra team can pre-approve network policies?

Appreciate it —
Daniel Park
Head of Data Platform, NexView Analytics
Daniel.Park@nexview.ai | +1 (646) 212-3300

Attachment: nexview-security-checklist.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 Aug 2026 11:28:00 -0400
Subject: SDK onboarding & credential patterns

Hi Daniel —

I put together a short SDK quickstart and credential template and dropped it into the shared Drive (link below). Highlights:

- We support two onboarding flows: service account (recommended for VPC/private deployments) and short-lived OAuth tokens (recommended for hosted, low-friction demos).
- For your PHI requirement I recommend service account + KMS-wrapped API key with a 24-hour rotation window. We can integrate with your HSM for signing if needed.
- Example Terraform snippets included for binding least-privilege roles and creating VPC egress rules.

Quickstart link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2bNexViewPOC (README: SDK-quickstart.md)

I’ll also attach a JSON example of a routing policy; Rishi — tagging you for the model variant perf numbers.

—Jordan
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Jordan.Blake@redwood.com

Attachment: SDK-quickstart.md (MD); credential-template.json (JSON)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Aug 2026 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: Re: SDK onboarding & credential patterns / routing examples

Folks —

Posting an example routing policy here (abbreviated) and the perf budget we used in other clinical POCs. Full JSON is in the Drive.

- Low-latency route: model_variant: redwood-llama-13b-rt
  - Target p99: 180ms (tokenization included)
  - Batching: continuous batch window 5ms, max batch 16
- Cost-fallback route: model_variant: redwood-13b-opt
  - Switch when queue length > 60 or median latency > 250ms

Embedding profile: we export 1536-d float32 vectors by default. We can add a normalization flag to match your storage layout.

Prompt safety notes (quick):
- System prompts: store in versioned namespace (prompt:v0.1, prompt:v0.2). Each change must reference a changelog entry and approval from security.
- Injection mitigations: context sanitizers (strip user-injected tool calls), tool whitelist enforcement in the runtime, and per-request policy tags to disable unknown tools.
- Red-team plan: two-week focused attack window post-POC where we will run adversarial prompts, then produce a remediation backlog.

Next asks:
- Daniel / Elena: confirm vector format (1536 float32 ok?) and provide sample throughput profile.
- Marissa: I’ll sync with you on the prompt versioning workflow — do you prefer Git-based prompts or a UI in Console?

Thanks,
Rishi
Staff Engineer, Inference Runtime, Redwood Inference

title: Next steps: integration & prompt safety for NexView POC
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@nexview.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 14:12:00 -0400
Subject: Next steps: integration & prompt safety for NexView POC

Hi Daniel / Elena —

Thanks again for the call today. Quick recap and proposed owners so we have a single source of truth for the POC plan. High level we heard:

- Goal: Validate real-time summarization + ranked insights for clinical notes (latency target < 200ms for tail, throughput ~120 req/s during peak).
- Primary constraints: PHI-handling requirements, VPC-only traffic, and a desire to run embeddings & reranker as part of the pipeline.

Proposed technical checklist and owners (please confirm):

1) SDK & auth setup (owner: Jordan)
   - Setup service account with scoped API keys and VPC egress rules. Evaluate short-lived tokens vs. static key + KMS rotation.
2) Routing & model variant policy (owner: Rishi)
   - Route high-priority traffic to a low-latency variant; failover to cost-optimized model on saturation.
3) Embeddings + reranker (owner: NexView + Redwood eng)
   - Store embeddings in your existing vector DB (we’ll provide embedding profiles).
4) Structured output / tool-calling (owner: Ava)
   - Define tool schema for lookup calls (patient lookup, medication mapping). We recommend explicit schema + strict type checking to reduce hallucination.
5) Prompt governance & safety (owner: Marissa + NexView Sec)
   - Versioned system prompts, injection mitigations (context window filters, whitelist/blacklist of tools), and a red-team plan.

Attachments: I’ve attached a one-page SOW and a first-pass architecture surface for discussion. Also included a small set of starter prompt templates (prompt-templates-v0.2.md).

Next steps / proposed timeline:
- Jordan: send SDK onboarding steps and credential template by EOD Thu (20 Aug).
- Rishi: share model routing policy examples and perf targets by Fri (21 Aug).
- NexView: provide sample anonymized notes + expected QPS by Mon (24 Aug).
- Marissa & Ava: organize a red-team kickoff for prompt injection tests (week of 8/31).

If anything above is off, flag it here. Otherwise I’ll update the HubSpot record and drive folder to reflect these owners.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: nexview-integration-sow-v1.pdf (PDF, 1 page); arch-surface-plan.drawio.png (PNG); prompt-templates-v0.2.md (MD)

From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@nexview.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 16:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Next steps: integration & prompt safety for NexView POC

Thanks Ava — this is excellent and aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- For PHI, our security team requires token rotation and HSM-backed keys for any persistent secrets. Short-lived tokens with a refresh flow are preferred.
- Vector DB: we plan to use our existing FAISS cluster behind the VPN. Can Redwood’s embeddings profile be normalized to match our F32 layout (we currently store 1536-d floats)?
- Tooling: we like the explicit schema approach. For the POC we only need patient lookup and a simple ICD mapping tool.

We’ll upload a small anonymized dataset by Monday (24 Aug). I attached a quick doc that lists the PHI handling checklist from our security review.

Two quick asks:
1) Can Jordan provide the SDK quickstart that shows service-account creation and least-privilege roles?
2) Can Rishi share any example routing JSON for low-latency vs. cost-fallback so our infra team can pre-approve network policies?

Appreciate it —
Daniel Park
Head of Data Platform, NexView Analytics
Daniel.Park@nexview.ai | +1 (646) 212-3300

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>

Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 Aug 2026 11:28:00 -0400
Subject: SDK onboarding & credential patterns

Hi Daniel —

I put together a short SDK quickstart and credential template and dropped it into the shared Drive (link below). Highlights:

- We support two onboarding flows: service account (recommended for VPC/private deployments) and short-lived OAuth tokens (recommended for hosted, low-friction demos).
- For your PHI requirement I recommend service account + KMS-wrapped API key with a 24-hour rotation window. We can integrate with your HSM for signing if needed.
- Example Terraform snippets included for binding least-privilege roles and creating VPC egress rules.

Quickstart link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2bNexViewPOC (README: SDK-quickstart.md)

I’ll also attach a JSON example of a routing policy; Rishi — tagging you for the model variant perf numbers.

—Jordan
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Jordan.Blake@redwood.com

Attachment: SDK-quickstart.md (MD); credential-template.json (JSON)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Daniel Park <daniel.park@nexview.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Aug 2026 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: Re: SDK onboarding & credential patterns / routing examples

Folks —

Posting an example routing policy here (abbreviated) and the perf budget we used in other clinical POCs. Full JSON is in the Drive.

- Low-latency route: model_variant: redwood-llama-13b-rt
  - Target p99: 180ms (tokenization included)
  - Batching: continuous batch window 5ms, max batch 16
- Cost-fallback route: model_variant: redwood-13b-opt
  - Switch when queue length > 60 or median latency > 250ms

Embedding profile: we export 1536-d float32 vectors by default. We can add a normalization flag to match your storage layout.

Prompt safety notes (quick):
- System prompts: store in versioned namespace (prompt:v0.1, prompt:v0.2). Each change must reference a changelog entry and approval from security.
- Injection mitigations: context sanitizers (strip user-injected tool calls), tool whitelist enforcement in the runtime, and per-request policy tags to disable unknown tools.
- Red-team plan: two-week focused attack window post-POC where we will run adversarial prompts, then produce a remediation backlog.

Next asks:
- Daniel / Elena: confirm vector format (1536 float32 ok?) and provide sample throughput profile.
- Marissa: I’ll sync with you on the prompt versioning workflow — do you prefer Git-based prompts or a UI in Console?

Thanks,
Rishi
Staff Engineer, Inference Runtime, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Kite-XS variant: integration gap review & temporary version lock request
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-12T09:14:00Z
Subject: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Team —

We just got the vendor compatibility report from Nimbus for the Kite-XS 4B family (see attached). High level: the model appears to run fine in our runtime, but there are two areas where the variant diverges from our standard tokenizer and KV layout which could cause downstream hallucination/regression for a subset of prompt shapes.

Quick summary: 
- Tokenization differences: subtle BOS/EOS treatment on edge-case punctuation sequences.
- KV cache ordering: variant uses packed KV dims which changes memory layout assumptions in a few lower-level kernels.

Ask: before we let this variant into the public catalog, I want to request a temporary version lock (pin) for Kite-XS on the current vetted variant (v2.3.1) while Eng runs a short integration sweep and we stage a canary. Proposed window: 2026-08-20 -> 2026-09-15.

Please confirm feasibility and list the minimal telemetry/QA checks you need from Product to unblock. I attached the compat report and a draft canary plan.

Thanks —
Ava

Ava Chen
Product Manager, Model Infra
Redwood Inference
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-13T11:04:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Ava —

Thanks for the write-up and the report. Read through the PDF and ran a short local pass on our staging infra. Concise notes:

- Tokenizer: we can normalize the BOS/EOS behavior on load with a tiny compatibility shim. Risk: ~0.2% tokenization divergence on long-chain punctuation tests.
- KV layout: this is the bigger one. Our current kernels expect a particular stride. We can add a fast-path that supports packed dims but it will need a micro-bench and a kernel bench job (2-3 dev days + a GPU bench slot).

Workaround proposal to enable the pin window quickly:
1) Temporary pin to v2.3.1 at routing layer so production requests use the vetted variant.
2) Deploy a staging canary (small % traffic) to the Kite-XS build behind a feature flag. Canary should collect: tokenization diffs, perplexity delta on our smoke evals, and tail-latency per-token.
3) Devs will deliver a kernel shim PR and a perf bench by 2026-08-26.

If Product signs off on the telemetry (we need the canonical prompt set + a short list of golden prompts from Nimbus), I can schedule the bench. Otherwise we risk re-introducing behavior changes into user-facing workloads.

I'll attach a short checklist in a follow-up.

— Dev

Dev Patel
Senior Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-15T15:29:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Folks —

Agree with Dev's assessment. Operational ask from Reliability: if we do a temporary lock, we need a rollback runbook + a capacity plan for the pinned variant in the event Nimbus's build has different perf characteristics.

Canary suggestions:
- 0.5% production traffic for 48 hours, check: tokenization delta, 99th percentile latency, error rate (non-200).
- If any metric crosses the threshold (tokenization delta > 1.0% on canonical set OR tail latency +25%), auto-fallback to v2.3.1.

I created a short canary template and added it as kite-xs-canary-plan.md (attached). If legal/compliance needs to review retention for telemetry, loop them in.

Laura

Laura Bennett
Principal SRE
Redwood Inference
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Sarah Mitchell <sarah@nimbus.ai>
Date: 2026-08-18T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Quick product decision: we are comfortable with the proposed temporary routing lock to v2.3.1 for the public catalog while engineering runs the integration work, provided Nimbus supplies a short golden prompt set (10-15 prompts) and agrees to a 30-day post-entry telemetry review.

Sarah — can you confirm you can provide the golden set and a compact eval script by EOD 2026-08-20?

Action items I want tracked:
- Nimbus: provide golden prompts + eval script (Sarah)
- Eng (Dev): kernel shim PR + perf bench by 2026-08-26
- SRE (Laura): canary deployment + auto-fallback policy implemented before 2026-08-20
- Product (Ava): sign-off on telemetry thresholds and canary window (you own)

I will mark this as a provisional exception in the intake tracker pending the above.

— Lauren

Lauren Bishop
Director, Model Product
Redwood Inference
From: Sarah Mitchell <sarah@nimbus.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Paul Torres <paul@cloudops.com>
Date: 2026-08-20T16:42:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Thanks all — Nimbus will provide the 12 golden prompts and a lightweight eval harness by COB today (uploaded to the doc link Lauren referenced). We agree to the 30-day telemetry review and will stay on standby for any debugging.

Per your note on pinning, we accept the temporary routing lock to v2.3.1 for the public catalog through 2026-09-15. If your bench results indicate the shim is sufficient we can schedule a variant refresh; otherwise we'll coordinate a smaller patch release.

Attachments: kite-xs-compat-report.pdf (from Nimbus), kite-xs-canary-plan.md (SRE template)

Best,
Sarah Mitchell
Head of Partnerships
NimbusAI

Date: 2026-08-12T09:14:00Z
Subject: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Team —

We just got the vendor compatibility report from Nimbus for the Kite-XS 4B family (see attached). High level: the model appears to run fine in our runtime, but there are two areas where the variant diverges from our standard tokenizer and KV layout which could cause downstream hallucination/regression for a subset of prompt shapes.

Quick summary: 
- Tokenization differences: subtle BOS/EOS treatment on edge-case punctuation sequences.
- KV cache ordering: variant uses packed KV dims which changes memory layout assumptions in a few lower-level kernels.

Ask: before we let this variant into the public catalog, I want to request a temporary version lock (pin) for Kite-XS on the current vetted variant (v2.3.1) while Eng runs a short integration sweep and we stage a canary. Proposed window: 2026-08-20 -> 2026-09-15.

Please confirm feasibility and list the minimal telemetry/QA checks you need from Product to unblock. I attached the compat report and a draft canary plan.

Thanks —
Ava

Ava Chen
Product Manager, Model Infra
Redwood Inference


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-13T11:04:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Ava —

Thanks for the write-up and the report. Read through the PDF and ran a short local pass on our staging infra. Concise notes:

- Tokenizer: we can normalize the BOS/EOS behavior on load with a tiny compatibility shim. Risk: ~0.2% tokenization divergence on long-chain punctuation tests.
- KV layout: this is the bigger one. Our current kernels expect a particular stride. We can add a fast-path that supports packed dims but it will need a micro-bench and a kernel bench job (2-3 dev days + a GPU bench slot).

Workaround proposal to enable the pin window quickly:
1) Temporary pin to v2.3.1 at routing layer so production requests use the vetted variant.
2) Deploy a staging canary (small % traffic) to the Kite-XS build behind a feature flag. Canary should collect: tokenization diffs, perplexity delta on our smoke evals, and tail-latency per-token.
3) Devs will deliver a kernel shim PR and a perf bench by 2026-08-26.

If Product signs off on the telemetry (we need the canonical prompt set + a short list of golden prompts from Nimbus), I can schedule the bench. Otherwise we risk re-introducing behavior changes into user-facing workloads.

I'll attach a short checklist in a follow-up.

— Dev

Dev Patel
Senior Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-15T15:29:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Folks —

Agree with Dev's assessment. Operational ask from Reliability: if we do a temporary lock, we need a rollback runbook + a capacity plan for the pinned variant in the event Nimbus's build has different perf characteristics.

Canary suggestions:
- 0.5% production traffic for 48 hours, check: tokenization delta, 99th percentile latency, error rate (non-200).
- If any metric crosses the threshold (tokenization delta > 1.0% on canonical set OR tail latency +25%), auto-fallback to v2.3.1.

I created a short canary template and added it as kite-xs-canary-plan.md (attached). If legal/compliance needs to review retention for telemetry, loop them in.

Laura

Laura Bennett
Principal SRE
Redwood Inference


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Sarah Mitchell <sarah@nimbus.ai>
Date: 2026-08-18T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Quick product decision: we are comfortable with the proposed temporary routing lock to v2.3.1 for the public catalog while engineering runs the integration work, provided Nimbus supplies a short golden prompt set (10-15 prompts) and agrees to a 30-day post-entry telemetry review.

Sarah — can you confirm you can provide the golden set and a compact eval script by EOD 2026-08-20?

Action items I want tracked:
- Nimbus: provide golden prompts + eval script (Sarah)
- Eng (Dev): kernel shim PR + perf bench by 2026-08-26
- SRE (Laura): canary deployment + auto-fallback policy implemented before 2026-08-20
- Product (Ava): sign-off on telemetry thresholds and canary window (you own)

I will mark this as a provisional exception in the intake tracker pending the above.

— Lauren

Lauren Bishop
Director, Model Product
Redwood Inference


From: Sarah Mitchell <sarah@nimbus.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Paul Torres <paul@cloudops.com>
Date: 2026-08-20T16:42:00Z
Subject: Re: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Thanks all — Nimbus will provide the 12 golden prompts and a lightweight eval harness by COB today (uploaded to the doc link Lauren referenced). We agree to the 30-day telemetry review and will stay on standby for any debugging.

Per your note on pinning, we accept the temporary routing lock to v2.3.1 for the public catalog through 2026-09-15. If your bench results indicate the shim is sufficient we can schedule a variant refresh; otherwise we'll coordinate a smaller patch release.

Attachments: kite-xs-compat-report.pdf (from Nimbus), kite-xs-canary-plan.md (SRE template)

Best,
Sarah Mitchell
Head of Partnerships
NimbusAI
