source.subject: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com>, Priya Desai <priya@opsly.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)

Hi Tom / Priya — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap + next steps so we have a common plan.

Call highlights:
- Opsly latency expectations: p95 ~80ms for inference on typical 512-token merchant requests; p99 target currently not required but helpful to track.
- Burst profile: baseline steady-state ~120 QPS (peak hourly 200 QPS), but you saw short spikes up to ~600 QPS for ~20–45s during promotions.
- Security: standard SOC2 controls are required; VPC/private endpoints are a later ask (we discussed Dedicated + Private as options).
- Time-to-value: you want to get an integration live for the holiday pilot in 6 weeks if possible.

Preliminary guidance (what we discussed):
- Hosted API is the fastest path to start (low setup). It's a reasonable fit if your steady-state + short bursts can be handled by autoscaled hosted capacity and you accept a fallback policy for sustained multi-minute spikes. Hosted is great when burst durations are short (<1 minute) and overall p95 target is in the 50–150ms range.
- Dedicated capacity reduces unit cost for high sustained QPS and gives predictable tail performance for strict latency SLOs. It's the recommended path if you see repeated >2 minute spikes at 400–600 QPS or need guaranteed isolation (or want to combine with a Private deployment later).

Concrete next steps I propose: 
1) Opsly: share a 10–30 minute representative traffic trace (sample attached: pilot-qps-sample.csv) — include timestamps and QPS by second plus example request sizes.
2) Redwood: run a short bench against a Redwood-verified model variant (we'll run hosted and a dedicated-style simulation) and return a p50/p95/p99 latency table + throughput curve.
3) Redwood: provide quick cost sketch for a 2-week dedicated pilot (Ben will own) and a hosted fallback pattern to keep the integration live during ramp.
4) Opsly & Redwood: agree on a minimal pilot SLA and a go/no-go checkpoint after the bench.

I've attached a short SLO proposal (slo-proposal.pdf) that maps your p95 target to simple routing rules we can use during the pilot. If you can send the trace by EOD Thursday PST we'll aim to return bench numbers within 48 hours.

Thanks — I’ll coordinate infra for the bench once we have the trace.

—
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
Redwood Inference
Attachments: pilot-qps-sample.csv (CSV), slo-proposal.pdf (PDF)

From: Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Desai <priya@opsly.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 15:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)

Thanks Ava — this is super helpful. I'm attaching a 12-minute trace (high-traffic window from last promo) that includes per-second QPS and payload sizes.

Two quick clarifications: 
- The 600 QPS spikes are usually ~30–40s, but we saw one ~90s spike last quarter.
- Security: our security team will push for a VPC/private option for production, but for the pilot they are willing to accept hosted endpoints with additional audit logging.

Can you also ballpark the difference in monthly cost between a hosted ramp and a small dedicated pool for the pilot so we can flag this to finance? No need for a detailed quote — a rough delta is fine.

Tom
CTO, Opsly
Attachments: opsly-20260612-trace.json (JSON)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)

On Tue, Aug 18, 2026 at 3:47 PM Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ava — this is super helpful. I'm attaching a 12-minute trace (high-traffic window from last promo) that includes per-second QPS and payload sizes.
> 
> Two quick clarifications: 
> - The 600 QPS spikes are usually ~30–40s, but we saw one ~90s spike last quarter.
> - Security: our security team will push for a VPC/private option for production, but for the pilot they are willing to accept hosted endpoints with additional audit logging.

Thanks Tom — that spike pattern is exactly the kind of signal we need.

Ben — can you take point on a quick sketch: 1) estimated monthly delta (hosted vs small dedicated pool for a 2-week pilot), and 2) a short note on whether the 90s spike changes the recommendation (i.e., if a single 90s event is a hard constraint to require Dedicated)?

Neha — I'll loop you on SLO mapping and which telemetry points we should include in the pilot agreement.

I'll schedule a short checkpoint call next week to review bench numbers.

—
Ava

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 17:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)

Thanks for the context — I've attached a short cost sketch (dedicated-cost-estimate.xlsx) with two scenarios:
- Scenario A (hosted-first): use Hosted API with autoscaling + burst smoothing; expected pilot cost ~ $3.2k for the 2-week window (higher per-token unit).
- Scenario B (dedicated pilot): reserve a small gpool for the pilot with burst headroom; expected pilot cost ~ $7.8k for the 2-week window (lower unit cost, predictable latency).

Observation on the 90s spike: a single 90s event won't automatically force Dedicated, but if you expect repeats (>=3 events/day) or need hard p99 guarantees during promos then Dedicated is the safer option. For the pilot we often recommend starting on Hosted with strict routing (rate limits + a transparent fallback to a slightly lower-capacity model) so you can get live traffic and validate behavior; if bench numbers or security gating require isolation we can flip to a Dedicated pilot quickly.

Next steps from my side: I will confirm the dedicated pilot pricing and include a short appendix that outlines how we handle rapid spikes (auto-burst policies, fallback model variants).

—
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0189
Attachments: dedicated-cost-estimate.xlsx (XLSX)

Two quick clarifications:

- Security: our security team will push for a VPC/private option for production, but for the pilot they are willing to accept hosted endpoints with additional audit logging.

Can you also ballpark the difference in monthly cost between a hosted ramp and a small dedicated pool for the pilot so we can flag this to finance? No need for a detailed quote — a rough delta is fine.

Tom
CTO, Opsly
Attachments: opsly-20260612-trace.json (JSON)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)

On Tue, Aug 18, 2026 at 3:47 PM Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ava — this is super helpful. I'm attaching a 12-minute trace (high-traffic window from last promo) that includes per-second QPS and payload sizes.
> 
> Two quick clarifications: 
> - The 600 QPS spikes are usually ~30–40s, but we saw one ~90s spike last quarter.
> - Security: our security team will push for a VPC/private option for production, but for the pilot they are willing to accept hosted endpoints with additional audit logging.

Thanks Tom — that spike pattern is exactly the kind of signal we need.

Ben — can you take point on a quick sketch: 1) estimated monthly delta (hosted vs small dedicated pool for a 2-week pilot), and 2) a short note on whether the 90s spike changes the recommendation (i.e., if a single 90s event is a hard constraint to require Dedicated)?

Neha — I'll loop you on SLO mapping and which telemetry points we should include in the pilot agreement.

I'll schedule a short checkpoint call next week to review bench numbers.

—
Ava

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Tom Rivera <tom@opsly.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 Aug 2026 17:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted vs Dedicated — latency & burst readiness (Opsly)

Thanks for the context — I've attached a short cost sketch (dedicated-cost-estimate.xlsx) with two scenarios:
- Scenario A (hosted-first): use Hosted API with autoscaling + burst smoothing; expected pilot cost ~ $3.2k for the 2-week window (higher per-token unit).
- Scenario B (dedicated pilot): reserve a small gpool for the pilot with burst headroom; expected pilot cost ~ $7.8k for the 2-week window (lower unit cost, predictable latency).

Observation on the 90s spike: a single 90s event won't automatically force Dedicated, but if you expect repeats (>=3 events/day) or need hard p99 guarantees during promos then Dedicated is the safer option. For the pilot we often recommend starting on Hosted with strict routing (rate limits + a transparent fallback to a slightly lower-capacity model) so you can get live traffic and validate behavior; if bench numbers or security gating require isolation we can flip to a Dedicated pilot quickly.

Next steps from my side: I will confirm the dedicated pilot pricing and include a short appendix that outlines how we handle rapid spikes (auto-burst policies, fallback model variants).

—
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0189
Attachments: dedicated-cost-estimate.xlsx (XLSX)

source.subject: Action required: complete supplier activation for Lumen Fabrications
messages:
From: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 Aug 2026 09:37:00 -0700
Subject: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Hi Ava,

We tried to complete the supplier activation link you sent this morning but the button on the activation page doesn't appear on our mobile webview (Android Chrome). Tapping the blank area does nothing. I attached a screenshot.

We can complete from desktop for now, but our AP team mostly works from tablets while on site and this will block our PO acknowledgements later this week. Can you resend a link or suggest a workaround?

Thanks,
Thomas Rey
Head of AP, Lumen Fabrications
onboarding@lumenfab.com
Attachment: screenshot-cta-hidden.png (image/png, 248 KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 Aug 2026 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Hi Thomas — thanks for the heads up and the screenshot.

Short answer: we saw a small subset of activation links render an empty CTA on certain Android WebViews when the deep-link parameter string exceeded ~1200 characters. That happens when a long return_url + campaign tracking + marketplace params are included. We pushed a mitigation last week but it looks like your link still had a long param set.

Workarounds:
- Open the link in a desktop browser (what you noted)
- Long-term: I'll reissue a trimmed activation link (no campaign params) and paste the direct deep-link token in the email body so mobile clients don't drop the button.

I'll re-send now and loop in Ben for an ops follow-up to ensure our link generator strips non-essential tracking for supplier activation flows.

Ava Chen
Enterprise Onboarding (Redwood Inference)
--
On Sat, Aug 15, 2026 at 09:37 AM Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com> wrote:
> Hi Ava,
> 
> We tried to complete the supplier activation link you sent this morning but the button on the activation page doesn't appear on our mobile webview (Android Chrome).
> 
> Thanks,
> Thomas
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
Date: Sun, 16 Aug 2026 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Hi team,

I dug into the generator and confirmed the issue: the link builder appended both hb_campaign_id and ga_client_id for marketing attribution. On small-screen WebViews the layout engine treated the long querystring as part of the inline button rendering, causing the CTA to flow off-canvas.

Planned fixes:
1) Short-term patch to strip non-required query params for activation emails (deploy tomorrow morning).
2) Telemetry: add a hit to capture activation page client type and render errors so we can proactively detect regressions.

Ava — can you resend the trimmed activation link to Thomas now? I'll open a ticket (SUP-8123) to track the patch and include a regression test against Android embedded WebView flows.

Ben
Customer Ops
Attachment: onboarding-instructions.pdf (application/pdf, 112 KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 Aug 2026 11:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Thomas — new trimmed activation link below. This one removes tracking parameters and should render the CTA correctly on mobile webviews. Please tap the link from your mobile device and confirm success. If it still fails, reply and we'll set up a quick screen-share.

Activation link: https://redwood.app/activate?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9--shorttoken123

Notes:
- Attachment: onboarding-instructions.pdf explains the AP/PO flow and how to add our billing email to your vendor master so POs route correctly.
- We've filed SUP-8123 to make the fix permanent. Priya, can you add the Android WebView regression to the automation backlog?

Thanks — let me know when you're activated and I'll confirm PO visibility from our side.

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Onboarding (Redwood Inference)
Phone: +1 415-555-0192
--
On Sun, Aug 16, 2026 at 08:21 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi team,
> 
> I dug into the generator and confirmed the issue...
> 
> Ben

source.subject: Vendor account establishment checklist — ClearWave Labs
messages:
From: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@clearwave.co>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Inquiry: vendor setup for ClearWave Labs

Hi Ava,

Hope you're well — I'm Sarah Liu, Accounts Receivable at ClearWave Labs. We're finalizing a purchase with your procurement team and need to get set up in Redwood's vendor system so we can receive POs and submit invoices. Can you confirm the onboarding steps and what documentation you need from us?

Quick context: we'll be providing hardware probes and a small recurring SaaS license. Our expected first invoice will be for an initial shipment in September.

If possible, send the vendor intake packet and any preferred invoice template so we can return a completed set.

Thanks,
Sarah Liu
Accounts Receivable | ClearWave Labs
accounts@clearwave.co
+1 415-555-0198

(Attachment: none in this message)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@clearwave.co>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Inquiry: vendor setup for ClearWave Labs

Hi Sarah — welcome, and thanks for the heads up. Glad to get you onboarded. I've attached our vendor onboarding packet to this thread and linked the intake form below. To get ClearWave added to the vendor master we typically need the following items:

- Legal company name (exactly as registered)
- Tax identifier (EIN for US entities / VAT or local tax id for others) + completed W-9 or W-8BEN as applicable
- Remit & legal address (street, city, state/province, country, postal code)
- Bank details or completed ACH authorization (DocuSign link below) for electronic payments; paper checks are supported but increase processing time
- Primary invoice contact (email + phone) and PO routing contact (who should receive POs and acknowledgements)
- Preferred invoice frequency & sample invoice file (PDF) so AP can validate line items and tax formatting
- DUNS number or company registration number (if you have it) — optional but helpful for larger accounts

Estimated timeline: once we have completed docs, AP typically publishes the vendor record within 3-7 business days. If you need an SLA for expedited onboarding, let me know and we can request priority processing.

Action items for ClearWave: complete the Vendor Intake Form (linked) and attach W-9 + bank form.

Links & attachments:
- Attachment: redwood-vendor-onboarding-packet.pdf (required fields + examples)
- Vendor Intake Form (fillable): https://drive.google.com/open?id=1Xy-Redwood-VendorIntake
- ACH authorization (DocuSign): https://www.docusign.net/envelope/ACH_envelope_6789

Cc'ing Karthik from AP to flag any PO routing or matching rules that may apply. Karthik — please see the high-level requirements above and add anything specific we should request from ClearWave.

Best,
Ava Chen
Procurement Lead | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0112

(Attachment: redwood-vendor-onboarding-packet.pdf)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@clearwave.co>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Aug 2026 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Inquiry: vendor setup for ClearWave Labs

Hi Sarah — Karthik on the AP side. Quick follow-ups to ensure we map your account correctly: 

- Please confirm legal tax classification (Corporation / LLC / Sole Prop / Non-Profit) and whether any US backup withholding status applies.
- We require a completed W-9 (US vendors) or W-8BEN (non-US) with a printed legal name matching your remittance record.
- For PO-to-invoice matching: do you want 2-way match (PO + invoice) or 3-way (PO + invoice + goods receipt)? For hardware shipments, many vendors choose 3-way but we can accept 2-way for SaaS line items.
- Provide the person who will receive POs (name + email). If different from invoices, include both contacts.

I've attached our PO routing template to show the fields we'll populate in the master record. Once I receive your W-9 and sample invoice we can validate tax formatting and turnaround for payment terms (net 30 by default).

Thanks — Karthik
Karthik Iyer
AP Manager | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

(Attachment: AP-PO-routing-template.xlsx)

From: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@clearwave.co>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 15 Aug 2026 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Inquiry: vendor setup for ClearWave Labs

Hi Ava / Karthik — thanks for the clear checklist. Attached are the documents you requested:

- ClearWave_W9.pdf (legal name: ClearWave Labs Inc.; EIN: 45-9876543)
- ClearWave_bank_form.pdf (ACH details included; account & routing provided)
- sample_invoice_080426.pdf (sample invoice layout we will send)
- DocuSign: ACH_DocuSign_envelope_6789.pdf (signed envelope confirmation)

Remit address:
ClearWave Labs Inc.
1201 Harbor Way, Suite 400
Oakland, CA 94607
USA

Invoice contact: accounts@clearwave.co (Sarah Liu)
PO routing contact: Tom Marsden <tom.marsden@clearwave.co> — Tom will confirm shipment acknowledgements.

Tax classification: C-Corporation (US) — no backup withholding.

PO matching preference: 3-way match for hardware shipments; 2-way is fine for recurring SaaS invoices.

Can you confirm when Redwood will issue the first PO for the initial hardware order? Our team is targeting shipment in early September and would like to align logistics.

Thanks again — attaching files now.

Best,
Sarah Liu
Accounts Receivable | ClearWave Labs
accounts@clearwave.co

(Attachments: ClearWave_W9.pdf, ClearWave_bank_form.pdf, sample_invoice_080426.pdf, ACH_DocuSign_envelope_6789.pdf)

Can you confirm when Redwood will issue the first PO for the initial hardware order? Our team is targeting shipment in early September and would like to align logistics.

Thanks again — attaching files now.

Best,
Sarah Liu
Accounts Receivable | ClearWave Labs
accounts@clearwave.co

(Attachments: ClearWave_W9.pdf, ClearWave_bank_form.pdf, sample_invoice_080426.pdf, ACH_DocuSign_envelope_6789.pdf)

Date: Thu, 13 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Inquiry: vendor setup for ClearWave Labs

Hi Ava,

Hope you're well — I'm Sarah Liu, Accounts Receivable at ClearWave Labs. We're finalizing a purchase with your procurement team and need to get set up in Redwood's vendor system so we can receive POs and submit invoices. Can you confirm the onboarding steps and what documentation you need from us?

Quick context: we'll be providing hardware probes and a small recurring SaaS license. Our expected first invoice will be for an initial shipment in September.

If possible, send the vendor intake packet and any preferred invoice template so we can return a completed set.

Thanks,
Sarah Liu
Accounts Receivable | ClearWave Labs
accounts@clearwave.co
+1 415-555-0198

(Attachment: none in this message)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@clearwave.co>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Inquiry: vendor setup for ClearWave Labs

Hi Sarah — welcome, and thanks for the heads up. Glad to get you onboarded. I've attached our vendor onboarding packet to this thread and linked the intake form below. To get ClearWave added to the vendor master we typically need the following items:

- Legal company name (exactly as registered)
- Tax identifier (EIN for US entities / VAT or local tax id for others) + completed W-9 or W-8BEN as applicable
- Remit & legal address (street, city, state/province, country, postal code)
- Bank details or completed ACH authorization (DocuSign link below) for electronic payments; paper checks are supported but increase processing time
- Primary invoice contact (email + phone) and PO routing contact (who should receive POs and acknowledgements)
- Preferred invoice frequency & sample invoice file (PDF) so AP can validate line items and tax formatting
- DUNS number or company registration number (if you have it) — optional but helpful for larger accounts

Estimated timeline: once we have completed docs, AP typically publishes the vendor record within 3-7 business days. If you need an SLA for expedited onboarding, let me know and we can request priority processing.

Action items for ClearWave: complete the Vendor Intake Form (linked) and attach W-9 + bank form.

Links & attachments:
- Attachment: redwood-vendor-onboarding-packet.pdf (required fields + examples)
- Vendor Intake Form (fillable): https://drive.google.com/open?id=1Xy-Redwood-VendorIntake
- ACH authorization (DocuSign): https://www.docusign.net/envelope/ACH_envelope_6789

Cc'ing Karthik from AP to flag any PO routing or matching rules that may apply. Karthik — please see the high-level requirements above and add anything specific we should request from ClearWave.

Best,
Ava Chen
Procurement Lead | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0112

(Attachment: redwood-vendor-onboarding-packet.pdf)

Hi Sarah — Karthik on the AP side. Quick follow-ups to ensure we map your account correctly: 

- Please confirm legal tax classification (Corporation / LLC / Sole Prop / Non-Profit) and whether any US backup withholding status applies.
- We require a completed W-9 (US vendors) or W-8BEN (non-US) with a printed legal name matching your remittance record.
- For PO-to-invoice matching: do you want 2-way match (PO + invoice) or 3-way (PO + invoice + goods receipt)? For hardware shipments, many vendors choose 3-way but we can accept 2-way for SaaS line items.
- Provide the person who will receive POs (name + email). If different from invoices, include both contacts.

I've attached our PO routing template to show the fields we'll populate in the master record. Once I receive your W-9 and sample invoice we can validate tax formatting and turnaround for payment terms (net 30 by default).

source.subject: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Neal <liam@launchlabs.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

Hi Liam — great to meet last week. Per our call, attaching the Founder Bundle term sheet and our current pricing sheet so your ops and finance folks can see the mechanics. Quick summary of what LaunchLabs asked for and what we're proposing: 

- Ask: split $50k in promotional credits across sandbox/onboarding (for mentor teams + demos) and production pilot usage; want ability to convert leftover credits to a Dedicated commitment later.
- Proposal (recommended): 60% sandbox/onboarding (to maximize demo velocity) + 40% pilot/production; credits usable against hosted API and convertible to a prorated Dedicated credit within 9 months; credits expire 12 months from activation.

Two commercial options we can offer: 
1) Standard allocation (no additional discount) — credits applied to usage until exhaustion.
2) Bundled onboarding (recommended for LaunchLabs): 50/50 split, + 10% applied discount on the first 3 months of paid usage after credits are exhausted, and a conversion credit to Dedicated if they convert within 9 months (conversion formula: remaining credit value * 0.85).

Operational asks for your team: can contracts@launchlabs.io accept the conversion language into the addendum (we can produce a short DocuSign-ready addendum), and do you have a preferred invoice path (po number or marketplace)?

I've attached a redlined term sheet (LaunchLabs_FounderBundle_v2.pdf) and our pricing worksheet. If you want, we can do a 20-min ops call with your finance on Thursday.

Next steps: confirm preferred split and whether conversion-to-Dedicated is acceptable; I’ll loop in legal for an addendum draft.

Thanks —
Ava
Ava Chen
Growth Sales
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: LaunchLabs_FounderBundle_v2.pdf (term sheet, 3 pages), redwood-pricing-sheet-2026.xlsx (pricing workbook)
From: Liam O'Neal <liam@launchlabs.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: contracts@launchlabs.io
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

Thanks, Ava — appreciate the clear options and the docs. A few quick clarifying Qs from our side before we pull finance/legal in: 

1) Private/on-prem: can any portion of the credits be applied to a Private deployment (we may need to run a small on-prem pilot for our regulated cohort)?
2) Expiry: you mentioned 12 months from activation — is there flexibility to extend to 18 months for strategic partners that have longer procurement cycles?
3) Invoice path: we prefer PO + standard net-30 invoice, but our marketplace procurement team (cc’d) also asked whether marketplace billing (AWS Marketplace) is possible for credits conversion.

If the conversion-to-Dedicated formula can be circulated as a short addendum, contracts can review quickly. Happy to take the 20-min ops call — Thursday at 10am ET works for us.

Liam
Liam O'Neal
Partnerships Lead, LaunchLabs
liam@launchlabs.io
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Aug 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

Ben / Marissa — forwarding LaunchLabs’ follow-up so we can lock commercial and ops. Key asks: private/on-prem applicability, expiry flexibility to 18 months, and invoice path PO vs marketplace. My recommendations below.

Recommendation (commercial + ops):
- Allocation: go with the Bundled onboarding offer but tweak to 55% sandbox / 45% pilot. Rationale: preserves demo velocity while giving LaunchLabs confidence they can run meaningful pilots.
- Private deployments: allow up to $10k of the credit pool to be earmarked for Private (on-prem/VPC) pilot usage. This must be explicitly tracked in billing and flagged on invoices.
- Expiry: extend to 15 months (compromise) for strategic partners; if they need 18 months, require an executive sponsor sign-off and log it on the deal.
- Conversion to Dedicated: accept the conversion clause as 85% of remaining credit value (our proposed formula). Add a clause that conversion must be invoked within 9 months of activation.
- Discount sweetener: keep the 10% applied discount for first 3 months post-credits.
- Invoice path: support PO + net-30 as default. Marketplace billing only if customer wants marketplace credits converted to Redwood invoicing (ops overhead and delays).

Ops asks: Marissa — can you confirm we can carve $10k of credits for Private pilots and flag those on the invoice? Ben — if you agree, I’ll instruct legal to draft a 1-page addendum and populate the DocuSign template (link below). Also propose we use the HubSpot deal DL-2026-043 for tracking.

If everyone agrees, I’ll send the addendum to contracts@launchlabs.io and schedule the 20-min ops call for Thursday 10am ET.

DocuSign addendum draft (placeholder): https://na2.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=abc-123
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/DEAL-2026-043

Thanks —
Ava

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Liam O'Neal <liam@launchlabs.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: contracts@launchlabs.io
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Founder bundle credit allocation & ops path

(see above)

source.subject: PLG signup: single-use onboarding coupon request for Streamline Labs
messages:
From: Elise Grant <elise@streamlinelabs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 9 Aug 2026 16:20:00 -0700
Subject: Quick favor — one-time onboarding credit for Streamline evaluation

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the product walkthrough earlier. We have a technical spike this week and I want to make sure our engineers can finish an end-to-end evaluation rather than hitting credit limits mid-test.

Would Redwood be able to issue a one-time, single-use onboarding coupon for $1,000 of credits tied to our PLG signup? We only need it for our engineering org to run a 48–72 hour stress test and a longer prompt pass.

Account details: Streamline Labs (hubspot deal STR-834) — I attached a short org summary.

We are trying to move quickly; if there are approvals needed please let me know who to loop in. Ideally we can have the coupon by Tuesday morning (PST).

Appreciate it —
Elise
Elise Grant
Head of Platform Engineering
Streamline Labs
elise@streamlinelabs.com

Attachment: Streamline_account_summary.png (image, 120KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elise Grant <elise@streamlinelabs.com>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Quick favor — one-time onboarding credit for Streamline evaluation

Thanks for the demo feedback, Elise — I can handle the ops side but need internal approval to issue a manual coupon outside of the self-serve thresholds.

Dev / Marissa — Streamline Labs (STR-834) is a PLG lead with a strong engineering motion. They asked for a single-use $1,000 onboarding coupon to complete a short technical spike. They are not eligible for the automated onboarding credit because their signup is via SSO and exceeded the auto-policy by org size.

Why I recommend approval:
- High chance to convert: Eng team committed to PoC with product team (signals in HubSpot).
- Short timeline: they need credits before Tuesday.
- Low risk: single-use, restricted to their account id, 30-day expiry.

Can we approve a one-off coupon with these constraints: $1,000 USD, single-use, expires in 30 days, limit to Streamline account ID STR-834? If approved, I will create the coupon in Promo tool, log it in finance (cc Marissa), and share code with Elise.

Attachments: Streamline_account_summary.png (from Elise)

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Elise Grant <elise@streamlinelabs.com>
Date: Sun, 9 Aug 2026 16:20:00 -0700
Subject: Quick favor — one-time onboarding credit for Streamline evaluation

[original message above]
From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 10:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Quick favor — one-time onboarding credit for Streamline evaluation

Ava — sounds reasonable to me for a one-time exception. Approve the $1,000 single-use coupon with these caveats:
- Expiry: 30 days from issuance
- Single-use and account-bound to STR-834
- Tag promo with reason = "PLG qualification — engineering spike"
- Finance (Marissa) to confirm reconciliation code after issuance

I'll authorize the exception. Please record the coupon under deal STR-834 in HubSpot and attach the issuance spreadsheet.

If anything looks off, escalate to me.

Thanks,
Dev Patel
Director, Sales Operations
dev_patel@redwood.ai
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Elise Grant <elise@streamlinelabs.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick favor — one-time onboarding credit for Streamline evaluation

Elise — approved. Thanks for the patience.

Coupon code: RWIN-STREAM-ONB-0831
Value: $1,000 USD
Type: single-use, account-bound to STR-834
Expiry: 2026-09-10 (30 days)
Terms: Redeemable only on your Streamline Labs account; not transferable.

I attached a short CSV export that I uploaded to our promo tracker for finance reconciliation (coupon_record_20260811.csv). Please redeem from your account and let me know once the spike is complete.

Best of luck with the tests — ping me if you need another short extension after the PoC review.

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai

Attachment: coupon_record_20260811.csv (text/csv)

source.subject: HelioHub — Executive intro + demo (VP/CIO attendance requested)
messages:
From: Elena Moss <elena.moss@heliohub.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 07 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick intro + request for VP/CIO at demo

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for the product overview last week. Our procurement and platform teams are aligned and we'd like to put together a 45-minute executive-level demo next week to show HelioHub's platform and where Redwood would slot into our stack. We generally invite our CTO (Raj Patel) and VP of Platform (Melissa Ng) — they prefer a short executive walkthrough + a 30-minute Q&A focused on reliability, data residency, and cost.

Would Redwood be able to have a senior sponsor (VP-level or above, ideally the person who owns exec briefing) attend? Our first-choice windows next week are:
 - Tue 2026-08-11 between 10:00–12:00 PDT
 - Wed 2026-08-12 14:00–16:00 PDT

I attached a short HelioHub overview and a draft agenda. If those slots don't work we can push to the week after.

Attaching: HelioHub_Overview_2026-08.pdf; also sending calendar stub.

Best,
Elena
Elena Moss
Head of Solutions
HelioHub
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Moss <elena.moss@heliohub.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 07 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick intro + request for VP/CIO at demo

Elena — thanks for the clear windows and the agenda. Looping in Irene (Director, Field Sponsorship) and Jordan (Solutions Engineer) so we can confirm the right mix for an executive walk-through.

Irene — short ask: HelioHub would like a VP-level sponsor for the demo. Can you cover one of the Tue/Wed windows below? If not, a VP proxy plus a brief pre-brief (15 min) so they can sign off is fine.

Jordan — please review the attached agenda and flag any technical pre-reqs (network/firewall notes, slide add-ons).

Proposed internal options:
 - Irene Choi (VP, Field) — Tue 2026-08-11 10:30–11:15 PDT (can move up to 11:45 if needed)
 - Alternate: Jordan + brief from Irene immediately before (15 min pre-brief at 10:00)

We can confirm once Irene says which she prefers.

Thanks — Ava
Ava Chen
Account Exec, Eastern Region
Redwood Inference
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Elena Moss <elena.moss@heliohub.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 09:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick intro + request for VP/CIO at demo

Thanks — quick technical notes before we lock the slot:

1) Network: if HelioHub intends to show a live integration with their staging cluster we need their IP ranges (or a short-lived SSH tunnel); otherwise screen-share is fine.
2) Data residency: happy to have a single-slide summary showing how Private/VPC deployments look — suggest Irene open on that point.
3) Deck request: can we get the attendee list (titles) and any specific SLA questions up front? Saves time for the Q&A.

Irene — if you're able to cover the VP sponsor role, Tue 08/11 at 10:30 PDT works for me (I'll be on to support the Q&A). If you prefer to pre-brief at 10:00 and have me present as the technical lead at 10:30 I'll run the live demo.

Attachments: heliohub-demo-agenda.ics

—Jordan
Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Moss <elena.moss@heliohub.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 Aug 2026 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick intro + request for VP/CIO at demo

Elena / team — I can serve as the VP sponsor but travel came up unexpectedly on Tuesday morning and I need to shift the slot by 45 minutes later, if HelioHub can accommodate. Proposed final:

 - Tue 2026-08-11 at 11:15–12:00 PDT (Irene as VP sponsor)

Jordan — happy to do a 10:45–11:00 pre-brief with you (15 min) to run through the residency slide and any asks. Ava — please send the short attendee list and any procurement questions so we can tailor the 10-minute executive summary.

Elena — if 11:15 is tight for HelioHub, we can move to Wed 08/12 14:30–15:15 PDT as a backup.

Irene Choi
VP, Field Sponsorship
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Order Form / MSA — carve-out request for IP indemnity (liability cap)
source.body:
From: Jill Tanner <jill.tanner@bluecore.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: BlueCore MSA - liability cap / IP indemnity redlines

Hi Ava / Karthik,

Attached is BlueCore's redline of the MSA and Order Form. Two items in particular are non-negotiable for them:

- A carve-out to the liability cap for IP infringement claims (they want IP indemnity and any related damages to be excluded from the liability cap).
- Clarification that consequential/indirect damages remain excluded for both parties, but the IP carve-out would survive.

They've flagged prior incidents with third-party IP claims as the rationale. Please circulate to legal and let us know whether Redwood can accept an IP carve-out, and if so whether you need any proof-of-rights / escrow remedies included.

Attachment: BlueCore_MSA_redline.docx (MS Word redline)

Best,
Jill
Jill Tanner
Director, Commercial Contracts
BlueCore, Inc.

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: BlueCore MSA - liability cap / IP indemnity redlines

Marissa — forwarding BlueCore's redline. Big deal for us (PoC live, ARR ~$320k first year). Customer wants an explicit carve-out so IP indemnity claims wouldn’t count toward the liability cap. They say it’s a standard procurement ask.

Quick context:
- Deal: BlueCore enterprise order form (annual commit)

source.subject: Private VPC: proposed control-plane/data-plane lane separation and egress termination
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: PlumeCorp Infra <infra@plumecorp.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Ava — thanks for taking this. We’re starting our internal review for PlumeCorp’s private VPC deployment and need to lock the control-plane/data-plane separation and a hardened egress termination plan before we schedule the preflight. High level asks: 

- Confirm whether Redwood requires a dedicated control-plane endpoint inside the customer VPC or if a controlled public egress with mTLS and IP allowlists is acceptable.
- We need reference schematics that show NAT/egress termination points, HA expectations, and preferred observability hooks (span tags, metrics).
- Any special requirements for KV-cache prefixing or cross-tenant routing that might require on-prem proxying.

Can you send a short proposal and a schematic we can bring to our infra review on Friday? We’re under a compliance window (internal audit) so we’ll need evidence of traffic zoning and egress filtering.

Thanks,
Rafael Mendes
Head of Platform — PlumeCorp

(Attachment: compliance-extract.csv)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 13:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Rafael — thanks for the clear ask. Attached is a short proposal and a schematic (controlplane-egress-proposal.pdf and vpc-schematic-v2.png) that outline two supported patterns:

1) Dedicated control-plane endpoint (recommended for high-compliance customers)
   - Control-plane: customer-deployed control-plane connector in a management subnet that terminates mTLS inbound from Redwood control nodes. No customer data plane traffic flows through this path.
   - Data-plane: separate tenant pod(s) in private subnets with NAT egress through a customer-managed egress appliance or cloud NAT.

2) Shared public-control with hardened egress (supported for lower-compliance customers)
   - Control-plane: public endpoint with strict mTLS + client cert pinning + IP allowlist. Requires documented allowlist windows for failover.
   - Data-plane: same as above but with additional per-tenant egress filtering enforced by Redwood sidecar and prefix-aware KV caching.

Key notes and differences in the attached PDF:
- Egress termination options (customer-managed vs Redwood-managed) and audit evidence we can provide for each.
- Observability hooks: span sampling at token ingress, per-route token cost labels, egress flow metrics and sample traces; we can drop an MRTG-style baseline for you.
- Canary approach: we propose a two-week canary using the canary-checklist.xlsx attached; it validates KVCACHE correctness and egress rule fidelity before full cutover.

Next steps I suggest:
- Quick 30-min design readout with your infra team (we can do Wed or Thu).
- If you want dedicated control-plane, we’ll provide an ops-runbook and a signed MOU for the connector deployment.

I’ll wait for a preferred time — looping in Karthik and Jonas for the detailed networking bits.

—
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0123
(Attachments: controlplane-egress-proposal.pdf, vpc-schematic-v2.png, canary-checklist.xlsx)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 10:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Rafael / team — quick follow-ups from a networking perspective after reviewing Ava’s proposal:

- If you choose dedicated control-plane (pattern 1), we strongly recommend a three-node connector cluster in your management subnet with an autoscaling policy disabled during initial rollouts (manual scale-up during emergency). This reduces transient certificate churn and makes auditing simpler.
- Egress fingerprinting: please ensure your NAT appliance preserves source port mapping or provides 1:1 SNAT for labeled control traffic; our observability relies on stable 5-tuple hashing for traces.
- MTU and fragmentation: we hit issues when customers had 1500 MTU plus overlay encapsulation. Recommend 1400 MTU for data-plane pods if you intend to use IP-in-IP or GRE for tenant isolation.
- Firewall rules: allow Redwood control IPs + Cloudflare (if using our public endpoint) on TCP 443 for mTLS. We’ll share a JSON snippet of the recommended security group/ACLs — attaching for your infra team.

I’ll join the readout. Jonas — can you add a short diagram showing where KV-cache prefixes are stored/how they're invalidated in the dedicated connector pattern?

-Karthik

(Attachment: redwood-acl-snippet.json)

From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Aug 2026 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Thanks — Rafael, we did a quick internal sketch and a short list of action items for your Friday review:

Action items (Redwood + PlumeCorp):
- PlumeCorp: provision management subnet with three control connector nodes behind the supplied ACLs (due: 2026-08-10).
- PlumeCorp: confirm egress appliance model and whether it supports 1:1 SNAT (due: 2026-08-09).
- Redwood: provide final MOU and ops-runbook for connector install (due: 2026-08-07).
- Joint: run the canary checklist (two-week window) once connector is installed; target start 2026-08-15.

Design clarifications:
- KV-cache: in the dedicated connector pattern the KV-store mapping is local to the connector; invalidation occurs via a signed lifecycle event from Redwood control when a model version rollout happens. We added a short appendix in the PDF.
- Egress auditing: we’ll supply flow-sampled traces and a compressed daily evidence bundle (TLS SNI mapping and egress IP table) for audit.

If that looks good I’ll draft the runbook headings for the MOU. Rafael — any immediate blocker on your side?

Cheers,
Jonas Weber
Principal Network Architect, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com

> On Wed, 5 Aug 2026 at 10:18 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> 
> > Rafael / team — quick follow-ups from a networking perspective after reviewing Ava’s proposal:
> > 
> > - If you choose dedicated control-plane (pattern 1), we strongly recommend a three-node connector cluster...
> > - Egress fingerprinting: please ensure your NAT appliance preserves source port mapping...
> >

title: Private VPC: proposed control-plane/data-plane lane separation and egress termination
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: PlumeCorp Infra <infra@plumecorp.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Ava — thanks for taking this. We’re starting our internal review for PlumeCorp’s private VPC deployment and need to lock the control-plane/data-plane separation and a hardened egress termination plan before we schedule the preflight. High level asks: 

- Confirm whether Redwood requires a dedicated control-plane endpoint inside the customer VPC or if a controlled public egress with mTLS and IP allowlists is acceptable.
- We need reference schematics that show NAT/egress termination points, HA expectations, and preferred observability hooks (span tags, metrics).
- Any special requirements for KV-cache prefixing or cross-tenant routing that might require on-prem proxying.

Can you send a short proposal and a schematic we can bring to our infra review on Friday? We’re under a compliance window (internal audit) so we’ll need evidence of traffic zoning and egress filtering.

Thanks,
Rafael Mendes
Head of Platform — PlumeCorp

(Attachment: compliance-extract.csv)


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 13:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Rafael — thanks for the clear ask. Attached is a short proposal and a schematic (controlplane-egress-proposal.pdf and vpc-schematic-v2.png) that outline two supported patterns:

1) Dedicated control-plane endpoint (recommended for high-compliance customers)
   - Control-plane: customer-deployed control-plane connector in a management subnet that terminates mTLS inbound from Redwood control nodes. No customer data plane traffic flows through this path.
   - Data-plane: separate tenant pod(s) in private subnets with NAT egress through a customer-managed egress appliance or cloud NAT.

2) Shared public-control with hardened egress (supported for lower-compliance customers)
   - Control-plane: public endpoint with strict mTLS + client cert pinning + IP allowlist. Requires documented allowlist windows for failover.
   - Data-plane: same as above but with additional per-tenant egress filtering enforced by Redwood sidecar and prefix-aware KV caching.

Key notes and differences in the attached PDF:
- Egress termination options (customer-managed vs Redwood-managed) and audit evidence we can provide for each.
- Observability hooks: span sampling at token ingress, per-route token cost labels, egress flow metrics and sample traces; we can drop an MRTG-style baseline for you.
- Canary approach: we propose a two-week canary using the canary-checklist.xlsx attached; it validates KVCACHE correctness and egress rule fidelity before full cutover.

Next steps I suggest:
- Quick 30-min design readout with your infra team (we can do Wed or Thu).
- If you want dedicated control-plane, we’ll provide an ops-runbook and a signed MOU for the connector deployment.

I’ll wait for a preferred time — looping in Karthik and Jonas for the detailed networking bits.

—
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0123
(Attachments: controlplane-egress-proposal.pdf, vpc-schematic-v2.png, canary-checklist.xlsx)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 10:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Rafael / team — quick follow-ups from a networking perspective after reviewing Ava’s proposal:

- If you choose dedicated control-plane (pattern 1), we strongly recommend a three-node connector cluster in your management subnet with an autoscaling policy disabled during initial rollouts (manual scale-up during emergency). This reduces transient certificate churn and makes auditing simpler.
- Egress fingerprinting: please ensure your NAT appliance preserves source port mapping or provides 1:1 SNAT for labeled control traffic; our observability relies on stable 5-tuple hashing for traces.
- MTU and fragmentation: we hit issues when customers had 1500 MTU plus overlay encapsulation. Recommend 1400 MTU for data-plane pods if you intend to use IP-in-IP or GRE for tenant isolation.
- Firewall rules: allow Redwood control IPs + Cloudflare (if using our public endpoint) on TCP 443 for mTLS. We’ll share a JSON snippet of the recommended security group/ACLs — attaching for your infra team.

I’ll join the readout. Jonas — can you add a short diagram showing where KV-cache prefixes are stored/how they're invalidated in the dedicated connector pattern?

-Karthik

(Attachment: redwood-acl-snippet.json)

From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@plumecorp.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 6 Aug 2026 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: private VPC egress + control/data separation

Thanks — Rafael, we did a quick internal sketch and a short list of action items for your Friday review:

Action items (Redwood + PlumeCorp):
- PlumeCorp: provision management subnet with three control connector nodes behind the supplied ACLs (due: 2026-08-10).
- PlumeCorp: confirm egress appliance model and whether it supports 1:1 SNAT (due: 2026-08-09).
- Redwood: provide final MOU and ops-runbook for connector install (due: 2026-08-07).
- Joint: run the canary checklist (two-week window) once connector is installed; target start 2026-08-15.

Design clarifications:
- KV-cache: in the dedicated connector pattern the KV-store mapping is local to the connector; invalidation occurs via a signed lifecycle event from Redwood control when a model version rollout happens. We added a short appendix in the PDF.
- Egress auditing: we’ll supply flow-sampled traces and a compressed daily evidence bundle (TLS SNI mapping and egress IP table) for audit.

If that looks good I’ll draft the runbook headings for the MOU. Rafael — any immediate blocker on your side?

Cheers,
Jonas Weber
Principal Network Architect, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com

> On Wed, 5 Aug 2026 at 10:18 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> 
> > Rafael / team — quick follow-ups from a networking perspective after reviewing Ava’s proposal:
> > 
> > - If you choose dedicated control-plane (pattern 1), we strongly recommend a three-node connector cluster...
> > - Egress fingerprinting: please ensure your NAT appliance preserves source port mapping...
> >
> > -Karthik

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>

Subject: Offer routing and approval matrix — Emma Jones (SWE II)

Hi team,

I have a draft offer ready for Emma Jones (SWE II, candidate from final loop last week). Before I kick the offer into Greenhouse and route for approvals, I want to confirm the routing matrix / approvers and the comp band justification so we have a clean audit trail.

Draft details (attached):
- Base: $165,000
- Target OTE (bonus): 10%
- Equity: 0.05% RSU (4y ttd)
- Proposed start: 2026-09-20

What I need from you:
1) Dylan / Markus — confirm manager sign-off on base + equity and that headcount is approved (headcount code / cost center).
2) Laura — confirm total comp sits in band C2 and OK to route for recruiter lead approval.
3) Marissa / Monica — note any HR/relocation language to include and if we should require signed offer + background before start scheduling.

Routing plan I intend to use in Greenhouse (please flag edits):
- Stage: Offer > Draft created by recruiter (me) > Manager approval (Dylan) > Recruiting Lead (Laura) > People Ops (Monica) > Finance visibility (auto-cc) > Candidate (DocuSign link)

Attachments: offer_emma_jones_v1.pdf (draft language), comp_band_guidance.pdf (internal matrix excerpt).

If I don't hear back by EOD Monday I will proceed with the routing above and loop Laura for expedited sign-off.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Technical Recruiter
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwoodinference.com

To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>

Subject: Re: Offer routing and approval matrix — Emma Jones (SWE II)

- Equity: 0.05% OK (follows the manager guidance for level).
- Headcount: FY26 HC code HC-ENG-23 is approved for this hire. Charge to cost center ENG-SERV-02.
- Start date: OK with 9/20 but would prefer 9/27 to allow for ramp planning — will defer to candidate preference.

Please route to Laura for comp band verification and Monica for People Ops language.

-Markus

Date: Sun, 2 Aug 2026 09:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Offer routing and approval matrix — Emma Jones (SWE II)

A couple of operational items to ensure smooth approvals in Greenhouse: 
- Use "Offer Template: Standard SWE" and set the internal approval checklist to include People Ops sign-off.
- I will be the recruiting lead approver. After my approval, auto-route to People Ops (Monica) then Finance for visibility.
- Please add a brief comp rationale in the "Offer Notes" box: "Above midpoint due to strong backend infra experience and 3 yrs relevant production service experience."

Monica — please confirm if we should include the relocation paragraph in section 4 (candidate is remote but requested $5k relocation stipend). If yes, I'll add it to the offer template.

Offer highlights (official packet attached):

- Base salary: $165,000
- Signing bonus: none

Next steps:

1) Dylan / Markus — confirm manager sign-off on base + equity and that headcount is approved (headcount code / cost center).
2) Laura — confirm total comp sits in band C2 and OK to route for recruiter lead approval.
3) Marissa / Monica — note any HR/relocation language to include and if we should require signed offer + background before start scheduling.

Routing plan I intend to use in Greenhouse (please flag edits):
- Stage: Offer > Draft created by recruiter (me) > Manager approval (Dylan) > Recruiting Lead (Laura) > People Ops (Monica) > Finance visibility (auto-cc) > Candidate (DocuSign link)

Attachments: offer_emma_jones_v1.pdf (draft language), comp_band_guidance.pdf (internal matrix excerpt).

If I don't hear back by EOD Monday I will proceed with the routing above and loop Laura for expedited sign-off.

Thanks,
Ava Chen

Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwoodinference.com

Subject: Re: Offer routing and approval matrix — Emma Jones (SWE II)

A few notes:
- Base $165k is fine and within my approved range for this role given experience.
- Equity: 0.05% OK (follows the manager guidance for level).
- Headcount: FY26 HC code HC-ENG-23 is approved for this hire. Charge to cost center ENG-SERV-02.
- Start date: OK with 9/20 but would prefer 9/27 to allow for ramp planning — will defer to candidate preference.

Please route to Laura for comp band verification and Monica for People Ops language.

-Markus

source.subject: Next: technical workshop + approval path for Atlas Finance
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.p@atlasfin.com>, Leila Gomez <leila.gomez@atlasfin.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.f@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up & proposed technical workshop slots

Hi Samir / Leila —

Thanks again for a productive discovery call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

- Key goals you shared: 1) low-latency inference for transaction enrichment, 2) on-prem tenant isolation for regulated pipelines, 3) SSO + SCIM for org provisioning.
- Success criteria we agreed: sub-150ms p95 for enrichment endpoint at 50 TPS; SOC2 evidence and SSO integration before pilot start.

Next steps I recommend:
1) Technical workshop (60–90 min) to walk your platform/security teams through architecture, SSO options, and performance tuning.
2) Procurement intro + mutual action plan so we can align timelines and any redlines.

Proposed workshop slots (PDT):
- Thu 23 Jul 10:30–11:30
- Fri 24 Jul 13:00–14:30
- Mon 27 Jul 09:00–10:00

If none of those work, share 2–3 windows and I’ll coordinate. I’m looping Diego (platform) on this note to cover SSO/SCIM details and Monica from Security for the compliance thread.

Attached: RDW-MAP-Atlas-20260722.pdf — draft mutual action plan; SSO-Checklist-Atlas.docx — initial SSO checklist.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Coordinating CIO/VP presence for NovaPower demo — availability check
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira@novapower.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: IT-Provisioning <it-provisioning@novapower.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the great call yesterday. As we finalize the agenda with our execs, our CIO Mark DeLong and VP of Ops want to attend a short sponsor-level demo to validate fit before we lock in a technical follow-up. Can you confirm whether Redwood can have an exec-appropriate overview (30–45 min) that covers SLA/hosting options, data controls, and a high-level cost/throughput slide?

Preferred windows from our side:
- Tue Jul 20: 9:00–10:00 AM PT
- Wed Jul 21: 2:00–3:00 PM PT

I attached a one-page agenda for reference. If a deeper tech breakout is needed, we can schedule a separate 60-minute slot.

Would appreciate confirmation of who from Redwood would join (VP/Head level) so our execs know who to expect.

Best,
Samira Khan
Head of Platform Partnerships
NovaPower

[attachment: NovaPower_Demo_Agenda_v1.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@novapower.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 09:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Hi Samira — thanks for sending the agenda. We can absolutely run a concise sponsor-level session that covers hosting modes, SLAs, and the unit economics primer.

Proposed attendees from Redwood:
- Marcus Lin, VP of Engineering (sponsor-level coverage on runtime and resilience)
- Rachel Kim, Solutions Engineer (short walkthrough and Q&A)
- Myself (product & commercial questions)

I have a potential calendar conflict the morning of Jul 20 but Marcus is usually flexible. Would a compressed 30–45 minute window on Jul 20 from 9:30–10:15 AM PT work instead of the 9:00 slot? If Mark (CIO) prefers the later 2:00 PM slot on Jul 21, we can do that — either way we can prepare a 30-minute core demo + 15-minute optional Q&A.

Attached a slightly trimmed agenda (for the 30-min format). Please confirm the preferred slot and whether Mark wants any pre-read.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Director, Solutions
Redwood Inference

[attachment: Demo_1pager.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Ava — I can join the sponsor session. Quick note: I have a leadership sync that morning that often runs long. If we aim for 9:30–10:15 AM PT on Jul 20 that should work better for me (gives a 30–45 minute buffer). If NovaPower needs later in the day on Jul 21, I can make time then too.

Marcus
VP, Engineering
Redwood Inference

From: Samira Khan <samira@novapower.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark DeLong <mdelong@novapower.com>, IT-Provisioning <it-provisioning@novapower.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Executive presence for Redwood demo - are CIO/VP available?

Thanks all — apologies for the back-and-forth. Mark confirms he can do Wed Jul 21 at 2:00 PM PT but he prefers the later slot to accommodate an upstream review. However, given Marcus's availability and Ava's suggestion, we'd like to propose this hybrid approach:

- Wed Jul 21 2:00–2:30 PM PT: sponsor-level session (Mark + VP-level attendees)
- Wed Jul 21 2:30–3:00 PM PT: optional tech Q&A with Marcus and Rachel if needed

If Redwood can commit Marcus for the optional Q&A immediately after the sponsor walkthrough, we can keep both the exec presence and the deeper technical conversation in one contiguous block. Please confirm and we will send a calendar invite (I will include a short mutual action plan).

Also attaching the mutual action plan that legal asked to see ahead of time — it's lightweight and includes the items we'll discuss in the meeting.

Confirming: Mark DeLong (CIO) will attend the sponsor slot.

Best,
Samira

[attachment: Mutual_Action_Plan.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

source.subject: Clarify renewal notice & convenience termination concessions — Polaris Health MSA
messages:
From: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@polarishealth.com
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -07:00
Subject: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement at Polaris reviewed the MSA and we have two outstanding items before we can route to Legal:

1) Auto-renew: Your standard MSA currently has a 90-day auto-renew notice window. For our internal approval cycles we typically need a 60-day opt-out window (for both pilot and enterprise contracts). Is Redwood open to a 60-day notice?

2) Termination for convenience: We prefer a mutual termination for convenience but need clarity on any termination fee or transition support. Our security/compliance team asked whether Redwood would agree to a 30-day pro-rata wind-down and a 30-day notice or whether there is a termination fee.

Attached are our procurement requirements and timeline. We are targeting a signed order form by 7/23 — ideally earlier. Can you confirm whether the 60-day notice and the proposed 30-day wind-down are acceptable, or provide alternative language Redwood prefers?

Appreciate a quick call if easier.

Thanks,
Elaine Wang
Senior Procurement Manager
Polaris Health
[Attachment: Polaris_Procurement_Requirements.pdf (pdf)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Hi Elaine — great to hear from you and thanks for the clear asks. Looping in Karthik (Legal) and Laura (Sales Ops) to confirm. Quick response below and we can follow up with tracked redlines this week.

Summary of Redwood's initial position (open to compromise):
- Auto-renew: We prefer 90-day notice for enterprise MSA to ensure capacity planning and orderly rollback. That said, we can accept 60 days for Polaris given pilot->enterprise path — would ask for a one-year minimum initial term and a 60-day opt-out for renewals thereafter.
- Termination for convenience: Redwood generally allows mutual termination for convenience with a pro-rated refund for prepaid fees and a customer-obligated transition fee for active dedicated capacity (if applicable). For standard hosted customers we usually require either: (a) 30 days' fees as a termination fee, or (b) 30 days' notice with Redwood providing 30 days of transition support (whichever is greater).

Proposed compromise we can put in redline form: 60-day written notice to opt-out of renewal; mutual termination for convenience permitted with either 30 days' transition support or payment equal to 30 days of average monthly fees (customer's choice). This keeps your 60-day preference while protecting Redwood operationally.

I'll attach a draft with tracked changes (Redwood_MSA_v3_redlines.docx). Karthik/Laura — can you confirm legal + billing are comfortable with the language below; if so I'll send formal redlines to Elaine and Polaris Legal.

Proposed snippet (informal):
"Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon sixty (60) days' prior written notice. Following receipt of such notice Redwood will provide up to thirty (30) days of transition support, or Customer may elect to pay a termination fee equal to thirty (30) days of the then-applicable monthly charges, pro-rated as applicable."

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20–30 minute call if Polaris would like to walk through points.

Best,
Ava Chen
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redlines.docx (docx)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 13:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Ava / Laura — quick legal view. Overall the compromise Ava proposed is reasonable for the typical enterprise pilot -> paid conversion we see. A couple of notes for drafting:

- Auto-renew language: make sure the clause states "automatic renewal for successive one (1) year terms" and clearly sets the notice window for non-renewal to "not less than sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current term." That prevents ambiguity around partial-month timing.

- Termination for convenience: Redwood should keep the customer choice (transition support v. termination fee) but we should also add a short carve-out for dedicated capacity that requires longer notice (e.g., 90 days) or reimbursement of decommitment costs. Suggest adding: "Notwithstanding the foregoing, termination of Agreement with respect to Dedicated Capacity requires ninety (90) days' prior written notice and reimbursement of decommissioning or re-provisioning costs reasonably incurred by Redwood."

- Data/Export obligations: add explicit export window of 30 days following termination and include obligations for customer to request data export in writing during that window. (Polaris's security team will expect this explicitly called out.)

If you want, I can prepare tracked redlines to the MSA and a one-page summary for your procurement tracker. Also recommend we set an internal approval threshold: if Polaris requests waiver of the dedicated-capacity carve-out, escalate to Legal Director.

Karthik
Associate General Counsel, Redwood Inference

On internal coordination: Laura, can you confirm whether Polaris's Order Form shows any dedicated pool line items (I saw their procurement doc but want to double-check billing lines)? If not, the 60-day path is straightforward.

From: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@polarishealth.com
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 15:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Ava / Karthik — thanks for the fast turnaround and for the proposed compromise. Polaris accepts the 60-day opt-out for renewals under the one-year initial term. We also accept the mutual termination framework where the customer chooses transition support or a 30-day fee.

Couple of asks before we send to Legal:
- Please confirm if our Order Form will show any "Dedicated Capacity" line items. If not, we will not need the 90-day carve-out.
- Please send the tracked redlines (MSA) + updated Order Form (showing any dedicated SKU if applicable).

We aim to finish Legal review by 7/20 so procurement can complete signatures by 7/23. If you can push the redlines and DocuSign envelope this week that would be ideal.

Thanks,
Elaine Wang
Senior Procurement Manager
Polaris Health
[Attachment: OrderForm_Polaris_Rev1.pdf (pdf)]

On internal coordination: Laura, can you confirm whether Polaris's Order Form shows any dedicated pool line items (I saw their procurement doc but want to double-check billing lines)? If not, the 60-day path is straightforward.


From: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@polarishealth.com
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 15:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Ava / Karthik — thanks for the fast turnaround and for the proposed compromise. Polaris accepts the 60-day opt-out for renewals under the one-year initial term. We also accept the mutual termination framework where the customer chooses transition support or a 30-day fee.

Couple of asks before we send to Legal:
- Please confirm if our Order Form will show any \"Dedicated Capacity\" line items. If not, we will not need the 90-day carve-out.
- Please send the tracked redlines (MSA) + updated Order Form (showing any dedicated SKU if applicable).

We aim to finish Legal review by 7/20 so procurement can complete signatures by 7/23. If you can push the redlines and DocuSign envelope this week that would be ideal.

Thanks,
Elaine Wang
Senior Procurement Manager
Polaris Health
[Attachment: OrderForm_Polaris_Rev1.pdf (pdf)]
text:
From: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@polarishealth.com
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -07:00
Subject: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement at Polaris reviewed the MSA and we have two outstanding items before we can route to Legal:

1) Auto-renew: Your standard MSA currently has a 90-day auto-renew notice window. For our internal approval cycles we typically need a 60-day opt-out window (for both pilot and enterprise contracts). Is Redwood open to a 60-day notice?

2) Termination for convenience: We prefer a mutual termination for convenience but need clarity on any termination fee or transition support. Our security/compliance team asked whether Redwood would agree to a 30-day pro-rata wind-down and a 30-day notice or whether there is a termination fee.

Attached are our procurement requirements and timeline. We are targeting a signed order form by 7/23 — ideally earlier. Can you confirm whether the 60-day notice and the proposed 30-day wind-down are acceptable, or provide alternative language Redwood prefers?

Appreciate a quick call if easier.

Thanks,
Elaine Wang
Senior Procurement Manager
Polaris Health
[Attachment: Polaris_Procurement_Requirements.pdf (pdf)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Hi Elaine — great to hear from you and thanks for the clear asks. Looping in Karthik (Legal) and Laura (Sales Ops) to confirm. Quick response below and we can follow up with tracked redlines this week.

Summary of Redwood's initial position (open to compromise):
- Auto-renew: We prefer 90-day notice for enterprise MSA to ensure capacity planning and orderly rollback. That said, we can accept 60 days for Polaris given pilot->enterprise path — would ask for a one-year minimum initial term and a 60-day opt-out for renewals thereafter.
- Termination for convenience: Redwood generally allows mutual termination for convenience with a pro-rated refund for prepaid fees and a customer-obligated transition fee for active dedicated capacity (if applicable). For standard hosted customers we usually require either: (a) 30 days' fees as a termination fee, or (b) 30 days' notice with Redwood providing 30 days of transition support (whichever is greater).

Proposed compromise we can put in redline form: 60-day written notice to opt-out of renewal; mutual termination for convenience permitted with either 30 days' transition support or payment equal to 30 days of average monthly fees (customer's choice). This keeps your 60-day preference while protecting Redwood operationally.

I'll attach a draft with tracked changes (Redwood_MSA_v3_redlines.docx). Karthik/Laura — can you confirm legal + billing are comfortable with the language below; if so I'll send formal redlines to Elaine and Polaris Legal.

Proposed snippet (informal):
\"Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon sixty (60) days' prior written notice. Following receipt of such notice Redwood will provide up to thirty (30) days of transition support, or Customer may elect to pay a termination fee equal to thirty (30) days of the then-applicable monthly charges, pro-rated as applicable.\"

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20–30 minute call if Polaris would like to walk through points.

Best,
Ava Chen
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_MSA_v3_redlines.docx (docx)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 13:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Ava / Laura — quick legal view. Overall the compromise Ava proposed is reasonable for the typical enterprise pilot -> paid conversion we see. A couple of notes for drafting:

- Auto-renew language: make sure the clause states \"automatic renewal for successive one (1) year terms\" and clearly sets the notice window for non-renewal to \"not less than sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current term.\" That prevents ambiguity around partial-month timing.

- Termination for convenience: Redwood should keep the customer choice (transition support v. termination fee) but we should also add a short carve-out for dedicated capacity that requires longer notice (e.g., 90 days) or reimbursement of decommitment costs. Suggest adding: \"Notwithstanding the foregoing, termination of Agreement with respect to Dedicated Capacity requires ninety (90) days' prior written notice and reimbursement of decommissioning or re-provisioning costs reasonably incurred by Redwood.\"

- Data/Export obligations: add explicit export window of 30 days following termination and include obligations for customer to request data export in writing during that window. (Polaris's security team will expect this explicitly called out.)

If you want, I can prepare tracked redlines to the MSA and a one-page summary for your procurement tracker. Also recommend we set an internal approval threshold: if Polaris requests waiver of the dedicated-capacity carve-out, escalate to Legal Director.

Karthik
Associate General Counsel, Redwood Inference

On internal coordination: Laura, can you confirm whether Polaris's Order Form shows any dedicated pool line items (I saw their procurement doc but want to double-check billing lines)? If not, the 60-day path is straightforward.


From: Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@polarishealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@polarishealth.com
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 15:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Polaris MSA — auto-renew notice window & termination for convenience

Ava / Karthik — thanks for the fast turnaround and for the proposed compromise. Polaris accepts the 60-day opt-out for renewals under the one-year initial term. We also accept the mutual termination framework where the customer chooses transition support or a 30-day fee.

Couple of asks before we send to Legal:
- Please confirm if our Order Form will show any \"Dedicated Capacity\" line items. If not, we will not need the 90-day carve-out.
- Please send the tracked redlines (MSA) + updated Order Form (showing any dedicated SKU if applicable).

We aim to finish Legal review by 7/20 so procurement can complete signatures by 7/23. If you can push the redlines and DocuSign envelope this week that would be ideal.

Thanks,
Elaine Wang
Senior Procurement Manager
Polaris Health
