source.subject: Settlement summary and conditional credit for May overage
messages:
From: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Finora Billing <billing@finora.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Hi Ava,

Quick note — our billing team reconciled May usage and found a 42k token delta compared to the committed capacity lines. It's driven by a handful of high-concurrency jobs during our product launch week (May 18–22).

Can you share a final invoice or settlement figure we can route to procurement? We need it broken down: one-time overage charge, any credits you can apply, and whether there's a short-term capacity top-up option.

Attaching our usage export. Happy to hop on a short call if helpful.

Thanks,
Samira
Samira Patel
Head of Ops, Finora Health
phone: (415) 555-0133

Attachments: Finora_usage_export_May2026.csv (CSV, 120KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Samira — thanks for the export and the heads-up. Diego and Grace copied for finance/CS context.

We ran the reconciler against our ingestion and confirm the ~42k token overage for May. Below is our preliminary settlement approach:

- One-time invoice: $3,150 (42k tokens at $0.075/token unit equivalent per your plan)
- Conditional credit: 50% of the one-time invoice ($1,575) if Finora signs the proposed 12-month capacity top-up amendment within 14 days (amendment adds 25% committed capacity and a 12-month term extension at current pricing).
- If amendment is not signed within 14 days, full one-time invoice will be issued and due in 30 days.

We can provide a formal PDF proposal and DocuSign link. Grace — can finance confirm the line-item and draft the PDF? Jonas — please prepare a short note on operational safeguards we can include (burst limits, alerting) so Samira's team can approve faster.

Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

Attachments: none

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Hi Samira, Ava —

I've attached a short settlement proposal PDF with the invoice line and the conditional credit language. The one-time invoice amount is $3,150; conditional credit of $1,575 applied as a credit memo to the next invoice once the amendment is fully executed and backdated to the start of the next billing cycle.

Payment terms: Net 30 on the invoice. Credit memo will appear within 2 billing cycles after signing and verification.

If you prefer the credit to be a direct refund instead of a credit memo, we can discuss that with finance but prefer credit memo to keep accounts tidy.

Diego
Diego Fernandez
Director, Finance Operations, Redwood Inference

Attachments: Redwood_settlement_proposal_06026.pdf (PDF, 48KB)

From: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Finora Billing <billing@finora.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 07:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Thanks, Diego — that looks reasonable. A couple quick clarifications before I take this to procurement:

1) Does the 12-month amendment change price per token for the new committed capacity? (We want rate certainty.)
2) If we sign, will the credit be visible on the invoice line-level so our procurement folks can reconcile easily?

If those are yes, we'll sign the amendment. Can you send the DocuSign link?

—Samira

On Wed, Jun 24, 2026 at 07:22 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
>
> [Quoted earlier message]
>

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 09:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May usage discrepancy & proposed settlement

Samira — quick answers from product/ops side:

1) Rate for the added committed capacity will remain at your current per-token unit price for the 12-month term. We'll include an explicit line in the amendment: "committed capacity price fixed at current schedule for the duration of the amendment."
2) Credit visibility: the credit memo will include a line referencing the original invoice number and a short description ("May 2026 Settlement Credit - RDW-4521"). We will also attach the credit memo PDF to the next invoice in the billing portal.

Additionally, I've added two operational items to the amendment: 24/7 alerting when utilization exceeds 85% of committed capacity for three consecutive hours, and an optional burst bucket (prepaid) offer. That should reduce surprise spikes.

Jonas Weber
Sr. Product Ops, Redwood Inference

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.p@finora.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Finora Billing <billing@finora.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Final — settlement confirmed and DocuSign link

Samira — thanks for the quick turnaround. Per our calls and the thread above, we have: 

- One-time invoice for May overage: $3,150 (issued today).
- Conditional credit: $1,575 (50% of invoice) to be issued as a credit memo against your next invoice once the amendment is executed and DocuSigned within 14 days.
- Amendment: 12-month capacity top-up (25% additional committed capacity), price fixed at current schedule. Operational safeguards included (alerts + optional burst bucket).

DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=abcd-1234

Please route to procurement/billing. Once signed, Diego will backdate and post the credit memo. Grace will follow up with invoice numbers and a short reconciliation note for your AP team.

Thanks — looking forward to continuing to scale with Finora.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

Attachments: Signed_Amendment_RDW-4521.pdf (PDF, 72KB)

source.subject: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 09:17:00 -07:00
Subject: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for the run-through yesterday. To make the dedicated capacity plan concrete for your SRE and procurement teams, I attached two artifacts: a capacity-modeling workbook (edgehealth_capacity_modeling.xlsx) and a proposed ops-ownership matrix (ops-ownership-matrix.csv).

As discussed, we want to align on:
- Peak concurrency and expected burst patterns (typical business hours vs promo windows)
- Target latency SLOs (p50/p90/p99 tail targets and acceptable degradation under burst)
- Region preference for primary capacity and acceptable failover region(s)

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 10:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Thanks Jordan — good numbers. I ran quick sims in the attached workbook with the inputs you gave (see tab "3x spike sim"). Short summary:

- Scenario A (Reserved + Burst): baseline pool sized at 200 concurrent GPUs (to comfortably serve 180 steady-state with room for cache/KV). Add a burst band of 800 concurrent for the 10–15 minute spikes. With pre-warm (30m prior) and admission control, median token cost increase ~18% over baseline but tail latency stays under p99 target for 95% of spikes.

- Scenario B (Smaller baseline + Multi-region spill): baseline 120 concurrent + auto-spill to us-east1 for sustained spikes >5 minutes. Cost baseline lower by ~12% but operational risk increases: cross-region KV consistency, cold-starts in secondary region add ~1.2–1.5s tail latency on first megacache misses.

My recommendation: proceed with Scenario A for initial activation to meet SLAs, but include a gated multi-region spill plan as an opt-in for specific event windows (we can enable per-campaign). That gives EdgeHealth predictability and the option to save costs when appropriate.

Notes on autoscale policy (draft):
- Warm-up: pre-warm for scheduled events (T-30m) via short-lived pre-provision calls.
- Burst admission: queue + probed admission for requests exceeding baseline until warmed nodes return to service.
- Max burst window: 20 minutes unconstrained; beyond that, we either escalate to multi-region spill or trigger manual approval to expand reserved pool.

I attached the ops-ownership matrix with suggested day-to-day responsibilities: EdgeHealth SRE owns traffic shaping rules and feature flags for pre-warm, Redwood owns underlying node health and kernel-level fallbacks + 24/7 on-call escalation.

— Priyom

source.subject: Request: Quarterly invoicing + Net-75 term for Quadrant Health (HQ-2026-042)
messages:
From: Sophia Bennett <sophia.bennett@quadranthealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 27 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the conversation this morning. As discussed, Quadrant Health's fiscal calendar is moving to a quarterly remittance model for large vendor contracts. We are requesting the following non-standard billing terms for the engagement under contract HQ-2026-042:

- Move from monthly invoices to quarterly invoices (one invoice per quarter)
- Change payment terms from Net-30 to Net-75
- Option to split quarterly invoices into up to 2 milestones if required by procurement

We recognize this is outside typical Redwood terms. The driver is their treasury consolidation project; they can only process quarterly vendor schedules for any vendor above $50k ARR.

Marco Ruiz (copied) is Quadrant's procurement lead and can provide any additional paperwork. If you need a formal procurement request I can loop in our legal / procurement team.

Attached: Quadrant_procurement_memo.pdf (summary of policy)

Best,

Sophia Bennett
Director, Vendor Relations
Quadrant Health
[sophia.bennett@quadranthealth.com]

---
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 27 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Ben / Marissa — forwarding Quadrant's request. 

Summary: they want quarterly invoicing + Net-75 (reason: treasury consolidation). Deal size ~ $420k ARR, first year committed. They asked if we can split a quarterly invoice into 2 milestone invoices (they prefer one, but procurement might insist).

Suggested trade-offs we could consider:
- Require 25% prepayment at kickoff and then quarterly invoices + Net-75 on the balance, or
- Offer Net-75 in exchange for a 2% cash discount if they pay within 30 days of invoice, or
- Stick to Net-60 instead of Net-75 and allow quarterly invoices.

Need: quick decision — Quadrant wants an answer by Monday EOD their time (Tue morning PT). Can you advise on finance tolerance and whether this needs exec sign-off? I'm happy to own commercial language and capture amendment in DocuSign.

Thanks,
Ava

[Attachment: Quadrant_procurement_memo.pdf] 

On Sat, Jun 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM Sophia Bennett <sophia.bennett@quadranthealth.com> wrote:
> (original message pasted)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 02:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Ava / Marissa — thanks for flagging. I support accommodating Quadrant given strategic nature (Healthcare vertical pilot) but only with mitigations. Proposal for approval: 

1) Require 25% upfront (non-refundable) at SOW signature; remainder invoiced quarterly.
2) Approve Net-75 on the quarterly invoices only if Quadrant accepts a 2% early-payment discount (pay within 30 days) or if they accept electronic ACH only.
3) Limit the concession to a 12-month term; revert to standard billing cadence after renewal unless explicitly re-approved.

I've attached a redline to the standard SOW marking the prepayment and discount language. If Finance gives the cashflow OK, I can present to Vivek for exec sign-off.

-Ben

[Attachment: qhealth_contract_redline.pdf]

On Sat, Jun 27, 2026 at 11:03 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> (forwarded message)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 09:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Team — quick finance read.

Net-75 + quarterly invoicing increases DSO risk materially. With a $420k ARR figure, this could push receivables out by >60 days vs. Net-30. That said, the 25% upfront significantly mitigates exposure. If we do this, I recommend: 

- 25% prepayment at SOW signature (recognize per ASC guidance on delivery terms)
- Quarterly invoices for the balance with Net-60 as a preference (Net-75 only if we include a 2% early-payment discount or other credit protection)
- Add a clause allowing us to invoice immediately for any professional services or onboarding expenses to improve cashflow.

I've attached a proposed invoice schedule (shows timing and expected cashflow) and created BILL-2117 to track accounting changes and revenue recognition notes. Legal should sign off on the redline Ben attached.

If you want to escalate, Vivek — Ben is asking for exec approval for the Net-75 concession tied to the prepayment and discount.

-Marissa

[Attachment: invoice_schedule_qhealth.xlsx]
[Related ticket: https://jira.internal.redwood/browse/BILL-2117]
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042) — Exec decision

Ben / Marissa / Ava — after reviewing the trade-offs I'm comfortable approving a limited concession to accommodate Quadrant, given the strategic value in Healthcare. Approvals and conditions below: 

- Approve: Quarterly invoicing AND Net-75 on quarterly invoices.
- Conditions:
  1) 25% prepayment at contract signing, applied to the first quarter invoice.
  2) 2% early-payment discount (applies if Quadrant pays any invoice within 30 days).
  3) Concession limited to the initial 12-month term; renewal to default to Net-30 and monthly invoicing unless re-approved.
  4) Legal to add language for milestone invoicing (no more than 2 splits per quarter) and a clause allowing Redwood to accelerate invoices in case of missed payments.

Ava — please capture this as an amendment and route via DocuSign. Marissa, please confirm accounting entries for the prepayment and that BILL-2117 is assigned to revenue operations. Ben — run the concession as a deal note in HubSpot (link below) and ensure procurement memo is attached.

Thanks — Vivek

[Action: Ava to send DocuSign amendment 
DocuSign prep: https://docusign.com/prepare/HQ-2026-042]

source.subject: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the productive conversation yesterday. As discussed, we want to kick off the renewal conversation early so we can align on:

- Capacity uplift (targeting ~2x average throughput with burst headroom)
- Term options (12 vs 24 month pricing cadence and commit levels)
- Procurement timeline and PO milestones
- QBR timeline and attendees to surface technical runbooks and SLOs

Next steps I propose:
1) Quick QBR to review usage, latency/cost profile, and uplift plan (30–45m) — propose windows below.
2) Share a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and term amendment draft (attached).
3) Confirm procurement contact and any legal lead for redlines.

Possible QBR windows (PST):
- Tue Jun 22, 10:00–10:45
- Wed Jun 23, 14:00–14:45
- Thu Jun 24, 09:30–10:15

I've attached a MAP and a term amendment draft to get the ball rolling. Can you confirm who on your side should receive the MAP (procurement / legal)?

Attachments: [Orbital_Mutual_Action_Plan_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Term_Amendment_Draft.docx (docx)]

Thanks — looking forward to aligning.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0193

Redwood Inference — production-first LLM inference for developers
From: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim Ortega <tim.ortega@orbital-analytics.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the note and for the MAP. Quick answers:

- Procurement lead is Tim Ortega (cc'd). He will handle PO and timing.
- We are actively evaluating a 2x capacity uplift; can you provide a ballpark pricing estimate (list vs committed) we can tee up with Finance?
- Security ask: please include SOC 2 attestation and our security questionnaire responses in the QBR materials. We may need an abbreviated SSO/SCIM plan for identity mapping.

Of the proposed slots, Tue Jun 22 10:00 PST works best for our core team (myself, Tim, and our Platform Lead Jonah Park). If you can confirm, we'll calendar it.

I attached a short order-of-magnitude costing request that our finance team uses to evaluate uplift scenarios.

Attachment: [Order_of_Magnitude_Costing.pdf (pdf)]

Regards,
Elena Torres
Head of Platform, Orbital Analytics
elena@orbital-analytics.com
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 14:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Thanks Elena — putting Tim on the thread is perfect.

Ava / Elena: I'll join the QBR to cover capacity planning and expected latency/throughput tradeoffs. I'd also like to loop in Priya (Solutions Engineering) to present the uplift runbook and a short demo of warm cache behavior under the proposed autoscaling profile. Priya, can you join Tue Jun 22 10:00 PST?

High-level asks from our side to make the QBR productive:
- Please share a 1–2 page doc that describes peak workload patterns (burst length, percent tail traffic) — this helps us tune batching and KV cache strategies.
- If SOC 2/SSO material is required, we'll bring an on-call security engineer to the QBR or follow up immediately after with artifacts.

A quick note on pricing: we can prepare a committed uplift proposal (12 or 24 month) and a short conversion table showing committed discount vs on-demand. Will post the estimate before the QBR.

Thanks — Marcus
Marcus Lin
Director, Customer Solutions, Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2026 09:00:00 -0700
Subject: Confirming QBR (Tue Jun 22 10:00 PST) + updated MAP and pricing summary

Elena / Tim / Team —

Confirmed: Tue Jun 22, 10:00–10:45 PST. Calendar invite sent.

I've updated the Mutual Action Plan with milestones for legal/procurement, an uplift timeline, and a price-summary page (see attachment). Key items included:
- Target uplift: 2.0x baseline average, 3.0x short burst headroom
- Proposed committed tiers: 12m (10% discount) / 24m (18% discount) — ballpark numbers in summary
- Procurement milestones: PO by 2026-07-15, contract countersigned by 2026-07-22

Attachments: [Orbital_Mutual_Action_Plan_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Redwood_Capacity_Pricing_Estimate.pdf (pdf)]

Deal ID: DL-2026-ORB-73 — please reference in any procurement notes.

Agenda for the QBR (30–45m):
1) 10m — Usage/SLAs and current performance overview (Marcus)
2) 10m — Uplift plan and runbook (Priya)
3) 10m — Pricing ranges and term options (Ava)
4) 5–15m — Procurement/legal next steps and Q&A

If you need SOC 2 artifacts ahead of the meeting, flag here and Rishi will share a short packet.

Thanks all — looking forward to it.

Best,
Ava
From: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim Ortega <tim.ortega@orbital-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Confirming QBR (Tue Jun 22 10:00 PST) + updated MAP and pricing summary

Thanks — confirmed on our side. Tim will share PO template details during the QBR and will follow up with procurement timeline. We'll also send over a short security questionnaire by Monday morning for Rishi to pick up.

One ask: could you include an item in the MAP for a short post-QBR technical deep-dive (30m) to walk through SCIM/SSO and the runbook? That will help our Platform team validate the integration points.

See you tomorrow.

Elena

messages:
From: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 14:05:00 -0400
Subject: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Hi Ava,

As we get into renewal planning for our account, Connor and I want to schedule the QBR + roadmap demo. Our execs have very tight calendars next week and would prefer a compact, focused format rather than a full-day workshop.

High-level ask: can Redwood run a short, split-format QBR — one exec-facing 30-minute review (business impact + renewal ask) and two 45-minute lane-specific deep dives (product roadmap and ops/compliance)? We think that will get the right stakeholders in for the right parts without losing momentum.

Preferred windows (ET):
- Tue 6/22, 11:00–13:30 ET (we can do an 11:00–11:30 exec slot followed by the dives)
- Wed 6/23, 14:00–16:30 ET

Must-haves: a short attendee matrix upfront so we can confirm who attends which lane, and a SOC 2 / data handling blurb in the ops/compliance lane (Connor will loop Legal if needed).

Attaching an attendee matrix draft for your host planning.

Thanks,
Amelia
Amelia Cross
Head of Platform Partnerships | Bluehaven Health
[attachment: attendee-matrix.csv]
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Amelia — thanks for the clear ask and the attendee matrix. We can absolutely run a compact, lane-based QBR. Proposed structure below (I built it so we can run it as a tight 2.5-hour block with a host rotation and a recorded exec summary):

Agenda proposal (total 2.5 hours):
- 11:00–11:30 ET: Exec review (Ava + exec owner) — renewal context, top metrics, ask
- 11:35–12:20 ET: Product roadmap lane (Stephanie) — feature highlights, roadmap, prioritized asks (45m)
- 12:25–13:10 ET: Ops & compliance lane (Rishi) — deployment model, SOC2/data handling, support SLA (45m)
- 13:10–13:25 ET: Quick next-step and action owner wrap (10–15m)

Host rotation: Ava will open the exec review and run the wrap; Stephanie will lead product lane; Rishi will lead compliance lane and has the SOC2 summary. We’ll record the exec review and provide the recording + one-pager afterwards.

We can do Tue 6/22 starting at 11:00 ET as you suggested — I’ll hold the slot tentatively. Linked below are the draft agenda and a DocuSign NDA we typically send before demos with sensitive access.

Attachments: draft-agenda.pdf (attached), attendee-matrix.csv (from you)
Links: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abc123def456, https://www.docusign.net/link-to-NDA-xyz

If that works, I’ll send calendar invites with explicit lane invites (exec only; product + ops attendee lists) and a short pre-read.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0190
From: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Connor Patel <connor@bluehaven.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 09:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Thanks, Ava — that structure looks great. Two small adjustments from our side:

1) CEO constraint: our CEO can only join 11:45–12:15 ET on Tue. Can we shift the exec review to start at 11:30ET and run 11:30–12:00ET instead? That would still give you time for the lane dives afterward if we compress slightly.

2) Ops/legal ask: Connor’s legal lead may join for a 15–20 minute SOC2 discussion; could Rishi have a 10-slide compliance one-pager available?

We confirm the attendee matrix and will finalize the list by EOD Monday.

Forwarding your schedule suggestion below for context.

On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 4:12 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Amelia — thanks for the clear ask and the attendee matrix. We can absolutely run a compact, lane-based QBR. Proposed structure below (I built it so we can run it as a tight 2.5-hour block with a host rotation and a recorded exec summary):
>
> Agenda proposal (total 2.5 hours):
> - 11:00–11:30 ET: Exec review (Ava + exec owner) — renewal context, top metrics, ask
> - 11:35–12:20 ET: Product roadmap lane (Stephanie) — feature highlights, roadmap, prioritized asks (45m)
> - 12:25–13:10 ET: Ops & compliance lane (Rishi) — deployment model, SOC2/data handling, support SLA (45m)
> - 13:10–13:25 ET: Quick next-step and action owner wrap (10–15m)
>
> Host rotation: Ava will open the exec review and run the wrap; Stephanie will lead product lane; Rishi will lead compliance lane and has the SOC2 summary.

—
Amelia
From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>, Connor Patel <connor@bluehaven.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Looping in a short ops note and confirming host coverage.

Ava — shifting the exec review to 11:30–12:00 ET on Tue 6/22 works for our team. I’ll compress the product lane slightly to 11:35–12:10 and Rishi can take 12:15–13:00 as discussed.

Rishi — can you confirm you have the SOC2 one-pager and a 10-slide backup for Legal? I attached a draft compliance one-pager you can reuse (compliance-onepager.pdf). Also: please capture the exact question set we should expect from Bluehaven Legal — Connor, if you can share any specifics, that helps us prepare.

Action items:
- Ava: send calendar invites with lane-specific attendee lists and attach draft-agenda.pdf (I’ll own slide deck for product lane)
- Rishi: finalize compliance one-pager and a 10-slide deck; confirm whether you need any infra access to reproduce logs in-demo (we should avoid sharing PII)
- Amelia/Connor: finalize attendee list by EOD Mon (we’ll lock invites at that point)

Attachments: compliance-onepager.pdf

Thanks — I’m good to present the roadmap lane and will coordinate with PM to keep it focused on impact + timelines.

—Stephanie Nguyen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[attachment: compliance-onepager.pdf]
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Connor Patel <connor@bluehaven.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Jun 2026 10:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Amelia / team — I’ve reviewed the timeline and the compliance one-pager. I’ll prepare the 10-slide compliance deck and have it uploaded by EOD Monday. I’ll also be prepared to run the SOC2 block at 12:15–12:35 ET and stay on for follow-up until 13:00 ET if needed.

One quick ask from Legal side: can we schedule a 20-minute pre-call with Bluehaven Legal on Mon 6/21 at 11:00 ET to triage questions? If Connor can confirm availability, I’ll send an invite.

I’ll also include a redacted logs checklist in the slides and will not expose any customer PII during the demo.

Thanks — looking forward to the session.

Rishi Malhotra
Head of Security Solutions, Redwood Inference
[will attach compliance slides before meeting]

- 11:00–11:30 ET: Exec review (Ava + exec owner) — renewal context, top metrics, ask
- 11:35–12:20 ET: Product roadmap lane (Stephanie) — feature highlights, roadmap, prioritized asks (45m)
- 12:25–13:10 ET: Ops & compliance lane (Rishi) — deployment model, SOC2/data handling, support SLA (45m)
- 13:10–13:25 ET: Quick next-step and action owner wrap (10–15m)

Host rotation: Ava will open the exec review and run the wrap; Stephanie will lead product lane; Rishi will lead compliance lane and has the SOC2 summary. We’ll record the exec review and provide the recording + one-pager afterwards.

We can do Tue 6/22 starting at 11:00 ET as you suggested — I’ll hold the slot tentatively. Linked below are the draft agenda and a DocuSign NDA we typically send before demos with sensitive access.

Attachments: draft-agenda.pdf (attached), attendee-matrix.csv (from you)
Links: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abc123def456, https://www.docusign.net/link-to-NDA-xyz

If that works, I’ll send calendar invites with explicit lane invites (exec only; product + ops attendee lists) and a short pre-read.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0190

2) Ops/legal ask: Connor’s legal lead may join for a 15–20 minute SOC2 discussion; could Rishi have a 10-slide compliance one-pager available?

Forwarding your schedule suggestion below for context.

Also: please capture the exact question set we should expect from Bluehaven Legal — Connor, if you can share any specifics, that helps us prepare.

Action items:

source.subject: Coordinating architect deep-dive — sunrise/sunset overlap options
messages:
From: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: HorizonHealth Architects <architects@horizonhealth.io>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 08:20:00 -0500
Subject: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the intro last week. Our solutions and infra architects (CC'd) would like a technical deep-dive focused on your runtime's KV cache behavior and multi-region failover. We need about 75 minutes (35–40m demo + 30m Q&A + buffer). Attendees are in Boston (US/Eastern), Berlin (CET), and Singapore (SGT).

Can you propose a few windows that give at least a 60–75 minute overlap across the three sites? DST is a little messy for us this quarter so any guidance on safe windows would help. We can be flexible on the exact day next week (Jun 28–Jul 2).

Also — please share a short agenda and any pre-read we should circulate.

Best,
Tom
Tom Hargreaves
Director, Platform Engineering
HorizonHealth

---
Attachment: horizon-infra-topics.pdf (PDF stub)
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Logan / Marissa — heads up, HorizonHealth asking for a 75-min deep-dive covering KV cache + cross-region failover. Attendees: Boston (EDT), Berlin (CEST?), Singapore (SGT). They suggested dates Jun 28–Jul 2 but said DST is messy.

Quick notes:
- We should avoid US late-afternoon which is late-night in SG. Best overlap windows typically are: early US morning / late EU afternoon / evening SG, or late US evening / early EU morning / morning SG — both have tradeoffs for on-call.
- Proposal: provide two safe multi-day options and one compromise that sacrifices one region's convenience (we can rotate for future sessions).

Can you draft three concrete time slots (UTC) with conversion table and attach the agenda + a short pre-read? I can take the external send once we align.

Thanks — Ava

--
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: request-notes.txt (text stub)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 13:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Ava / Marissa — draft slots below (I included UTC and local conversions; attaching a quick CSV matrix). I tried to pick times that give >= 70 minutes overlap.

Option A (US-anchored morning):
- UTC 13:00–14:15 (Boston 09:00–10:15 EDT, Berlin 15:00–16:15 CEST, Singapore 21:00–22:15 SGT)
Pros: Boston morning, Berlin afternoon; con: Singapore evening.

Option B (EU-anchored afternoon):
- UTC 15:30–16:45 (Boston 11:30–12:45 EDT, Berlin 17:30–18:45 CEST, Singapore 23:30–00:45 SGT)
Pros: Berlin comfortable; con: Singapore late-night shortfall for some.

Option C (APAC-early compromise):
- UTC 04:30–05:45 (Boston 00:30–01:45 EDT, Berlin 06:30–07:45 CEST, Singapore 12:30–13:45 SGT)
Pros: Singapore midday; con: Boston overnight, Berlin early morning.

I recommend we lead with Option A (most friendly to US and EU, tolerable evening for SG if they can do it once). If they prefer rotating convenience we can propose a follow-up with Option C as the standard for future hands-on labs.

Attached: timezone-overlap-matrix.csv (rows: UTC, Boston, Berlin, Singapore) and a two-slide agenda PDF for the deep-dive.

--
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

Attachments: timezone-overlap-matrix.csv, redwood-deepdive-agenda.pdf
From: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: HorizonHealth Architects <architects@horizonhealth.io>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 10:45:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Thanks, Logan and Ava — this is really helpful. We reviewed the three options internally with the Berlin and Singapore architects. Short answers:
- Option A (UTC 13:00) looks best for this session — Berlin can make that in their afternoon; Boston is easy; Singapore is willing to join at 21:00 SGT for a one-off.
- Can we schedule for Tue Jun 29, UTC 13:00–14:15 (BST/CEST note: Berlin will be UTC+2)? Please mark calendar invite for 75 minutes and include the agenda + a link to a shared test cluster we can use for the KV cache demo.

Two small asks: 1) Can you add an abbreviated timezone note in the invite body (local times for attendees)? 2) One of our architects asked if you have a fallback window (Option C) in case someone in SG must step out; they'd prefer an AM Singapore fallback.

If you send a calendar invite, I'll confirm attendees and the test-cluster credentials request with our infra team.

Best,
Tom

--
Tom Hargreaves
Director, Platform Engineering
HorizonHealth

On Sun, 21 Jun 2026 at 10:12 -0700 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Logan / Marissa — heads up, HorizonHealth asking for a 75-min deep-dive covering KV cache + cross-region failover...

source.subject: On-prem tiers, billing runway and migration credit — Acme RFP
messages:
From: Tom Blake <tom.blake@acmehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com, Sara Lim <sara.lim@acmehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Ava —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attached is a short excerpt from our RFP (Acme_RFP_2026.pdf). Two asks from our procurement + infra teams:

1) We need a commercial summary that separates the one-time on-prem license (if any), the recurring platform fee (monthly/annual), and estimated per-token or per-inference metering. Our finance lead needs line items for capex vs opex justification.

2) We're particularly interested in understanding whether Redwood will consider a migration / onboarding credit to offset professional services and initial GPU churn. If you have a simple rule (e.g., X% credit up to $Y with a minimum commit), share it.

Timing: Acme's fiscal Q3 starts July 1; procurement is trying to lock a contract by 7/15 if possible.

Couple of clarifying items in the RFP: we plan to run a private VPC deployment with a phased rollout (pilot -> prod). We expect 24/7 tier-2/oncall coverage during pilot and a higher SLA for prod.

Can you send a short commercial (one page) and a suggested support-tier mapping we can circulate?

Thanks —
Tom
Tom Blake
Director, Platform Engineering | Acme Health
Attachments: Acme_RFP_2026.pdf (PDF, 842KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Blake <tom.blake@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Tom —

Appreciate the RFP excerpt and timeline. I'll pull together a concise commercial and a support-tier mapping and get that to you by EOD Friday. Quick questions while I assemble:

- Do you prefer pricing presented as (A) annual committed spend with an amortized platform fee, or (B) metered + smaller platform fee? We can show both.
- Is the pilot chargeable under the same commit as prod, or do you want a separate pilot allowance?
- Any constraint on invoice cadence (monthly vs quarterly vs annual)? You mentioned FY start — we can align billing to July if a signature happens before 7/15.

I'll loop in our solutions lead (Vivek) and commercial lead (Rachel) to propose a clean option set.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Strategic & Enterprise | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 415-555-0189
Attachments: RDW_Initial_SOW_outline.pdf (PDF, 56KB)

On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 09:12 AM Tom Blake <tom.blake@acmehealth.com> wrote:
> Ava —
> 
> Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attached is a short excerpt from our RFP (Acme_RFP_2026.pdf). Two asks from our procurement + infra teams:...
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 14:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Team —

Forwarding Acme's RFP excerpt and Tom's note. They want: a one-page commercial separating license / platform fee / metering, and a clear migration/onboarding credit rule. Target sign by 7/15 if we want to align with their FY.

Quick requirements pulled from the RFP: pilot -> prod phased rollout; 24/7 tier-2 coverage during pilot; stricter SLA for prod; likely GCP us-west region for the pilot.

Suggested asks from our side that we should confirm:
- Commit bands: $250k, $500k, $750k annual committed spend — each with tiered discounts to list price.
- Platform fee: option to amortize an annual platform fee ($X) into the monthly invoices vs. invoice as a single annual line.
- Migration credit: allow up to 25% of onboarding fees back as a credit applied to the first 3 invoices if customer commits >= $750k. Alternatively, a fixed $Y credit if they pick annual prepay.

What I need from you:
- Rachel: recommended discount bands and redlines we should avoid (e.g., no unlimited credits).
- Vivek: support-tier definitions and rough $ for managed services during pilot vs prod (PS days and runbook onboarding).
- Sean: finance/booking implications — if we amortize platform fee over 12 months vs bill upfront, what's the preferred booking and any revenue recognition flags?

Attachments: acme_rfp_excerpt.txt (text, 4KB)

Thanks —
Ava

> On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 10:05 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Tom —
> > 
> > Appreciate the RFP excerpt and timeline. I'll pull together a concise commercial...
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Folks —

Thanks for the context. Below is a pragmatic commercial structure that balances Acme's capex/opex asks and our unit economics. Numbers are rough — I can refine if you want a playbook table.

1) Commitment bands (annual committed spend):
   - Band A: $250k/year — list discounts: 10% off hosted list; platform fee $40k/year (or $3.3k/mo amortized).
   - Band B: $500k/year — list discounts: 18%; platform fee $30k/year (or $2.5k/mo amortized).
   - Band C: $750k+/year — list discounts: 25%; platform fee $20k/year (or $1.7k/mo amortized).

2) Pilot treatment:
   - Offer a 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit that does not count against the annual commit if they sign within 45 days of pilot close. This helps remove friction and keeps pilot from eating into committed capacity.

3) Migration / onboarding credit:
   - Conditional credit: up to 25% of professional services fees (capped at $120k) applied as an invoice credit over first 3 monthly invoices if customer signs Band C (>= $750k).
   - Alternative: $50k one-time onboarding credit if customer prepays annual fee.

4) Support tiers (recommended mapping):
   - Standard (included): business hours support, 48-hr SLA for severity 1
   - Premium: 24/7 monitoring + 4-hr response for sev1, dedicated CS engineer (adds $6k/mo)
   - Enterprise: 24/7 on-call with guaranteed on-site/remote runbook triage within 2 hours, uptime SLA 99.95% (adds $13k/mo + optional oncall retainer)

5) Billing / booking notes (Sean):
   - We can amortize the platform fee for invoicing purposes; Finance should be okay as long as we have a signed SOW capturing the amortization schedule. If they insist on a single upfront platform fee, expect different revenue recognition.

6) Holdbacks / redlines to avoid:
   - No unlimited credits or open-ended PS estimates. Credits must be conditional on commit or prepay.
   - Keep on-call response SLAs tied to the support tier purchase.

If that sounds good, I'll draft a short one-pager and a recommended clause for the PS credit.

-Vivek
VP, Solutions & Partnerships | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Insurance-backed penalty proposal & stepdown thresholds for SLA breaches
messages:
From: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Ava —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Heliox is framing our procurement ask around not just credits but an enforceable upside for us if SLA misses are repeated. Rather than escrow or large immediate termination rights, procurement proposed an insurance-backed construct that pays capped penalties after sustained breaches. Attached is a simple model for the mechanics (heliox-request-model.xlsx). Key asks: 

- Insurance-backed fund: insurer issues a performance bond that pays penalties up to 6% of monthly committed fees per month in the worst tier.
- Stepdown thresholds: penalties escalate after 3 consecutive weekly SLO misses (tier 1), and again after 6 consecutive misses (tier 2) with higher payout.
- Termination: right to terminate only after 3 months of tier-2 level breaches, not on a single incident.

We prefer this instead of a straight termination clause or credit-only approach. Can Redwood confirm feasibility and any regulatory or accounting concerns? Also looping in procurement for visibility.

Tom
Tom Baxter
Director, Procurement
Heliox Therapeutics

Attachments: heliox-request-model.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Tom —

Appreciate the clear framing and the model — that helps a lot. Short answer: the concept is something we can work with, but there are a few practical considerations on our side (insurance procurement path, capital exposure, and how penalties interact with Dedication vs Hosted plans).

High-level thoughts: 
- Insurance bond is an interesting alternative to escrow; it reduces the cash on our balance sheet but creates administrative overhead. We need to validate whether our insurers will underwrite this with SLA-language tied to external monitoring metrics.
- The stepdown thresholds are reasonable, but we push to define SLO measurement sources (Redwood Console vs Heliox synthetic checks) and a reproducible reconciliation window.
- Termination only after sustained Tier-2 makes sense; we would ask for a cure period and a remediation playbook before termination triggers.

I've attached an initial draft of the clauses we would expect to see (redwood-draft-terms.docx). Ben and Marissa are on copy to weigh in on commercial and legal risk. Can we set a quick 30-min sync next week to walk through the model?

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx (draft terms)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — commercial input

Ava / Marissa —

Quick notes from commercial perspective after reading Tom's model and Ava's draft: 

- Fee-at-risk sizing: Heliox's model assumes up to 6% per month in worst-case payouts. We should counter with a cap tied to committed spend (e.g., max 12% of monthly committed fee per quarter) to protect runway.
- Pricing impact: If an insurer is involved, there will be an effective premium or monitoring fee — we should ask Heliox to accept that premiums cannot be underwritten by Redwood for long-term deals; alternatively a pass-through or shared-cost model.
- Monitoring: Recommend using Redwood Console as source-of-truth for SLA failures but allow Heliox to submit supplemental synthetic evidence with defined window (48 hrs).

I'm fine with the tiered stepdown approach but want to add an automatic review meeting when Tier-1 is hit twice in a rolling 90-day window — gives us time to debug before Tier-2.

Ben
Ben Carter
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — legal comments

Team —

Legal thumbs-up in principle with the insurance-backed mechanism; a few required guardrails: 

- Clear metric definitions: "SLO misses" must be unambiguous (p99 latency > X ms over a 1-hour window OR availability < 99.5% per week). Avoid vague language that insurers won't accept.
- Indemnity carve-outs: Redwood needs to ensure penalties don't cascade with other indemnities or create double recovery scenarios.
- Audit rights: Redwood will require insurer confirmation language and a process for validating paid claims. We should insist on an explicit insurer contact and SLA for payout timing.
- Termination: recommend keeping a remediation plan and a final 30-day cure period after Tier-2 before termination is available — this aligns with our operational playbook.

I'll draft markups into the redwood-draft-terms.docx and share. Also recommend we propose a shared-cost model for the insurance premium (split 70/30 Heliox/Redwood) or ask Heliox to obtain the bond directly to avoid our balance-sheet exposure.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: Insurance-backed penalty proposal & stepdown thresholds for SLA breaches
preview:
From: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Ava —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Heliox is framing our procurement ask around not just credits but an enforceable upside for us if SLA misses are repeated. Rather than escrow or large immediate termination rights, procurement proposed an insurance-backed construct that pays capped penalties after sustained breaches. Attached is a simple model for the mechanics (heliox-request-model.xlsx). Key asks: 

- Insurance-backed fund: insurer issues a performance bond that pays penalties up to 6% of monthly committed fees per month in the worst tier.
- Stepdown thresholds: penalties escalate after 3 consecutive weekly SLO misses (tier 1), and again after 6 consecutive misses (tier 2) with higher payout.
- Termination: right to terminate only after 3 months of tier-2 level breaches, not on a single incident.

We prefer this instead of a straight termination clause or credit-only approach. Can Redwood confirm feasibility and any regulatory or accounting concerns? Also looping in procurement for visibility.

Tom
Tom Baxter
Director, Procurement
Heliox Therapeutics

Attachments: heliox-request-model.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Tom —

Appreciate the clear framing and the model — that helps a lot. Short answer: the concept is something we can work with, but there are a few practical considerations on our side (insurance procurement path, capital exposure, and how penalties interact with Dedication vs Hosted plans).

High-level thoughts: 
- Insurance bond is an interesting alternative to escrow; it reduces the cash on our balance sheet but creates administrative overhead. We need to validate whether our insurers will underwrite this with SLA-language tied to external monitoring metrics.
- The stepdown thresholds are reasonable, but we push to define SLO measurement sources (Redwood Console vs Heliox synthetic checks) and a reproducible reconciliation window.
- Termination only after sustained Tier-2 makes sense; we would ask for a cure period and a remediation playbook before termination triggers.

I've attached an initial draft of the clauses we would expect to see (redwood-draft-terms.docx). Ben and Marissa are on copy to weigh in on commercial and legal risk. Can we set a quick 30-min sync next week to walk through the model?

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx (draft terms)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — commercial input

Ava / Marissa —

Quick notes from commercial perspective after reading Tom's model and Ava's draft: 

- Fee-at-risk sizing: Heliox's model assumes up to 6% per month in worst-case payouts. We should counter with a cap tied to committed spend (e.g., max 12% of monthly committed fee per quarter) to protect runway.
- Pricing impact: If an insurer is involved, there will be an effective premium or monitoring fee — we should ask Heliox to accept that premiums cannot be underwritten by Redwood for long-term deals; alternatively a pass-through or shared-cost model.
- Monitoring: Recommend using Redwood Console as source-of-truth for SLA failures but allow Heliox to submit supplemental synthetic evidence with defined window (48 hrs).

I'm fine with the tiered stepdown approach but want to add an automatic review meeting when Tier-1 is hit twice in a rolling 90-day window — gives us time to debug before Tier-2.

Ben
Ben Carter
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — legal comments

Team —

Legal thumbs-up in principle with the insurance-backed mechanism; a few required guardrails: 

- Clear metric definitions: \"SLO misses\" must be unambiguous (p99 latency > X ms over a 1-hour window OR availability < 99.5% per week). Avoid vague language that insurers won't accept.
- Indemnity carve-outs: Redwood needs to ensure penalties don't cascade with other indemnities or create double recovery scenarios.
- Audit rights: Redwood will require insurer confirmation language and a process for validating paid claims. We should insist on an explicit insurer contact and SLA for payout timing.
- Termination: recommend keeping a remediation plan and a final 30-day cure period after Tier-2 before termination is available — this aligns with our operational playbook.

I'll draft markups into the redwood-draft-terms.docx and share. Also recommend we propose a shared-cost model for the insurance premium (split 70/30 Heliox/Redwood) or ask Heliox to obtain the bond directly to avoid our balance-sheet exposure.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

source.subject: POC: Finlytics hands-on lab & staging prep — reschedule + load-test slots
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@finlytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC kick-off & environment check — proposed hands-on lab week of 6/15

Hi Samir —

Thanks again for agreeing to run the hands-on POC with Finlytics. We want to make the session practical (prompt review, routing rules, and light load tests) so we can validate latency/costs for your typical queries. To get the most out of the session we need a couple of environment items ahead of time:

- Staging endpoint (URL) + any allowlist IPs or VPC peering info so we can test routing.
- Sandbox API key(s) scoped to the test model.
- A short prompt set (5–10 prompts) you want us to run during the session.
- Preferred time windows for hands-on lab (we're proposing 60–90 minute blocks).

I'm available Tue/Wed/Thu afternoons PST next week (6/15–6/17). If those don't work, please suggest two alternate slots and we'll accommodate.

Attaching a quick pre-checklist that shows the minimal config we need.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123
Redwood Inference

Attachment: finlytics-poc-precheck.pdf (staging checklist, API key instructions)

From: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@finlytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mia Chen <mia.chen@finlytics.com>, ops@finlytics.com
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC kick-off & environment check — proposed hands-on lab week of 6/15

Ava —

Thanks for the checklist, and this sounds great. We can provide a staging URL and a scoped API key. Ops will need the allowlist format — copying ops and Mia who owns infra.

Proposed slots:
- Tue 6/15 1:00–2:30pm PT
- Wed 6/16 10:30–11:30am PT
- Thu 6/17 2:00–3:30pm PT (best for our engineering team)

We will also send a short example prompt set by EOD Thursday so you have it. Two quick questions:
1) For the load test portion, what concurrency levels do you normally run for these POCs?
2) Can you confirm we need to provide a VPC peering tunnel or will an allowlist of 3 IPs suffice for the staging tests?

—Samir
Samir Gupta
Head of Product, Finlytics
samir.gupta@finlytics.com | +1 212-555-0199

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@finlytics.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC kick-off & environment check — proposed hands-on lab week of 6/15

Samir —

Thanks — Thu 6/17 2:00–3:30pm PT works well for our engineers too. We'll plan for a 90-minute session focused on: prompt walkthrough (20–25 mins), routing & model variant discussion (20 mins), and a short load test run (30–35 mins) with time for Q&A.

To answer your questions:
- Typical POC concurrency: we run a step-up pattern: 1, 5, 10 concurrent clients for 60s each to observe latency and token throughput. If you expect very different traffic, tell us and we can adapt.
- Allowlist: three static IPs will be fine for the staging tests; if you need persistent integration later we'll talk VPC peering.

Attached is an allowlist template (staging-allowlist-template.txt). For API keys please create a sandbox key labeled finlytics-poc-2026 and share it as a secret via your vault or reply to ops with the key.

We'll use the following agenda and will circulate a shared Drive doc with the prompt set once you send it.

Agenda (90m):
- 00:00–00:10: welcome & objectives
- 00:10–00:35: prompt review & prompt-engineering tips
- 00:35–00:55: routing, model variants, and cost/latency tradeoffs
- 00:55–01:25: light load tests (step-up concurrency) + monitoring
- 01:25–01:30: next steps / mutual action items

See you on 6/17.

Ava

Attachment: staging-allowlist-template.txt

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@finlytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC kick-off & environment check — proposed hands-on lab week of 6/15

Quick technical note —

We'll bring a lightweight load harness that runs streaming generation, so the test will exercise both prefix cache behavior and streaming latency. For the load test we will:
- Start with single client (baseline latency)
- Ramp to 5 clients (observe batching efficiency)
- Ramp to 10 clients if the staging instance can handle it (we'll watch GPU utilization)

If Finlytics wants token limits during the test, please provide max_tokens per prompt (default we'll use 256). Also: if your staging endpoint terminates TLS with an appliance, please share the CA bundle ahead of time — otherwise the harness will fail TLS validation.

-Dev
Dev Patel
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@finlytics.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Jun 2026 14:30:00 -0700
Subject: UPDATED: POC hands-on — confirmed 6/17 14:00 PT (calendar invite attached)

Samir / team —

Confirming our hands-on POC for Thu 17 Jun, 2:00–3:30pm PT. Calendar invite sent. Thanks for sharing the prompt set earlier today — we've added it to the shared Drive doc and preloaded a few sanity checks against your staging endpoint.

Action items for Finlytics before the session:
- Provide sandbox API key via ops (label: finlytics-poc-2026)
- Add these three Redwood IPs to your allowlist: 54.182.11.7, 52.24.99.12, 54.210.6.45 (or confirm VPC peering cadence)
- Share any CA bundle if TLS is terminated by a proxy

We'll run the step-up load test as discussed. If anything changes, reply to this thread and we'll reschedule.

Looking forward to it —
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Calendar: Redwood Invite attached (no binary)
Related links: https://drive.google.com/finlytics-poc-plan

source.subject: HavenRx production regressions after model v2.3 quantization
messages:
From: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:12:04 -0700
Subject: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Ava —

We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
- p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
- throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing

This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.

Please treat as high priority.

— Laura
Laura Mendez
Sr. Engineering Manager, Platform
HavenRx

Attachments: none in this message
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:20:17 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Hi Laura — thank you for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We're pulling the on-call and diagnostics right now.

Immediate next steps:
1) I've looped in our infra lead (David) and VP Eng (Marcus) — we'll triage logs and determine whether a rollback is needed.
2) Could you share the precise hit window + a sample request id? That will speed root cause.
3) If you prefer, let's jump on a 30-min call — I'm available 08:30 or 09:00 PT.

We'll prioritize the safety path (rollback to last known good) if it looks like QoS/latency regression rather than an edge case in your inputs.

— Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

On Mon, Jun 14, 2026 at 08:12 AM Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com> wrote:
> Ava —
>
> We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
> - p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
> - error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
> - throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing
>
> This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.
>
> Please treat as high priority.
>
> — Laura
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:45:33 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update (technical notes)

Folks — quick triage summary from the runtime team.

What we observed: the v2.3 model with the new quantization profile (q3->q4 mixed path) introduces a different decode kernel path that increases per-token CPU/GPU synchronization under the beam/ sampling config used by HavenRx. Under typical sequences the KV cache expands differently and memory paging between CUDA memory + host becomes more frequent at the tail latencies.

Measured from Canary -> Prod telemetry:
- baseline (v2.2, q3_profile): p99 ~420ms, error ~0.3%
- post-upgrade (v2.3, q4_mixed): p99 ~1.8s, 5xx errors to 4.8% concentrated on requests >512 tokens
- throughput per GPU decreased ~18-25% depending on batch mixing

Root cause hypothesis: the quantization selection pushed some attention kernels to a slower fallback that greatly impacts longer sequences. There are also memory fragmentation effects that cause transient OOMs->5xx.

Immediate remediation options (ranked by speed of mitigation):
A) Roll back to v2.2 (last known good) — fastest, <30m to flip and validate if we have hot spares and the model artifacts cached.
B) Adjust quantization profile to q4_light (tighter bias toward q3 path) — medium effort, ~2-4 hours to build/test and canary.
C) Apply request-side throttling / drop long-sequence sampling for the next 2 hours — quick but degrades quality for those inputs.

Attachments: p99_latency_report_20260614.csv (aggregated telemetry), error_trace_645.log (sample 5xx trace).

Recommendation: Start with rollback (A) to stabilize production, then implement B as the long-term fix with canary. I'll prepare a rollback playbook (rollback_playbook.md) and target a rollback window once we get sign-off.

— David
David Kim
Principal Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Jun 14, 2026 at 08:20 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Laura — thank you for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We're pulling the on-call and diagnostics right now.
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Leadership Distribution List (internal)
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: EXEC: HavenRx production regression — propose immediate rollback + customer credit discussion

Team — looping in leadership because this is high visibility with HavenRx (large strategic account on Dedicated capacity). Impact: multiple customer endpoints degraded, potential SLA breach.

Summary: runtime analysis indicates the new v2.3 quantization profile is the likely cause (see David's notes and attached telemetry). David recommends rollback to v2.2 as the fastest stabilization.

Ask: approval for immediate rollback and an incident call with HavenRx at 09:30 PT. Customer will likely request a credit; please advise on policy for a strategic account. Preliminary containment estimate: rollback + validation within 30–45 minutes if we have cache artifacts; full postmortem and mitigation plan within 48 hours.

Proposed customer-facing remedies:
- Immediate rollback to v2.2 on their production host
- 10% invoice credit for the impacted billing window (to be finalized by finance)
- Joint postmortem + technical walkthrough within 48 hours

I'm authorizing the runtime team to proceed with rollback when David says ready. Evelyn — please prepare CS comms; Jonas — please prepare a short product note on model rollout guardrails we can share.

— Marcus Lin
VP, Engineering — Serving
Redwood Inference
From: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 09:20:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Thanks for the quick responses, team.

Roll back to v2.2 immediately — we accept that plan. We also expect a formal postmortem and a credit for the SLA window. Please confirm when you have a public status update and dial-in for 09:30 PT call.

— Laura
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 12:15:42 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update — rollback complete & next steps

Laura / team — quick update from our side: rollback to v2.2 completed at 12:03 PT and validated across the impacted endpoints. Key notes:
- p99 returned to ~430ms in the validation window (see attached telemetry snapshot).
- 5xx errors fell back to baseline (~0.35%).
- Throughput returned to expected levels; no residual OOMs observed in the last 20 minutes.

Action items we are committing to:
1) Postmortem: full RCA + timeline document by EOD 2026-06-15 (we will share via the Drive link).
2) Credit: proposing a 10% credit for the impacted billing window; finance will confirm and follow up with formal paper.
3) Mitigation: implement the q4_light profile and canary it for 48 hours before reintroducing v2.3; product will add stronger rollout guardrails (staged traffic ramp + sequence-length profiling).
4) Follow-up call: we scheduled the 09:30 PT call earlier; I moved it to a postmortem slot at 14:00 PT today per your availability — conference link sent separately.

Again — apologies for the disruption and thank you for the quick coordination. I'll share the rollback_playbook.md and the incident doc (https://drive.google.com/incident/HAVENRX-20260614) shortly.

— Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: rollback_playbook.md, p99_latency_report_20260614.csv (validation snapshot)
