On Mon, Jun 14, 2026 at 08:12 AM Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com> wrote:
> Ava —
>
> We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
> - p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
> - error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
> - throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing
>
> This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.
>
> Please treat as high priority.
>
> — Laura


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:45:33 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update (technical notes)

Folks — quick triage summary from the runtime team.

What we observed: the v2.3 model with the new quantization profile (q3->q4 mixed path) introduces a different decode kernel path that increases per-token CPU/GPU synchronization under the beam/ sampling config used by HavenRx. Under typical sequences the KV cache expands differently and memory paging between CUDA memory + host becomes more frequent at the tail latencies.

Measured from Canary -> Prod telemetry:
- baseline (v2.2, q3_profile): p99 ~420ms, error ~0.3%
- post-upgrade (v2.3, q4_mixed): p99 ~1.8s, 5xx errors to 4.8% concentrated on requests >512 tokens
- throughput per GPU decreased ~18-25% depending on batch mixing

Root cause hypothesis: the quantization selection pushed some attention kernels to a slower fallback that greatly impacts longer sequences. There are also memory fragmentation effects that cause transient OOMs->5xx.

Immediate remediation options (ranked by speed of mitigation):
A) Roll back to v2.2 (last known good) — fastest, <30m to flip and validate if we have hot spares and the model artifacts cached.
B) Adjust quantization profile to q4_light (tighter bias toward q3 path) — medium effort, ~2-4 hours to build/test and canary.
C) Apply request-side throttling / drop long-sequence sampling for the next 2 hours — quick but degrades quality for those inputs.

Attachments: p99_latency_report_20260614.csv (aggregated telemetry), error_trace_645.log (sample 5xx trace).

Recommendation: Start with rollback (A) to stabilize production, then implement B as the long-term fix with canary. I'll prepare a rollback playbook (rollback_playbook.md) and target a rollback window once we get sign-off.

— David
David Kim
Principal Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Jun 14, 2026 at 08:20 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Laura — thank you for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We're pulling the on-call and diagnostics right now.


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Leadership Distribution List (internal)
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: EXEC: HavenRx production regression — propose immediate rollback + customer credit discussion

Team — looping in leadership because this is high visibility with HavenRx (large strategic account on Dedicated capacity). Impact: multiple customer endpoints degraded, potential SLA breach.

Summary: runtime analysis indicates the new v2.3 quantization profile is the likely cause (see David's notes and attached telemetry). David recommends rollback to v2.2 as the fastest stabilization.

Ask: approval for immediate rollback and an incident call with HavenRx at 09:30 PT. Customer will likely request a credit; please advise on policy for a strategic account. Preliminary containment estimate: rollback + validation within 30–45 minutes if we have cache artifacts; full postmortem and mitigation plan within 48 hours.

Proposed customer-facing remedies:
- Immediate rollback to v2.2 on their production host
- 10% invoice credit for the impacted billing window (to be finalized by finance)
- Joint postmortem + technical walkthrough within 48 hours

I'm authorizing the runtime team to proceed with rollback when David says ready. Evelyn — please prepare CS comms; Jonas — please prepare a short product note on model rollout guardrails we can share.

— Marcus Lin
VP, Engineering — Serving
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 09:20:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Thanks for the quick responses, team.

Roll back to v2.2 immediately — we accept that plan. We also expect a formal postmortem and a credit for the SLA window. Please confirm when you have a public status update and dial-in for 09:30 PT call.

— Laura


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 12:15:42 -0700
Subject: Re: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update — rollback complete & next steps

Laura / team — quick update from our side: rollback to v2.2 completed at 12:03 PT and validated across the impacted endpoints. Key notes:
- p99 returned to ~430ms in the validation window (see attached telemetry snapshot).
- 5xx errors fell back to baseline (~0.35%).
- Throughput returned to expected levels; no residual OOMs observed in the last 20 minutes.

Action items we are committing to:
1) Postmortem: full RCA + timeline document by EOD 2026-06-15 (we will share via the Drive link).
2) Credit: proposing a 10% credit for the impacted billing window; finance will confirm and follow up with formal paper.
3) Mitigation: implement the q4_light profile and canary it for 48 hours before reintroducing v2.3; product will add stronger rollout guardrails (staged traffic ramp + sequence-length profiling).
4) Follow-up call: we scheduled the 09:30 PT call earlier; I moved it to a postmortem slot at 14:00 PT today per your availability — conference link sent separately.

Again — apologies for the disruption and thank you for the quick coordination. I'll share the rollback_playbook.md and the incident doc (https://drive.google.com/incident/HAVENRX-20260614) shortly.

— Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: rollback_playbook.md, p99_latency_report_20260614.csv (validation snapshot)

source.subject: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, HealthLane Security <security@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Hi Jane / HealthLane team,

Thanks again for the conversation this week. Per our roadmap and the Order Form timeline, I wanted to kick off a short working thread to align on three things we need to close before legal redlines: (1) pen-test scope & timing, (2) SOC 2 / attestation delivery cadence, and (3) how evidence will be routed (ticketing + artifact naming).

From Redwood's side:
- We can accept a staging-only penetration test (no production access) and provide ephemeral creds for the window.
- SOC 2: we can provide the latest report plus a scoped list of control mappings; we normally deliver the full report on an annual cadence and can share an interim attestation in some cases.
- Evidence ticketing: we use a single JIRA board for evidence uploads (one ticket per artifact) and can create a templated ticket that maps to your evidence requests.

Attached: security_exhibit_v1.docx (draft exhibit with initial suggested language).

Asks: 
1) Do you have preferred pen-test windows (weeks in June/July)?
2) Is quarterly or annual SOC 2 delivery sufficient for procurement, or do you need something in between during onboarding?
3) Who should be the single point of contact on your side for evidence review?

If it helps we can set a 30-minute call next week to walk through the draft exhibit line-by-line.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
Ava.chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: security_exhibit_v1.docx (docx)]

From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthLane Security <security@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Hi Ava — thanks for the clear summary.

Quick answers: 
- Pen-test windows: we prefer mid-July (week of July 12) or the first two weeks of August if you need more runway. Staging-only is fine — we'll coordinate scoping details.
- SOC 2 cadence: Procurement prefers annual, but Security will ask for a short attestation during onboarding if there are any gaps; quarterly would be ideal, but not strictly required. For now, annual + onboarding attestation should cover internal approvals.
- POC: I'm the technical POC (Jane Doe) and procurement will be copied for contract steps.

Attaching our SOC 2 assertions spreadsheet to show the mapping we'll want verified.

Can your legal/controls team confirm whether you will accept a 30-day notice period for inspections and whether Redwood requires reciprocal rights? Procurement will need that for the Order Form.

Thanks,
Jane
Director, Security Operations — HealthLane
jane.doe@healthlane.com
[Attachment: HealthLane-soc2-assertions.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 08:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane — appreciate the quick reply and the assertions sheet. A few operational clarifications from engineering/security: 

- Pen-test scope: we'll scope to non-production endpoints only. We'll provide a staging environment with time-limited keys and an agreed test plan. Staging window preference: week of July 12 is workable for us.

- Inspection/notice: operationally, 30 days notice for an inspection or evidence pull is reasonable. For the MSA clause we typically avoid open-ended inspection rights (we ask for bounded windows and purpose-limited access). Attached is a short snippet with suggested pen-test + inspection language you can share with Procurement/legal.

- Evidence routing: recommend we create a single JIRA project (SEC-EVIDENCE-HealthLane) where each artifact (SOC 2 report, control screenshots, configuration exports) is uploaded to a ticket and linked back to your assertions spreadsheet. This keeps evidence immutable and auditable.

Next steps: I'll draft the pen-test clause and the JIRA template today and loop in Neha for legal redlines.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Security Operations — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: proposed_pen_test_clause_snippet.txt (txt)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane / team — looping in legal for a couple of redline points after reviewing Ben's snippet and Ava's draft exhibit. High level: we support staging-only pentest and the proposed ticketing model, but two contract items need explicit confirmation or alternate language from HealthLane procurement: 

1) Duration of inspection rights: the draft currently reads "inspection rights for the lifetime of the Agreement." We propose a bounded period tied to the Order Form term + 12 months post-termination for evidence access (to support certain compliance obligations). Please confirm if Procurement can accept that.

2) Notice for penetration testing / inspection: we propose a 30-day notice for scheduled inspections and a 72-hour emergency notification for incident-related forensic access (not for regular pentest windows).

If those two items are acceptable, we will incorporate them into the MSA redline. Also: please confirm whether HealthLane requires reciprocal audit rights into your systems — our standard position is that vendor inspection is one-way (HealthLane inspects Redwood), unless a separate forensic agreement is established.

I've attached the redline note PDF with inline comments for the exhibit. Neha will shepherd the legal changes through to the Order Form once we have confirmation on the two points above.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: msa_redline_comments.pdf (pdf)]

From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 10:52:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Laura/Ben/Ava — thanks for the detail and the proposed language. Procurement is comfortable with: 
- Bounded inspection window (Order Form term + 12 months post-termination)
- 30-day scheduled notice for planned inspections; 72-hour notification for incident triage only

Reciprocal rights: Procurement will not grant Redwood inspection rights into HealthLane systems. If forensic access is needed, we will coordinate on a case-by-case basis under an NDA and a scoped forensic agreement.

SOC 2 cadence: Procurement confirms annual delivery is sufficient; Security will request a short onboarding attestation (we can accept emailed attestation with linked JIRA tickets for artifacts during onboarding). HealthLane is open to bi-weekly evidence uploads during the first 6 weeks of integration, then monthly for the next quarter, then annual for steady-state. Can Redwood support that ramp?

We can confirm the July 12 window for the staging pentest. Procurement expects the Order Form signature in the next two weeks if legal redlines are incorporated.

Thanks — Jane
[Quoted text follows]
On Mon, Jun 8, 2026 at 09:10 AM Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Jane / team — looping in legal for a couple of redline points after reviewing Ben's snippet and Ava's draft exhibit. High level: we support staging-only pentest and the proposed ticketing model, but two contract items need explicit confirmation or alternate language from HealthLane procurement: ...

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Great — appreciate the quick confirm, Jane. Summary & next steps from Redwood: 

ACTION ITEMS:
- Ben: finalize the pen-test + inspection clause and post to the shared drive (due EOD Wed).
- Neha: incorporate Laura's redline suggestions into the MSA + Order Form (target: draft redline for Procurement review by Fri).
- Ava: create the SEC-EVIDENCE JIRA project and the ticket template; invite HealthLane security + procurement to the board (I will send invites today).
- Ops: we'll schedule the staging pentest for the week of July 12; Ben to share a test plan and staging access checklist two weeks prior.

Links: 
- Working doc: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-security-exhibit-HL
- JIRA board: https://jira.redwood.com/browse/SEC-EVIDENCE-123
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/HL-2026-043

If there are no surprises from Procurement on the signed Order Form timeline, we'll target an onboarding kickoff the week after signature and follow the ramped evidence cadence Jane outlined (bi-weekly for 6 weeks, then monthly for the quarter, then annual steady-state).

Thanks everyone — I'll set the 30-minute redline walkthrough for Thu if that works for Procurement/legal.

Best,
Ava Chen
Sr. Customer Success Manager — Redwood Inference

Can your legal/controls team confirm whether you will accept a 30-day notice period for inspections and whether Redwood requires reciprocal rights? Procurement will need that for the Order Form.

Thanks,
Jane
Director, Security Operations — HealthLane
jane.doe@healthlane.com
[Attachment: HealthLane-soc2-assertions.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 08:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane — appreciate the quick reply and the assertions sheet. A few operational clarifications from engineering/security: 

- Pen-test scope: we'll scope to non-production endpoints only. We'll provide a staging environment with time-limited keys and an agreed test plan. Staging window preference: week of July 12 is workable for us.

- Inspection/notice: operationally, 30 days notice for an inspection or evidence pull is reasonable. For the MSA clause we typically avoid open-ended inspection rights (we ask for bounded windows and purpose-limited access). Attached is a short snippet with suggested pen-test + inspection language you can share with Procurement/legal.

- Evidence routing: recommend we create a single JIRA project (SEC-EVIDENCE-HealthLane) where each artifact (SOC 2 report, control screenshots, configuration exports) is uploaded to a ticket and linked back to your assertions spreadsheet. This keeps evidence immutable and auditable.

Next steps: I'll draft the pen-test clause and the JIRA template today and loop in Neha for legal redlines.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Security Operations — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: proposed_pen_test_clause_snippet.txt (txt)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane / team — looping in legal for a couple of redline points after reviewing Ben's snippet and Ava's draft exhibit. High level: we support staging-only pentest and the proposed ticketing model, but two contract items need explicit confirmation or alternate language from HealthLane procurement: 

1) Duration of inspection rights: the draft currently reads "inspection rights for the lifetime of the Agreement." We propose a bounded period tied to the Order Form term + 12 months post-termination for evidence access (to support certain compliance obligations). Please confirm if Procurement can accept that.

2) Notice for penetration testing / inspection: we propose a 30-day notice for scheduled inspections and a 72-hour emergency notification for incident-related forensic access (not for regular pentest windows).

If those two items are acceptable, we will incorporate them into the MSA redline. Also: please confirm whether HealthLane requires reciprocal audit rights into your systems — our standard position is that vendor inspection is one-way (HealthLane inspects Redwood), unless a separate forensic agreement is established.

I've attached the redline note PDF with inline comments for the exhibit. Neha will shepherd the legal changes through to the Order Form once we have confirmation on the two points above.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: msa_redline_comments.pdf (pdf)]

From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>

Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 10:52:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

We can confirm the July 12 window for the staging pentest. Procurement expects the Order Form signature in the next two weeks if legal redlines are incorporated.

Thanks — Jane

source.subject: DevGarden developer onboarding & endpoint migration playbook
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira@devgarden.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-05T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Hi Ava / Monica,

Thanks again for the intro. Attached is our initial requirements doc (seed_integration_requirements.pdf) — it highlights our primary use-cases: chat assistants for product pages and server-side embedding indexing.

Quick list of things we want to confirm before the sandbox cutover:
- Recommended SDK initializers for backend (Python) and for our Node lambda workers
- Endpoint migration plan: sandbox -> staging -> prod (min downtime expectation)
- Retry/timeouts strategy for short-lived lambdas (15s max runtime)
- Rate limit handling: how to surface 429 vs transient throttles and how to resume safely
- Any telemetry hooks we should add to detect quality regressions after model pinning

Can we get a proposed mutual action plan and a short code snippet to unblock our engineers? We have a kickoff on Monday and would like a short pre-read.

Thanks,
Samira
Developer Relations
DevGarden Inc.

Attachment: seed_integration_requirements.pdf (PDF, 72KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@devgarden.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-05T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Hi Samira — great to meet you (virtually). Short answers and a playbook sketch below; attaching a one-page quickstart (redwood_sdk_quickstart.md) that your engs can use right away.

High level plan (3 sprints, estimate):
1) Sprint 0 (3 days): Sandbox SDK install + basic smoke tests against the sandbox inference endpoint.
2) Sprint 1 (1 week): Endpoint migration testing (sandbox -> staging), retries & telemetry instrumented, run load tests.
3) Sprint 2 (1 week): Production cutover + monitor and rollback guardrails.

Sandbox endpoints and migration notes:
- Sandbox: api.sandbox.redwood.ai (no billing; limited throughput).
- Staging: api.staging.redwood.ai (closer to prod perf).
- Prod: api.redwood.ai (final routing; pin model versions).
Migration approach: always validate on staging with the same model pin and request shape used in prod. Use canary rollout (5% traffic) for first 24–48h then increase.

Retries/timeouts guidance for short-lived lambdas:
- Client-side timeout: set request timeout to 10s for single-request lambdas (reserve 5s for function processing).
- Retries: use idempotent retry logic for safe calls (embeddings, reranks). For potentially non-idempotent generation calls, prefer server-side fallbacks.
- Backoff: 3 retries with exponential backoff (base=250ms, factor=2, jitter=0.25). Don't exceed overall lambda budget.

Rate-limiting best practices:
- Surface 429 separately from 5xx. 429 implies client throttling; implement token-bucket locally and pause non-critical jobs.
- Use windowed counters and request batching for embeddings to reduce unit cost.
- If you receive 429s during a rollout, automatically shift to a lower-capacity compatible model variant (we can map variants).

Quickcode: Python SDK init + call (minimal)

from redwood import RedwoodClient
client = RedwoodClient(api_key="REPLACE_ME", endpoint="https://api.sandbox.redwood.ai")
resp = client.generate(model="redwood-2-small", prompt="Summarize the product page in two bullets", timeout=10)
print(resp.text)

Node example (fetch + timeout)

const { RedwoodClient } = require('@redwoodai/sdk')
const client = new RedwoodClient({ apiKey: process.env.RW_KEY, endpoint: 'https://api.sandbox.redwood.ai' })
const resp = await client.generate({ model: 'redwood-2-small', prompt: 'Quick TL;DR', timeout: 10000 })
console.log(resp.text)

Telemetry hooks to add:
- Request id (x-rw-request-id) propagation
- Latency and token counts per request
- Quality checks: sample outputs stored for periodic evals (we can provide eval harness)

Next steps: I attached the quickstart and a draft mutual action plan (MAP-DevGarden-v1.xlsx) — Monica, can you take the MAP and create the initial milestones? Vivek, assigning you to confirm recommended backoff knobs on the runtime.

Best,
Ava Chen
Customer Success, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_sdk_quickstart.md (Markdown, 6KB)

From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: DevGarden Engineering <eng@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Thanks Ava — couple follow-ups from the eng team:
- How does Redwood's KV cache interact when we do streaming responses? Is the KV cache safe to reuse across short-lived lambda containers?
- For embedding batching: what's the recommended max batch size and how do we handle partial failures?

Forwarding this to your runtime SME — Vivek, could you advise?

—Tom

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

(see above)

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-06T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Quick runtime answers for Tom and Samira:
- KV cache & streaming: KV caches are tied to a serving instance; short-lived lambdas should treat KV as ephemeral. For stable caching across requests, use our prefix caching API (client-side) which stores validated prefixes in a small Redis-backed cache we manage for dedicated customers, or persist locally for repeated warm paths.

- Streaming + cache safety: Streaming doesn't change KV semantics, but avoid reusing partial stream state across distinct user sessions. Always initialize per-session state and rely on prefix caching only for identical prefix queries.

- Embedding batching guidance:
  * Recommended max batch size: 128 vectors per request (depends on vector dim; tune if large).
  * Partial failures: our API returns a per-item status for batch calls. Best practice: retry only failed items with a capped exponential backoff and merge results in-order to preserve indexing consistency.

- Concrete backoff knobs (example pseudo):
  retries = 3
  base_ms = 250
  for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
      delay = base_ms * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) * (1 + random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25))
      sleep(delay / 1000)

- Node/HTTP client settings: set socket timeout to 12s and a per-request header X-RW-Request-ID. For lambdas with 15s budget, we recommend synchronous calls only for embeddings and async streaming for long generations (or offload generation to a job queue).

I'll add these as comments in the MAP. Happy to pair with your engs for a live debug session.

—Vivek
Senior Runtime Engineer
Redwood Inference

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@devgarden.io>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-07T09:30:00-07:00
Subject: Draft Mutual Action Plan: DevGarden integration (v1)

Samira — attaching the MAP spreadsheet (MAP-DevGarden-v1.xlsx). High-level milestones:
- M0: Sandbox smoke + SDK integration (owner: DevGarden Eng) — target: 2026-06-09
- M1: Staging migration & throughput tests (owner: Redwood Eng + DevGarden Eng) — target: 2026-06-14
- M2: Canary prod cutover (owner: DevGarden Eng) — target: 2026-06-18
- M3: Full production + post-cutover review (owner: CS Ops) — target: 2026-06-22

Action items:
1) DevGarden: implement SDK quickstart (redwood_sdk_quickstart.md) and run smoke tests.
2) Redwood (Vivek): confirm embedding batch size & partial-failure flow documented in MAP.
3) CS (Ava/Monica): schedule cutover call (30m) and share invitation.

Link to HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/DG-2026-09

Please confirm owners and I will convert into tasks in our tracker.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Implementation Manager, Redwood Inference

Attachment: MAP-DevGarden-v1.xlsx (Excel, 24KB)

From: Samira Khan <samira@devgarden.io>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-08T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Draft Mutual Action Plan: DevGarden integration (v1)

Monica / Ava — thanks, the MAP looks good. Owners confirmed on our side. I've scheduled the 30m cutover planning call for 2026-06-09 10:00am PT and sent a calendar invite. We'll run the sandbox tests tomorrow and share results in the shared Google Drive (link in calendar).

We have one ask: please include a short runbook snippet in the MAP for emergency rollback (how we’ll pin traffic back and trigger fallback model). If you can add that before tomorrow's call it'd be ideal.

Appreciate the quick responses — looking forward to the call.

Best,
Samira
DevGarden Inc.

- Sandbox: api.sandbox.redwood.ai (no billing; limited throughput).
- Staging: api.staging.redwood.ai (closer to prod perf).
- Prod: api.redwood.ai (final routing; pin model versions).
Migration approach: always validate on staging with the same model pin and request shape used in prod. Use canary rollout (5% traffic) for first 24–48h then increase.

Retries/timeouts guidance for short-lived lambdas:
- Client-side timeout: set request timeout to 10s for single-request lambdas (reserve 5s for function processing).
- Retries: use idempotent retry logic for safe calls (embeddings, reranks). For potentially non-idempotent generation calls, prefer server-side fallbacks.
- Backoff: 3 retries with exponential backoff (base=250ms, factor=2, jitter=0.25). Don't exceed overall lambda budget.

Rate-limiting best practices:
- Surface 429 separately from 5xx. 429 implies client throttling; implement token-bucket locally and pause non-critical jobs.
- Use windowed counters and request batching for embeddings to reduce unit cost.
- If you receive 429s during a rollout, automatically shift to a lower-capacity compatible model variant (we can map variants).

Quickcode: Python SDK init + call (minimal)

from redwood import RedwoodClient
client = RedwoodClient(api_key="REPLACE_ME", endpoint="https://api.sandbox.redwood.ai")
resp = client.generate(model="redwood-2-small", prompt="Summarize the product page in two bullets", timeout=10)
print(resp.text)

Node example (fetch + timeout)

const { RedwoodClient } = require('@redwoodai/sdk')
const client = new RedwoodClient({ apiKey: process.env.RW_KEY, endpoint: 'https://api.sandbox.redwood.ai' })
const resp = await client.generate({ model: 'redwood-2-small', prompt: 'Quick TL;DR', timeout: 10000 })
console.log(resp.text)

Telemetry hooks to add:
- Request id (x-rw-request-id) propagation
- Latency and token counts per request
- Quality checks: sample outputs stored for periodic evals (we can provide eval harness)

Next steps: I attached the quickstart and a draft mutual action plan (MAP-DevGarden-v1.xlsx) — Monica, can you take the MAP and create the initial milestones? Vivek, assigning you to confirm recommended backoff knobs on the runtime.

Best,
Ava Chen
Customer Success, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_sdk_quickstart.md (Markdown, 6KB)

From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>

Cc: DevGarden Engineering <eng@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Thanks Ava — couple follow-ups from the eng team:
- How does Redwood's KV cache interact when we do streaming responses? Is the KV cache safe to reuse across short-lived lambda containers?
- For embedding batching: what's the recommended max batch size and how do we handle partial failures?

Forwarding this to your runtime SME — Vivek, could you advise?

—Tom

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@devgarden.io>
Date: 2026-06-06T08:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

(see above)


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-06T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DevGarden integration kickoff: initial requirements and questions

Quick runtime answers for Tom and Samira:
- KV cache & streaming: KV caches are tied to a serving instance; short-lived lambdas should treat KV as ephemeral. For stable caching across requests, use our prefix caching API (client-side) which stores validated prefixes in a small Redis-backed cache we manage for dedicated customers, or persist locally for repeated warm paths.

- Streaming + cache safety: Streaming doesn't change KV semantics, but avoid reusing partial stream state across distinct user sessions. Always initialize per-session state and rely on prefix caching only for identical prefix queries.

- Embedding batching guidance:
  * Recommended max batch size: 128 vectors per request (depends on vector dim; tune if large).
  * Partial failures: our API returns a per-item status for batch calls. Best practice: retry only failed items with a capped exponential backoff and merge results in-order to preserve indexing consistency.

- Concrete backoff knobs (example pseudo):
  retries = 3
  base_ms = 250
  for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
      delay = base_ms * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) * (1 + random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25))
      sleep(delay / 1000)

- Node/HTTP client settings: set socket timeout to 12s and a per-request header X-RW-Request-ID. For lambdas with 15s budget, we recommend synchronous calls only for embeddings and async streaming for long generations (or offload generation to a job queue).

I'll add these as comments in the MAP. Happy to pair with your engs for a live debug session.

—Vivek
Senior Runtime Engineer
Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: Devon Ortiz <devon.ortiz@opti-retail.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation onramp — sample assets and MAP request

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the latency numbers looked promising and the team is excited to kick off a short evaluation. Before we schedule the technical onboarding, could you send over a short mutual action plan (MAP) and an example prompt pack we can run in our sandbox?

Scope: a 2-week onramp to validate answer quality on product search and reranking flows. We have light PII in a small subset of the corpus and will need clear usage constraints and retention guarantees.

As next steps, can you:
- Share a short MAP with milestones and owners
- Provide a sample prompt pack (few dozen seed prompts + expected acceptance criteria) under limited-use terms
- Confirm whether Redwood can host the evaluation dataset (ephemeral access) or if we need to host it and grant API access

We'd like to get started week of May 25, so a draft MAP by EOD Friday would help.

Thanks,
Maya
Head of Data Science, OptiRetail

---

Attachment: optiretail_request_spec.pdf (pdf) — short scope doc
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 12:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation onramp — sample assets and MAP request

Hi Maya — great to hear! Attaching a concise MAP and a small sample prompt pack we typically use for product-search validations. High level answers to your questions below, and the MAP has the milestone owners mapped to our team.

Quick answers:
- MAP: attached ("redwood_map_onramp_v1.pdf") — 6 checkpoints across planning, data intake, model tuning, QA, acceptance, and teardown.
- Prompt pack: attached as a compressed tarball with README and acceptance criteria ("optiretail_sample_promptpack_v1.tar.gz").
- Hosting options: Redwood can host evaluation datasets ephemeral in a sandbox we control (recommended for speed) or OptiRetail can host and provide named API keys. If you have regulatory constraints, we'll default to OptiRetail-hosted and we can run within your VPC.

Usage constraints we enforce in both modes:
- Ephemeral evaluation data is retained for a maximum of 21 days unless you request early teardown.
- No sharing outside of the named evaluation project team; we require a one-page limited-use addendum (we've attached a template).
- No commercial use beyond the evaluation scope; derivative assets must be destroyed after the onramp if not licensed.

Next steps:
1) Review the attached MAP and prompt pack.
2) Confirm hosting preference (Redwood sandbox vs. OptiRetail-hosted).
3) Sign the limited-use addendum or propose edits.

If it's helpful, Karthik can join a short 30-min intake call to finalize the timeline and access method.

Thanks — attaching both files now.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0191

Attachments: redwood_map_onramp_v1.pdf (pdf), optiretail_sample_promptpack_v1.tar.gz (tgz)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation onramp — sample assets and MAP request

Hi Maya — looping in a couple of details from the infra/ops side so you have a clear operational plan.

Access & security notes:
- If Redwood hosts the dataset: we provide ephemeral S3 storage scoped to a single evaluation job, IAM keys rotate daily, and we surface an audit log for every inference call (token-level request/response metadata). Retention: automatically destroyed 21 days post-teardown.
- If OptiRetail hosts: we'll request a named service account with IP allowlist + short-lived keys; we will not persist raw payloads in Redwood unless explicitly authorized.

Prompt pack & acceptance:
- We recommend starting with the provided seed prompts and a small set of golden transcripts (we included 30 in the pack). Acceptance criteria are per-prompt F1/accuracy thresholds and a human-review pass rate (details in README).

Operational MAP highlights (new):
- Day 0: Kickoff call + access provisioning (owners: Ava / Maya)
- Day 2–5: Data ingestion and baseline run (owner: Redwood infra / OptiRetail engineering based on hosting)
- Day 6–10: Tuning and prompt iterations (owners: solutions + OptiRetail DS)
- Day 11–14: Acceptance sprint and final QA signoff (owner: OptiRetail Product).

Open items for your procurement/security teams:
1) Limited-use addendum (one-pager) — we'll send template for review.
2) SOC2 evidence: we can provide SOC2 Type II summary and an audit-friendly data flow diagram.
3) If you require an addendum for PII handling, please share the specific retention policy and we can reflect it in the MAP.

Could you confirm who on your side will sign the limited-use addendum and whether you prefer Redwood-hosted ephemeral storage? If you want us to host, we'll provision a sandbox by Friday.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Platform Reliability | Redwood Inference

---

> Hi Maya — great to hear! Attaching a concise MAP and a small sample prompt pack we typically use for product-search validations.
> 
> (See attachments)
From: Maya Patel <maya@optiretail.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Devon Ortiz <devon.ortiz@opti-retail.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2026 11:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evaluation onramp — dataset exchange & limited-use prompt pack

Hi Ava, Karthik — thanks for the clear MAP and the prompt pack. Devon and I reviewed the limited-use addendum and have two small asks:

1) Retention: can we shorten the Redwood-hosted retention to 14 days instead of 21? Our compliance team prefers a tighter window for eval data containing customer emails.
2) Audit trail: we'd like a CSV export of inference call metadata (timestamp, request_id, route, response_status) at the end of the trial for our records.

If those two items are acceptable, Devon will sign the one-pager and we can proceed with Redwood hosting. We'd like to lock kickoff for Monday, June 1 at 10am PT.

Acceptable to proceed?

Thanks,
Maya

source.subject: Redwood demo: organizer swap to preserve external join tokens
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 23 May 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Hi Ava,

Thanks for scheduling. I accepted the invite but a bunch of folks in our org report the join button is greyed out or shows an expired token when launched from our shared customer calendar (FinOps Customer Shared). Our SSO strips external meeting tokens when events are created by a calendar resource.

Can you resend from a Redwood user account (so the organizer is a person, not the shared calendar) and include a Meet link with PST dial-in and conference ID? Procurement needs the dial-in in the event body (some clients don't surface attachments). If Zoom is created, we need the organizer to be an approved external partner, which I don't think you are on file for.

I'm copying procurement. We want to keep Monday 09:00 PT if possible.

Thanks,
Samira Khan
Senior IT Ops Manager, FinOps.io
Attachment: finops-demo-invite.ics (calendar stub)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 23 May 2026 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Hi Samira 14 thanks for the note and the context.

That matches a few support cases we've seen. Plan:
- I'll recreate the event from my personal Redwood account so the organizer is an internal user (avoids token stripping).
- Include both a Google Meet URL (per your procurement preference) and a Redwood-hosted Zoom link as a fallback.
- Paste PST dial-in + conference ID into the event body (and the email body).
- Attach a fresh .ics and the demo agenda.

If procurement insists on Meet-only, I'll remove Zoom and keep Meet as the sole URL. Which do you prefer?

I'll resend tomorrow morning so it propagates before Monday.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Attachment: redwood-demo-templates.zip (agenda + .ics templates)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: infra-scheduling@redwood.com
Date: Sun, 24 May 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: FinOps shared calendar token strip (context)

Forwarding for awareness: FinOps shared calendar strips external join tokens when the organizer is a calendar resource. Quick playbook:
- Recreate events from the salesperson's user account and invite the shared calendar as an attendee. That preserves external join tokens.
- If customer policy requires Meet-only, ensure event is created by an internal user who has permission to create external Meet links.
- Always paste dial-in + conference ID in the event body (some mail clients or calendar syncs drop attachments).

I'll have Ava resend with these constraints applied.

---Original message quoted---
> From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
> To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
> Date: Sat, 23 May 2026 15:02:00 -0700
> Subject: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Attachment: internal-note.txt (brief playbook)

From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finops.io
Date: Sun, 24 May 2026 11:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Ava 14 a couple of procurement constraints to confirm before you resend:
- Prefer Meet links for external demos for audit logging; Zoom allowed only if organizer is an approved external partner.
- Dial-in and conference ID must be pasted in the event body (attachments not reliable).
- Do not create the event from FinOps Customer Shared; create from your internal user and invite that shared calendar as an attendee if you want it on the shared calendar.

Procurement also wants the demo recording uploaded to a shared Drive after the session; I can provide the folder if needed.

Samira
Attachment: procurement-rules.pdf (summary)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@finops.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, procurement@finops.io
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 09:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo invite: join button greyed out

Hi Samira 14 invite resent from my account. Details pasted below and attached. Please confirm receipt.

Event: Redwood platform demo for FinOps.io
When: Mon, 09:00 PT
Organizer: Ava Chen (ava.chen@redwood.com)
Google Meet: https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij
Dial-in (US PST): +1 669-555-0110, PIN: 4321#
Zoom (fallback): https://redwood.zoom.us/j/123456789 (recording enabled)
Agenda: quick intro, demo (30 min), Q&A (15 min)
I've attached redwood-demo.ics and demo-agenda.pdf. I'll upload the recording to the Drive folder after the session.

Thanks,
Ava

source.subject: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>, Maria Gonzales <maria.gonzales@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin / Maria — great talking yesterday. Per our discussion, attached is a proposed MAP (nova_map_v1.xlsx) that maps the testcase subsets we can share, acceptance gates, and the close timeline for the evaluation exchange. High level:

- Redwood provides an ephemeral subset of our testcases (testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz) for Nova to run a single closed-run benchmark in your staging environment.
- Transfer is gated on: an executed MPA clause (see sheet 'Gates'), acknowledgement of sandbox retention rules, and acceptance of the usage constraints PDF (redwood_usage-constraints-202605.pdf).
- Target close: dataset transfer complete and benchmark started by 2026-05-25.

Please review the MAP and the usage constraints. Two asks: (1) confirm the acceptance gate owners on your side, (2) share preferred S3 user principal for transfer (we'll initiate a pre-signed S3 copy with time-limited access).

Attachments: nova_map_v1.xlsx (MAP), testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz (sample pack), redwood_usage-constraints-202605.pdf (legal/ops constraints)

Thanks — Ava
Ava Chen
Enterprise Solutions – Redwood Inference

From: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Gonzales <maria.gonzales@nova-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Ava — thanks, the MAP looks sensible. Quick questions before we loop legal/ops:  

1) The MAP notes a 30-day retention in our staging environment — can you confirm that restriction applies to derivative models too?
2) For the benchmark run, can we provision a single-run license that allows us to log outputs for internal analysis (no downstream distribution)?

If both items are clear, we can route the MAP into our procurement + security queue and aim for the 05/25 kickoff. S3 principal: integrations@nova-analytics.com (we can provide an IAM principal ARN if needed).

Kevin

On a separate note: can you confirm whether Redwood wants CNF redlines versus a short form MPA?

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Thanks Kevin — good questions. Looping in Rohan (ops) and Monica (legal) to confirm exact language. Quick answers from product/sales perspective: 

- Retention: our standard MAP for ephemeral evals is 30 days for both raw testcases and any model outputs produced during the benchmark. Derivatives (fine-tuned checkpoints) are explicitly prohibited under the usage constraints — the PDF contains the clause: 'No checkpointing, fine-tuning, or external redistribution of outputs.'
- Single-run logging: allowed for internal analysis only; logs must be stored in your sandbox that meets our ephemeral retention covenant (30 days) and cannot be shared externally.

We'll accept either: (A) a short-form MPA that references Redwood's usage constraints PDF and your internal PO, or (B) CNF redlines if procurement requires it. Monica / Rohan — can you confirm which approach is faster from legal/ops? Also Kevin — please send the IAM principal ARN when ready so we can pre-configure the transfer bucket policy.

Ava

From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin / Ava — quick legal read and redlines attached (see summary below). I kept this intentionally short to avoid delaying the transfer: 

- Short-form MPA is adequate provided Nova signs a one-paragraph acceptance that references Redwood's usage constraints PDF (we'll add signature block to the MAP).
- Add explicit clause: 'No derivative training or checkpoint storage; any model outputs stored for analysis must be deleted at day 30.'
- For auditability, we request a single post-benchmark attestation from Nova confirming deletion and a CSV manifest of buckets accessed.

If you want the CNF strikes, we can do that, but it will add 2–3 business days. I recommend short-form to hit the 05/25 target.

Attachments: redwood_usage-constraints-202605.pdf (same as provided), MAP_signable_v1.xlsx (MAP with signature block)

—Monica
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Gonzales <maria.gonzales@nova-analytics.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:16:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Monica — thanks, that approach works for us. We can sign the short-form MPA and provide the post-benchmark attestation. Confirming details:

- IAM principal ARN: arn:aws:iam::987654321012:role/nova-integration-sandbox
- We accept the 30-day deletion window and the prohibition on checkpointing.
- We'll schedule one benchmark run the morning of 2026-05-25 (start window 09:00–12:00 ET).

We'll sign MAP_signable_v1.xlsx and return the DocuSign link by EOD today. Please advise a target time when Redwood will enable the pre-signed copy.

Thanks — Kevin

From: Rohan Mehta <rohan_mehta@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2026 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin — thanks, ARN received and all set. We'll: 

1) Stand up the pre-signed S3 transfer by 2026-05-24 18:00 PT with a 48-hour access window.
2) Seed the bucket with testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz and a transfer manifest.
3) Watch for the signed MAP (DocuSign); we'll only enable the bucket when the signature completes.
4) Post-benchmark, we'll provide a short attestation template you can fill and return.

Closing note: target timeline remains benchmark start 2026-05-25; internal ops will monitor and disable access as soon as attestation is received or after 30 days per the MAP.

—Rohan
Infrastructure Ops, Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Kevin Li <kevin.li@nova-analytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2026 14:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Testcase accreditation & limited-use transfer — MAP and timeline

Kevin — thanks, ARN received and all set. We'll: 

1) Stand up the pre-signed S3 transfer by 2026-05-24 18:00 PT with a 48-hour access window.
2) Seed the bucket with testcase_subset_202605.tar.gz and a transfer manifest.
3) Watch for the signed MAP (DocuSign); we'll only enable the bucket when the signature completes.
4) Post-benchmark, we'll provide a short attestation template you can fill and return.

Closing note: target timeline remains benchmark start 2026-05-25; internal ops will monitor and disable access as soon as attestation is received or after 30 days per the MAP.

source.subject: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hi Priya and team,

We're finalizing the configuration for the Acme Corp webinar on May 25 and I wanted to align on two things before we lock the rehearsal schedule: registration field mapping (ON24 -> HubSpot & Zoom) and the streaming fallback plan if the ON24 encoder hits bitrate limits.

Quick ask: can you confirm the canonical field names we should surface in the ON24 registration form so they map cleanly into HubSpot? We need: full name, email, company, job title, region, and a consent checkbox for marketing. Also, can ON24 pass a persistent registrant_id that we can use as a foreign key in HubSpot?

For streaming: our preference is to stream primarily through ON24 (primary) with Zoom webinar as an overflow/backfill stream. If ON24 encounters encoder bitrate spikes we want an automated fallback to a Zoom RTMP ingest (and record on Zoom). What capabilities does ON24 expose for automatic fallback, and do we need a separate RTMP endpoint from Zoom for this to work?

I've attached the ON24 production checklist and a proposed registration field list.

Thanks — planning to schedule the full tech rehearsal for Friday 5/22.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Events Program Manager, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
Redwood Inference

Attachments: on24-prod-checklist.pdf
From: Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 12:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hi Ava — thanks for the detailed note. Short answers below, followed by a recommended config.

1) Registration mapping: ON24 supports custom field API keys and will pass those back in the registration webhook payload. Use these keys: registrant_full_name, registrant_email, registrant_company, registrant_title, registrant_region, marketing_consent. We can also attach a persistent registrant_id in the webhook for matching to HubSpot. We'll send sample webhook payloads after this thread.

2) Fallback streaming: ON24 does not provide an automatic, seamless fallback to a third-party RTMP by default, but we can implement a heartbeat + script-based failover on our encoder layer. Practically that means provision a warm Zoom RTMP ingest endpoint and a small lambda (or ON24 event script) to trigger RTMP redirect when bitrate drops below threshold for X seconds. Yes, Zoom can accept RTMP and record on the Zoom side.

Recommended items (short):
- Provide Zoom RTMP endpoint and backup stream key.
- Agree on bitrate threshold (we suggest 2.5 Mbps for 720p slides + speaker audio).
- Confirm where recordings will live (ON24 archive vs Zoom cloud).
- Confirm webhook endpoint for registration POSTs and sample payload.

I'll attach a short checklist and sample webhook. Priya
ON24 CSM
Priya Shah
priya@on24.com

Attachments: on24-production-checklist.pdf
From: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 08:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Thanks Priya — a couple clarifying Qs from ops:
- For the registrant_id, can ON24 guarantee uniqueness across events (we sometimes have multi-event attendees)? We need a stable id per person, not per registration. If not, we'll need to dedupe on email in HubSpot.
- When the webhook fires, what headers should our endpoint expect for verification (HMAC, shared secret)?

Also: Laura, can you confirm whether we want the Zoom recording to be the canonical recording for Acme (they asked for a single hosted MP4), or should we use the ON24 archive as the source and stitch if needed?

On tests: Zoom support offered an additional stream test window on 5/21 at 10:00 PT — can we reserve that in case we need to test the RTMP switch?

—Tessa
From: Zoom Support <support@zoom.us>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 May 2026 14:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Hello Tessa and Ava,

We can provide a dedicated RTMP ingest and a spare stream key for testing. For verification we recommend the following test slots: Thu 5/21 10:00 PT (reserved), Fri 5/22 09:00 PT (pre-rehearsal smoke), and Mon 5/25 08:00 PT (final). Please confirm which ones you'd like to hold.

Note on recording: Zoom cloud recording will produce a single MP4 and is exportable via API. If you want Zoom to be canonical, ensure the ON24 fallback triggers the RTMP to Zoom before ON24 disables recording.

Best,
Zoom Support Team
support@zoom.us
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Quick update: I confirmed with DevOps that we can host the webhook endpoint and will implement HMAC verification using a shared secret. We can also persist registrant records in our DB and expose a registrant_id that is stable across events (we'll dedupe by email on ingest and store a uuid).

Action items I can own:
- Provide Zoom RTMP endpoint & spare key to Priya by EOD 5/18.
- Deploy webhook endpoint + HMAC secret by 5/20 and share secret via secure channel.
- Run the RTMP failover test with Zoom on 5/21 10:00 PT.

I'll attach the preliminary stream test results from our internal smoke run.

—Dylan
Attachments: zoom-stream-test-results.csv
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya@on24.com>, Zoom Support <support@zoom.us>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Jenna Ortiz <jenna.ortiz@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Joint ON24+Zoom webinar: registration mapping & stream fallback plan

Thanks all — summary and confirmed schedule: 

1) Registration: ON24 fields set to the keys Priya supplied. Dylan will persist a registrant_id deduped by email and post that to our HubSpot record. HubSpot link: https://hubspot.com/contacts/Acme-7890
2) Fallback: Dylan to provide Zoom RTMP endpoint + key by EOD 5/18. ON24 and our encoder script will trigger RTMP failover at 2.5 Mbps sustained drop for 8 seconds.
3) Recordings: Confirmed Acme wants the single MP4 from Zoom as canonical. Priya and Zoom: please keep Zoom recording retention for 30 days and provide share link post-event.
4) Tests & rehearsals: RTMP failover test confirmed for 5/21 10:00 PT. Full tech rehearsal on 5/22 09:00 PT. Final smoke 5/25 08:00 PT.

ACTIONS: Dylan (RTMP key), Dylan (webhook + secret), Priya (webhook sample payload), Zoom (hold test slot 5/21 10:00 PT), Laura (attendee comms copy for registration email).

If no objections, I'll send the event calendar invites and the pre-rehearsal checklist to Acme by EOD.

—Ava

source.subject: Warm signup — a few quick fit questions before we lock a demo slot
messages: From: Ava Chen To: Sofia Garcia Cc: Ben Carter Date: 2026-05-14T09:12:00-07:00 Subject: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Hi Sofia — thanks for signing up and trying Redwood! I pulled your account and saw some early activity — would love to do a brief call to make sure a demo is a fit and to surface the right pilot advice. Before we schedule, two quick areas to help us tailor the session: 1) Primary use case: what are you most looking to build? (examples: live customer support summarization, search/embeddings, content generation for marketing, internal knowledge base) 2) Traffic: rough QPS or daily requests today and target after 3 months (ballpark is fine) 3) Timeline & priority: are you looking for a pilot in 2 weeks / 1 month / 2+ months? If you can send 2–3 short bullets on those, I’ll propose three demo slots that work for our CS/solutions folks. I can also include Ben (SDR) or Monica (Customer Success) depending on who you want on the call. Example slots (PT): Tue 5/18 10:00–10:30, Wed 5/19 14:00–14:30, Thu 5/20 09:00–09:30 — happy to adjust to your timezone. Thanks — Ava Ava Chen Account Executive, Redwood Inference redwood.com Attachments: none in this message From: Sofia Garcia To: Ava Chen Date: 2026-05-14T10:03:00-07:00 Subject: Re: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Hi Ava — thanks for reaching out. Happy to answer quickly: 1) Use case: customer support triage + short-response generation and root-cause extraction. We want to triage incoming tickets and produce short summaries to push to agents and also generate suggested replies for first-touch. 2) Traffic: currently ~40k incoming messages/month (~1.3k/day) but we expect to ramp quickly once we integrate. Goal is ~120k/day within 3 months for peak season (we know this is aggressive; looking for guidance on batching and cost options). 3) Timeline: we’d like a pilot in ~4 weeks if possible (team bandwidth is limited in the next 2 weeks). Preferences / notes: we are fine starting on hosted, but we’ll likely need a dedicated/private option later due to data retention policies. Our team is PST (based in San Diego). Please include any pre-demo info we should prepare. —Sofia Sofia Garcia Head of AI, LeanRetail sofia@leanretail.io From: Ava Chen To: Sofia Garcia Cc: Ben Carter Date: 2026-05-14T10:28:00-07:00 Subject: Re: Quick fit questions after your self-signup Thanks Sofia — super helpful. Based on that traffic profile and timeline we’ll route you to our solutions CS to make the pilot concrete (Monica handles pilot scoping; copying her on the next message). A few quick notes: - For 120k/day you’ll want to consider prefix caching and batching; we’ll share a cost/throughput sketch on the call. - Hosted is fine to start; Dedicated/Private is an option when you need retention/isolated capacity. Can you confirm whether your data retention requirements are strict (e.g., prohibit persistence outside your VPC) or flexible for the pilot? Also, can you attach any traffic estimate or representative sample (CSV/Excel) — that helps our sizing. I’m attaching a short traffic template (traffic_estimates.xlsx) you can fill; if you prefer a call first we can lock a slot. Proposed slots (PT) — pick one, or tell me a time that works for you: • Tue 5/18 10:00–10:30 PT • Wed 5/19 14:00–14:30 PT • Thu 5/20 09:00–09:30 PT If none of those work, I can propose morning windows next week. source.subject: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Hi Samir / Elena —

Thanks again for a constructive discovery call today. Quick recap and the concrete asks we agreed to: actionable items first, then the assumptions we used so the econ model matches your expectations.

Action items (owner / ETA)
- Nimbus: confirm expected monthly token volume ranges and peak qps window (Samir / Tue 5/19)
- Redwood: provide a draft PoA (pricing + POC config) and batch/concurrency knobs for your workload (Ava / Mon 5/18) — attached draft proposal + token profile CSV
- Both: schedule a 30-min tech sync to validate KV/prefix reuse and latency SLOs (tentative Wed 5/20)

What we heard from your team during the call
- Core workload: conversational triage + summarization for clinical intake (short prompts, average response ~180 tokens).
- Expected steady-state traffic: 50–120 requests/sec (rps) with strong diurnal trough/peak (peak ~10:00–14:00 PT).
- Sample request composition: 70% single-turn generation (no long history), 30% multi-turn with small context reuse (~3 turns, ~350 token prefix average).
- Target unit-econ: $300–$500 per 1M tokens (goal is to land at or below $400/1M for production).

Assumptions I used for the draft pricing and POC config (please flag anything off):
- Average generated tokens per request: 180 tokens; average prompt tokens: 50 tokens => ~230 tokens total per request.
- Baseline qps at steady-state: 75 rps (avg), peak concurrency target 180 concurrent requests (short tail).
- Continuous batching: expect ~15–25ms batching windows on inference nodes with prefix reuse/KV cache hits for the 30% multi-turn.
- Typical batch sizes: 8–32 depending on instance and sequence length (we'll tune during POC).
- Latency SLO: p95 generation < 300ms.
- Month estimate: 50M–200M tokens (varies with pilot scale).

Because your target unit-econ is sub-$500/1M, we recommended the Dedicated pool (reserved capacity) with a mixing policy: primary dedicated for steady traffic + burst to shared hosted for spikes. That gets predictable cost while allowing you to meet the p95 latency target.

Please confirm the monthly token low/mid/high bands and the current procurement timeline so we can finalize the PoA.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0124

Attachment stubs: poa-proposal-draft.pdf (pricing + config), nimbus_poc_token_profile.csv (sample token histogram)
From: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 17:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Thanks Ava — useful recap. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Token volumes
- Pilot (month 1): ~12–18M tokens (we'll be routing ~10k–14k requests/day).
- Ramp (months 2–4): 40–75M tokens/month as usage spreads across clinics.
- Production target (6–12 months): 150–220M tokens/month if we onboard 60% of clinics.

2) Concurrency / qps profile
- Baseline rps: 20–30 rps across the fleet (global), but concentrated spikes to 110–180 rps during intake waves in the morning.
- Long tail: occasional bursts up to 300 rps for short periods (30–90s) during system-wide batch imports.

3) Latency tolerance
- Clinically acceptable p95: <350ms is OK for triage flows; <250ms preferred for clinician-facing summary screens.

4) Cost target
- We are aiming for an all-in production cost <= $350 / 1M tokens. For pilot we can accept a bit higher while we tune batching and caching.

We'll confirm the CSV with exact request-length histograms by Tue 5/19. Also, happy to take your PoA draft and iterate.

Best,
Samir
Head of Product, Nimbus Health
samir@NimbusHealth.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: internal: quick notes on config knobs for Nimbus POC

Ava / Monica — internal quick hit list before we send the tuned PoA: 

- Model choice: recommend a 13B-ish open variant with 8k context to balance latency vs cost. Will provide comparisons in the pricing sheet.
- Batching window: start with 20ms continuous batching on dedicated nodes; if KV hit rate > 30% we can tighten to 12–15ms.
- Cache policy: prefix-level KV caching for 30% multi-turn; expire 30m by default.
- Autoscale policy: target CPU/GPU usage 60–70% for steady capacity, with immediate burst to shared hosted for +30–50% spikes (keeps reserved cost smaller).
- Instrumentation: add token-level cost breakdown in console and a per-route p95 heatmap.

I'll attach a quick sim (separate doc) with cost per 1M tokens across batch sizes 8/16/32 for their token-length profile.

-ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Samir — great, thanks for the clarifications. Confirming next steps and proposed PoC plan: 

1) PoC scope (3 weeks)- Week 0–1: ingest sample traffic (you provide histograms CSV) + verify prefix reuse and KV hit rates.- Week 2: tune batch window and autoscale thresholds; run 48-hour production-like soak to measure p95 and token-unit cost.- Week 3: present final econ and recommended Dedicated sizing + cost per 1M tokens.

2) Technical targets- Target pilot p95: <= 350ms; goal <= 300ms for clinician UI flows.- Expected tuned unit-econ: $320–$380 / 1M tokens with Dedicated + continuous batching (final depends on batch hit rates and average seq length).

3) Scheduling- Please send the token histogram CSV by Tue 5/19.- Tech sync: Wed 5/20 11:00 PT (I sent a calendar invite).

If that all sounds reasonable we'll finalize the PoA and send the DocuSign for a small reserved pilot commit to lock in the rate.

Thanks everyone — looking forward to the tech sync.

Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0124
