Because your target unit-econ is sub-$500/1M, we recommended the Dedicated pool (reserved capacity) with a mixing policy: primary dedicated for steady traffic + burst to shared hosted for spikes. That gets predictable cost while allowing you to meet the p95 latency target.

Please confirm the monthly token low/mid/high bands and the current procurement timeline so we can finalize the PoA.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0124

Attachment stubs: poa-proposal-draft.pdf (pricing + config), nimbus_poc_token_profile.csv (sample token histogram)


From: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 17:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Thanks Ava — useful recap. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Token volumes
- Pilot (month 1): ~12–18M tokens (we'll be routing ~10k–14k requests/day).
- Ramp (months 2–4): 40–75M tokens/month as usage spreads across clinics.
- Production target (6–12 months): 150–220M tokens/month if we onboard 60% of clinics.

2) Concurrency / qps profile
- Baseline rps: 20–30 rps across the fleet (global), but concentrated spikes to 110–180 rps during intake waves in the morning.
- Long tail: occasional bursts up to 300 rps for short periods (30–90s) during system-wide batch imports.

3) Latency tolerance
- Clinically acceptable p95: <350ms is OK for triage flows; <250ms preferred for clinician-facing summary screens.

4) Cost target
- We are aiming for an all-in production cost <= $350 / 1M tokens. For pilot we can accept a bit higher while we tune batching and caching.

We'll confirm the CSV with exact request-length histograms by Tue 5/19. Also, happy to take your PoA draft and iterate.

Best,
Samir
Head of Product, Nimbus Health
samir@NimbusHealth.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: internal: quick notes on config knobs for Nimbus POC

Ava / Monica — internal quick hit list before we send the tuned PoA: 

- Model choice: recommend a 13B-ish open variant with 8k context to balance latency vs cost. Will provide comparisons in the pricing sheet.
- Batching window: start with 20ms continuous batching on dedicated nodes; if KV hit rate > 30% we can tighten to 12–15ms.
- Cache policy: prefix-level KV caching for 30% multi-turn; expire 30m by default.
- Autoscale policy: target CPU/GPU usage 60–70% for steady capacity, with immediate burst to shared hosted for +30–50% spikes (keeps reserved cost smaller).
- Instrumentation: add token-level cost breakdown in console and a per-route p95 heatmap.

I'll attach a quick sim (separate doc) with cost per 1M tokens across batch sizes 8/16/32 for their token-length profile.

-ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@NimbusHealth.com>
Cc: Elena Park <elena.park@nimbushealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: scope & budget confirmation — batching & concurrency assumptions

Samir — great, thanks for the clarifications. Confirming next steps and proposed PoC plan: 

1) PoC scope (3 weeks)- Week 0–1: ingest sample traffic (you provide histograms CSV) + verify prefix reuse and KV hit rates.- Week 2: tune batch window and autoscale thresholds; run 48-hour production-like soak to measure p95 and token-unit cost.- Week 3: present final econ and recommended Dedicated sizing + cost per 1M tokens.

2) Technical targets- Target pilot p95: <= 350ms; goal <= 300ms for clinician UI flows.- Expected tuned unit-econ: $320–$380 / 1M tokens with Dedicated + continuous batching (final depends on batch hit rates and average seq length).

3) Scheduling- Please send the token histogram CSV by Tue 5/19.- Tech sync: Wed 5/20 11:00 PT (I sent a calendar invite).

If that all sounds reasonable we'll finalize the PoA and send the DocuSign for a small reserved pilot commit to lock in the rate.

Thanks everyone — looking forward to the tech sync.

Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0124

source.subject: Reserved capacity tenor & GPU cost contingency — Velociti AI
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sam Rios <sam@velociti.ai>, procurement@velociti.ai
Date: 2026-05-18T09:12:00Z
Subject: Reserved capacity tenor & GPU cost contingency — Velociti AI

Team / Sam,

Following our demo and sizing call last week, Velociti is verbally committing to a reserved GPU pool, but procurement asked for clarity on how we handle GPU market price swings in the contract. They want a disciplined approach to discounts/credits tied to market cost changes. I attached a draft SOW and a simple scenario workbook showing a few market moves.

Key asks for internal alignment:
- Recommended contract tenor (12 vs 24 months) and the commercial tradeoffs
- A simple contingency mechanism (e.g., tiered credits or monthly true-up) we can offer without exposing Redwood to open-ended downside
- Approval thresholds for discounts (who signs off at what % off list)

Sam / procurement: we want to give you a clear, implementable schedule so procurement can route approvals on their side. If helpful, we can propose a canary clause for the first 3 months.

Attachments: VEL-2317_Draft_SOW.pdf, GPU_Price_Scenarios.xlsx

Next steps: internal recommendations by EOD so I can send a proposal to Sam before EOD tomorrow.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sam Rios <sam@velociti.ai>, procurement@velociti.ai
Date: 2026-05-18T10:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity tenor & GPU cost contingency — Velociti AI

Ava / team — quick thoughts:

1) Tenor recommendation: aim for a 12-month firm term with an optional 12-month extension at pre-agreed rates. That lowers our tail risk and gives Velociti runway.

2) Contingency mechanism (simple, cap-limited):
   - Define baseline GPU cost index (monthly average of public spot/wholesale price)
   - If index rises >20% vs baseline for two consecutive months -> customer receives a service credit equal to X% of monthly committed spend (propose 10%) capped at 2 months per 12-mo period
   - If index falls >20% -> we consider downward pass-through at pre-agreed tiers (helps procurement)

3) Approval thresholds: discounts/credits <=10% can be approved by Sales Director (Ava), >10% up to 20% requires Finance (Marcus) sign-off, >20% needs VP/exec approval.

I attached a template credit schedule we use with smaller customers — we can adapt. If Marcus can share the downside P&L hit at 10%/15%/20% that will help finalize thresholds.

-Karthik
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sam Rios <sam@velociti.ai>, procurement@velociti.ai
Date: 2026-05-18T12:27:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity tenor & GPU cost contingency — Velociti AI

Ava / Karthik — did a quick sensitivity using the attached commit_model_v3.csv (summarized below). Numbers are illustrative but directionally accurate:

- Baseline: committed monthly spend = $180k (Velociti sizing)
- 10% market price increase -> incremental cost ~ $18k/month (we'd absorb vs credit if not capped)
- 10% credit to customer equals $18k; at 3 months that is $54k (material)

Recommendation:
- Keep credit cap per 12-mo window (e.g., 2 months max) and limit single-event exposure (prevents blowups if market spikes).
- Use a tiered approach (10% credit for 20–30% price move; 15% credit for 30–50% move; >50% -> automatic review + exec approval).

Operational note: we need a clear index provider and a monthly reconciliation process (finance + billing). Without that we can't reconcile credits reliably. I can own the reconciliation design but need product/ops commitment.

Attaching commit_model_v3.csv for the numbers. Approve routing rules and I'll break out P&L scenarios for legal/contract.

-Marcus
From: Sam Rios <sam@velociti.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@velociti.ai
Date: 2026-05-18T13:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity tenor & GPU cost contingency — Velociti AI

Ava / all,

Thanks for the quick turn. Procurement needs a deterministic schedule they can include in their purchase order. Our preference is:

- 12-month firm term + 12-month option
- A clear discount/credit table tied to a public GPU cost index (we use vendorSpotIndex.com)
- Cap and trigger definitions so our CFO can sign off

If you can send a redlineable table and a one-paragraph explanation of the reconciliation process, procurement can route legal and finance in parallel. We can also sign a time-limited canary for the first quarter if Redwood wants to limit long-term exposure.

Sam Rios
Head of Infrastructure, Velociti AI
sam@velociti.ai
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sam Rios <sam@velociti.ai>, procurement@velociti.ai
Date: 2026-05-18T15:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity tenor & GPU cost contingency — Velociti AI

Thanks everyone — good alignment. Action items I will take this afternoon:

1) Draft a one-page contingency table (tiered triggers, caps, index source) and attach as an exhibit to the SOW.
2) Propose approval flow: Ava (Sales) up to 10%; Marcus (Finance) 10–20%; Laura (Legal) looped for >15% language; VP Sales for anything >20%.
3) Share the redlineable SOW + exhibit with Sam and procurement by 09:00 PT tomorrow.

Marcus: please send the P&L scenarios by EOD so I can include the recommended caps. Karthik: please send the small-customer template if you still have it.

Will mark this as high-priority — thank you.

Regards,
Ava Chen
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

source.subject: Findly pilot: relevancy / reranker pilot checklist & next steps
messages:
From: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@findly.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: 

- Goal: replace heuristic reordering in our search results with a lightweight reranker for top 50 candidates; target improvement: +10–15% NDCG@10 and measurable reduction in user re-clicks.
- Data: we can provide ~2.5M query -> candidate lists (JSONL) with click labels for a rolling 90d window. Average candidates per query ~120, median 48.
- Security: we need an overview of how Redwood handles PII in logs, where embeddings are stored, and whether keys can be scoped to a VPC/private deployment.
- Integration asks (high level): SDK vs HTTP direct; auth options; support for structured output / tool-calling if we want to attach reranker signals back into our orchestration.

Attached: Findly_Rank_POC_requirements.pdf — sketch of data schema and acceptance criteria.

Next steps: could you share a short checklist of what your eng team will need (auth, sample ingestion path, routing for candidate generation vs rerank call, eval harness)? Also copying infra (infra@findly.com) — they had a couple of low-level questions on allowed IPs for dedicated endpoints.

Thanks,
Claire Huang
Head of Search, Findly

---
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 11:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Hi Claire — great call, appreciate the detail. High-level replies and an initial engineer checklist below; Ben/rishi/kim will scope and follow up with a proposed timeline.

Quick answers:
- Pembedding storage / PII: we support customer-controlled key management (KMS) for hosted and Private deployments; we can provide SOC 2 evidence and a short whitepaper on retention controls.
- Auth: API keys for quick POC, JWT/OIDC for integrations, and mTLS available for Dedicated/Private — we’ll include sample config.
- SDK vs HTTP: our SDK wraps retry/backoff, streaming, and structured output parsing; direct HTTP is fine for simple calls but SDK will speed up dev time.

Engineer checklist (what we’ll need from Findly):
1) Sample JSONL (2k rows) with candidate lists + click labels for offline eval.
2) Volume estimate (QPS) and peak token-length expectations.
3) Which fields you want returned with structured output / metadata (e.g., reranker_score, provenance_id).
4) Allowed IP ranges for any dedicated endpoints (if infra needs IP allowlisting).
5) Contact for infra + SRE for network details.

Ben/Rishi — would you pick this up and propose a pilot runbook (scoping, infra, eval metric baseline) by EOD Thu? Kimberly — can you propose acceptance criteria aligned to Findly’s product KPIs?

Regards,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference

On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 09:15 Claire Huang <claire@findly.com> wrote:
> Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: ...

---
From: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Findly Infra <infra@findly.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@findly.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 08:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Thanks Ava — infra contact (copied) is infra@findly.com. A couple of clarifications from our engineers: 

- Data size confirmation: the 2.5M queries is correct; we can export a 2k-row sample for you by EOD.
- Candidate generation: we currently return ~120 candidates from our retrieval step; for the pilot we plan to keep the same generator and call a reranker for top 50.
- Evaluation metrics: we will compute offline MRR, NDCG@10, and also run pairwise preference judgments for a ~500-query human eval tranche. Curious whether Redwood has recommended baselines and tooling for computing these (NDCG variants, bootstrap CI, A/B gating).
- Top_k behavior: we expect to test k in {10, 20, 50} and measure latency/cost tradeoffs; what's a typical latency impact for your neural reranker at top50?

Claire

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Claire / team — looping in a short technical plan and recommended defaults from the Redwood side. I'll attach a short playbook.

Top-level architecture (recommended):
- Candidate-gen service (Findly) => pass top-50 candidates to Redwood Reranker endpoint.
- Option A (fastest to POC): direct HTTP POST to Redwood ranking API, using API key scoped to a project; SDK not required but recommended.
- Option B (production): SDK + JWT/OIDC + regional routing to a Dedicated pool (if you need consistent latency).

Auth and networking:
- POC: API key in header (x-api-key). We log only metadata (request size/tokens); payload retention can be turned off per team.
- Production: OIDC tokens with short TTL + audience-based routing; mTLS for Dedicated endpoints.
- IP allowlist: we can provide stable egress IPs for Dedicated; for Hosted we recommend using region-based routing and fallback.

Reranker performance notes:
- At top_k=10: add ~8–12ms per request per token on an A100-like pod with our optimized kernels (depends on sequence length).
- At top_k=50: expect 3–4x increase in per-call compute vs top_k=10; batching helps (we can batch multiple rerank calls if QPS justifies).
- Cost estimate: we’ll model cost based on average candidate length and request rate; Ben will share a simple cost sheet.

Evaluation and metrics:
- Offline: recommend computing NDCG@10, MRR, and hit-rate@k. Use bootstrap CIs (1k resamples) for significance.
- Online: canary A/B with Bayesian bandit or fixed-split; we recommend starting with a 5% traffic canary for 2 weeks with predefined success criteria.

Suggested pilot run (milestones):
1) Week 0–1: sample ingest + offline baseline (Findly provides 2k sample)
2) Week 2: deploy POC reranker (top50) + offline eval & tuning (k sweep)
3) Week 3–4: 5% canary with telemetry (latency, NDCG delta, re-click rate)

Attachments: redwood_rerank_playbook.md (short ops checklist); I'll share the cost sheet separately.

- Goal: replace heuristic reordering in our search results with a lightweight reranker for top 50 candidates; target improvement: +10–15% NDCG@10 and measurable reduction in user re-clicks.
- Data: we can provide ~2.5M query -> candidate lists (JSONL) with click labels for a rolling 90d window. Average candidates per query ~120, median 48.
- Security: we need an overview of how Redwood handles PII in logs, where embeddings are stored, and whether keys can be scoped to a VPC/private deployment.
- Integration asks (high level): SDK vs HTTP direct; auth options; support for structured output / tool-calling if we want to attach reranker signals back into our orchestration.

Attached: Findly_Rank_POC_requirements.pdf — sketch of data schema and acceptance criteria.

Next steps: could you share a short checklist of what your eng team will need (auth, sample ingestion path, routing for candidate generation vs rerank call, eval harness)? Also copying infra (infra@findly.com) — they had a couple of low-level questions on allowed IPs for dedicated endpoints.

Claire Huang

source.subject: POC ownership, success criteria & decision escrow (post-call recap)
source.messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Cole <ethan.cole@stellarfin.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC ownership, success criteria & decision escrow (post-call recap)

Hi Ethan / team,

Thanks again for the call this morning — quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth. Please review and confirm owners where noted.

High-level objective:
- Validate that Redwood Inference can meet Stellar Financial's latency & throughput needs for your batch scoring and nearline chat assistant use cases, while maintaining auditability and data residency controls.

Proposed success criteria (short list):
1) Performance: average 95th pctl latency for chat < 250ms for 512-token requests in our staging traffic shape.
2) Throughput: sustained 1.5M tokens/day during peak batch windows without >5% error rate.
3) Quality: top-line prompt eval set F1 within 6% of baseline model (as agreed in eval doc).
4) Compliance: data ingestion and audit logs for trial are retained for 90 days and accessible to Stellar security team.

Evaluation plan & timeline (draft):
- Week 0 (this week): finalize SOW & RACI. (ACTION: Ethan confirm procurement contact)
- Week 1: infra onboarding + private VPC network handshake (Redwood infra lead + Stellar cloud ops)
- Weeks 2–3: sample data ingestion, prefix cache validation, baseline evals
- Weeks 4–5: stress/throughput run, latency tuning & quantization checks
- Week 6: handoff readout + decision (go/no-go)

Owner asks (proposed RACI entries):
- Infra (VPC, networking, capacity): Redwood Dedicated eng — OWNER: Ben Carter (Redwood) / RACI: Ben - R/A, Connor - C, Ethan - I
- App integration (client SDKs, ingestion pipeline): Stellar app eng — OWNER: Ethan Cole / RACI: Ethan - R/A, Kimberly - C, Connor - C
- Security & compliance (data residency, audit logs, evidence): Stellar security — OWNER: Sophia Martinez / RACI: Sophia - R/A, Mina - C, Ava - I
- Procurement / contracts: Stellar procurement — OWNER: procurement@stellarfin.com / RACI: procurement - R/A, Ben - C

Attachments: 2026-05-04_POC_SOW.pdf (PDF stub), 2026-05-04_RACI_proposal.xlsx (Excel stub)

Open questions for Stellar:
- Can your security team accept a Redwood-managed audit log export (S3) for the trial, or do you need an on-demand snapshot into your tenancy?
- Any mandatory vendor checklist items beyond SOC2 that will block infra onboarding?

Next steps I propose:
- Ethan to confirm security and procurement names (today)
- Redwood to update RACI and return RACI_v2 by EOD tomorrow
- Schedule infra kickoff (30–45m) once procurement gives green light

Thanks — short and pragmatic. If you want me to capture this into a mutual action plan in HubSpot, say the word and I’ll push a draft.

—
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192


From: Ethan Cole <ethan.cole@stellarfin.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sophia Martinez <sophia.martinez@stellarfin.com>, Procurement <procurement@stellarfin.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 11:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: POC ownership, success criteria & decision escrow (post-call recap)

Hi Ava — thanks for the clear recap. Confirming a few points from Stellar's side: we agree the success criteria broadly, but two asks before we can sign SOW:

1) Data residency: for the portion of PII we need the data to remain in our EU tenant — can Redwood support a region-limited trial node or provide a proof-of-delivery mechanism for audit logs?
2) Security evidence: Sophia will run the vendor checklist; she indicated we may need a short questionnaire on key management and retention controls (not full SOC2 evidence immediately).

Owners from our side:
- App owner: Ethan Cole (me)
- Security lead: Sophia Martinez (cc'd)
- Procurement: procurement@stellarfin.com (cc'd)

Procurement note: we generally need a short MA (mutual agreement) rather than a full enterprise contract for a 6-week trial. Procurement will confirm whether a PO or MA is required.

I’ll review the SOW and RACI and get back later today with any edits.

Best,
Ethan
VP, Platform Engineering | Stellar Financial
ethan.cole@stellarfin.com

> Hi Ethan / team,
> 
> Thanks again for the call this morning — quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth. Please review and confirm owners where noted.

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: FW: POC ownership, success criteria & decision escrow (post-call recap)

Team — forwarding Ethan's confirmation. I put Mina and Connor on because of the security + infra asks. Quick recommedation from my side (we'll refine):

Suggested internal owner assignments (Redwood side):
- Infra lead (networking, VPC setup, capacity planning): Ben Carter (owner) — primary contact for Stellar cloud ops.
- Solutions eng (ingestion, SDKs, prefix caching): Connor O'Brien — will run the onboarding scripts and do the KV cache smoke tests.
- Security liaison (evidence + questionnaire): Mina Haddad — will pickup the vendor checklist and draft a short KMS/retention note.
- AE (commercial / procurement questions): Ava Chen — will coordinate SOW & mutual agreement (MA) paperwork.

I’ve started a quick checklist in the Drive link — Connor, can you confirm the quick network prerequisites (eg IP allowlist + VPN options) by EOD Wednesday?

Ben
Senior Solutions Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Cole <ethan.cole@stellarfin.com>, Sophia Martinez <sophia.martinez@stellarfin.com>, Procurement <procurement@stellarfin.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC ownership, success criteria & decision escrow (post-call recap)

source.subject: MAP: Ramp & Pacing Playbook for Final Performance Eval
messages:
From: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@sage-analytics.com
Date: 2026-05-05T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. As we finalize the MAP for the POC close, the platform team asked for a short playbook that defines the ramp/pacing profiles we'll run and the pass/fail gating for TTFT, sustained throughput, and queue length under load. They want to understand how Redwood will validate burst vs steady-state behavior and any recommended rate-limit tuning to avoid head-of-line blocking.

Can you share a proposed schedule (dates/times), the exact ramp scripts you plan to run, and the acceptance thresholds your team will use? We need this to commit the final sign-off on 5/12.

TIA — Emma

Emma Price
Director, Platform Engineering | Sage Analytics
[Attachment: spec-snapshot.pdf (PDF)]

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-05T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Emma,

Appreciate the clear ask — attaching a compact playbook and ramp script. Summary here so it's in the thread:

Planned window: 2026-05-06 09:00–13:00 PT (warmup + 3 profiles) and 2026-05-07 10:00–14:00 PT (regression and final acceptance).
Profiles:
- Spike profile (sawtooth bursts): 30s ramp to 8x baseline QPS, hold 60s, drop to baseline; repeated 12x over 40 minutes to stress concurrency and observe queue shedding.
- Ramp-and-hold (steady-state): linear ramp from baseline to target over 5 minutes, hold for 30 minutes at each target (targets = 1x, 2x, 4x, 6x baseline throughput).
- Micro-burst series: 5 x 10s microbursts at 12x baseline to observe head-of-line blocking and KV-cache thrash.

Key metrics we will capture (token-level where possible):
- TTFT (time-to-first-token) median, p95, p99 by model & route
- Token/sec throughput (observed) and accepted QPS (ingress)
- Service queue depth and per-worker pending requests
- Error rate and 429s; origin of 429 (ingress vs internal queue drop)
- Cold vs warmed KV-cache behavior via prefix-hit tagging

Acceptance gating (pass/fail):
- TTFT: p95 <= 200ms, p99 <= 500ms for steady-state at the committed target; allowance for transient spikes during 30s bursts but p99 <= 800ms for bursts.
- Sustained throughput: observed token/sec within +/-15% of target for steady-hold windows (30m) without sustained queue growth.
- Queueing: service queue depth must stabilize after warmup; no sustained increase > 50% baseline for more than 5 minutes.

Rate-limit recommendations: windowed throttling at ingress per-customer with a short token-bucket to smooth microbursts; add an adaptive lane that allows brief overcommit for warmup but drops large bursts that cause queue blowups. We'll include exact bucket settings in ramp_script.yaml.

Attaching: ramp_script.yaml, pacing_profile_definitions.md, queueing_observability-checklist.pdf.

If the gate thresholds look OK, we'll lock the schedule and run a dry-run the morning of 5/6.

Thanks,
Ava

Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: ramp_script.yaml (YAML)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-05T12:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Quick technical notes from runtime side:

1) For burst profiles we should enable per-worker request queue caps and early drop with a distinct 429 code so we can separate ingress limiter hits from internal pressure drops. We'll tag dropped requests for traceability.
2) Set KV-cache warming: pre-seed prefixes for the longest prompt sets to reduce cold-TTFT variance. I'll attach a short snippet for the loader.
3) Observability: ensure token-level timings are exported to the tracing backend and we capture per-model kernel-switch counts — that'll help debug outliers.

Also: during the first spike run let's keep autoscaler off for deterministic results; we'll re-enable on 5/07 for end-to-end check.

-Karthik

[Attachment: kv_warm_loader.py (PY)]

From: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@sage-analytics.com
Date: 2026-05-06T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Thanks — schedule looks good. Two quick asks before the dry-run:
1) Can you add a short column in the results CSV that maps each sample to the MAP milestone (e.g., "warmup", "spike-1", "hold-4").
2) For the rate-limit tuning: can you propose two sets of token-bucket params (conservative and aggressive) so we can discuss tradeoffs with security/procurement?

We'll be ready for the dry-run at 09:00 PT.

—Emma

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-06T10:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Emma/Team — following the dry-run we've uploaded preliminary results to the shared drive and a compact CSV (pacing-results-20260507.csv). Observations:

- Dry-run spike series produced transient p99 TTFT ~740ms with a single 8-minute window where queue depth trended up; root cause was ingress burst smoothing not applied on their generator.
- Steady-state holds (2x and 4x) met the steady gating: p95 TTFT 120–180ms, p99 320–420ms, observed token/sec within +/-10% of target.
- Micro-bursts exposed a head-of-line effect for 1% of requests tied to an older kernel path (we flagged and pinned those workers in the run).

Actions:
- We'll apply the conservative token-bucket (params in the attached) and re-run the spike suite tomorrow 10:00 PT.
- We'll annotate the CSV with MAP milestone labels per Emma's request.

Next: confirm 05/07 10:00 for the acceptance run where we'll enable autoscaler and validate fallbacks.

-Marcus

[Attachment: pacing-results-20260507.csv (CSV)]

From: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@sage-analytics.com
Date: 2026-05-07T16:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Ramp & Pacing Playbook for Final Performance Eval — acceptance confirmation

Team — thanks for the quick turnaround. We've reviewed the annotated CSV and the conservative rate-limit run. The acceptance criteria are satisfied for the steady-state windows and the mitigations for the micro-burst head-of-line issue look reasonable. Sage will sign the MAP contingent on the following:

1) Redwood to provide final ramp_script.yaml and the two token-bucket param sets in a single doc.
2) A post-run summary with the final per-route p95/p99 TTFT and a short note on which workers were pinned and why.
3) A confirmation that autoscaler behavior (enabled in the 05/07 run) did not invalidate the steady-state gating.

If you can provide those by EOD 05/10 we'll finish procurement paperwork and target a production enablement on 06/01.

Emma

Emma Price
Director, Platform Engineering | Sage Analytics
[Attachment: procurement-req.docx (DOCX)]

They want to understand how Redwood will validate burst vs steady-state behavior and any recommended rate-limit tuning to avoid head-of-line blocking.

Can you share a proposed schedule (dates/times), the exact ramp scripts you plan to run, and the acceptance thresholds your team will use? We need this to commit the final sign-off on 5/12.

TIA — Emma

Emma Price
Director, Platform Engineering | Sage Analytics
[Attachment: spec-snapshot.pdf (PDF)]


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-05T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Emma,

Appreciate the clear ask — attaching a compact playbook and ramp script. Summary here so it's in the thread:

Planned window: 2026-05-06 09:00–13:00 PT (warmup + 3 profiles) and 2026-05-07 10:00–14:00 PT (regression and final acceptance).
Profiles:
- Spike profile (sawtooth bursts): 30s ramp to 8x baseline QPS, hold 60s, drop to baseline; repeated 12x over 40 minutes to stress concurrency and observe queue shedding.
- Ramp-and-hold (steady-state): linear ramp from baseline to target over 5 minutes, hold for 30 minutes at each target (targets = 1x, 2x, 4x, 6x baseline throughput).
- Micro-burst series: 5 x 10s microbursts at 12x baseline to observe head-of-line blocking and KV-cache thrash.

Key metrics we will capture (token-level where possible):
- TTFT (time-to-first-token) median, p95, p99 by model & route
- Token/sec throughput (observed) and accepted QPS (ingress)
- Service queue depth and per-worker pending requests
- Error rate and 429s; origin of 429 (ingress vs internal queue drop)
- Cold vs warmed KV-cache behavior via prefix-hit tagging

Acceptance gating (pass/fail):
- TTFT: p95 <= 200ms, p99 <= 500ms for steady-state at the committed target; allowance for transient spikes during 30s bursts but p99 <= 800ms for bursts.
- Sustained throughput: observed token/sec within +/-15% of target for steady-hold windows (30m) without sustained queue growth.
- Queueing: service queue depth must stabilize after warmup; no sustained increase > 50% baseline for more than 5 minutes.

Rate-limit recommendations: windowed throttling at ingress per-customer with a short token-bucket to smooth microbursts; add an adaptive lane that allows brief overcommit for warmup but drops large bursts that cause queue blowups. We'll include exact bucket settings in ramp_script.yaml.

Attaching: ramp_script.yaml, pacing_profile_definitions.md, queueing_observability-checklist.pdf.

If the gate thresholds look OK, we'll lock the schedule and run a dry-run the morning of 5/6.

Thanks,
Ava

Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: ramp_script.yaml (YAML)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-05T12:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Quick technical notes from runtime side:

1) For burst profiles we should enable per-worker request queue caps and early drop with a distinct 429 code so we can separate ingress limiter hits from internal pressure drops. We'll tag dropped requests for traceability.
2) Set KV-cache warming: pre-seed prefixes for the longest prompt sets to reduce cold-TTFT variance. I'll attach a short snippet for the loader.
3) Observability: ensure token-level timings are exported to the tracing backend and we capture per-model kernel-switch counts — that'll help debug outliers.

Also: during the first spike run let's keep autoscaler off for deterministic results; we'll re-enable on 5/07 for end-to-end check.

-Karthik

[Attachment: kv_warm_loader.py (PY)]

From: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@sage-analytics.com
Date: 2026-05-06T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Thanks — schedule looks good. Two quick asks before the dry-run:
1) Can you add a short column in the results CSV that maps each sample to the MAP milestone (e.g., \"warmup\", \"spike-1\", \"hold-4\").
2) For the rate-limit tuning: can you propose two sets of token-bucket params (conservative and aggressive) so we can discuss tradeoffs with security/procurement?

We'll be ready for the dry-run at 09:00 PT.

—Emma


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-06T10:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: final perf eval — clarifying pacing expectations

Emma/Team — following the dry-run we've uploaded preliminary results to the shared drive and a compact CSV (pacing-results-20260507.csv). Observations:

- Dry-run spike series produced transient p99 TTFT ~740ms with a single 8-minute window where queue depth trended up; root cause was ingress burst smoothing not applied on their generator.
- Steady-state holds (2x and 4x) met the steady gating: p95 TTFT 120–180ms, p99 320–420ms, observed token/sec within +/-10% of target.
- Micro-bursts exposed a head-of-line effect for 1% of requests tied to an older kernel path (we flagged and pinned those workers in the run).

Actions:
- We'll apply the conservative token-bucket (params in the attached) and re-run the spike suite tomorrow 10:00 PT.
- We'll annotate the CSV with MAP milestone labels per Emma's request.

Next: confirm 05/07 10:00 for the acceptance run where we'll enable autoscaler and validate fallbacks.

-Marcus

[Attachment: pacing-results-20260507.csv (CSV)]

From: Emma Price <emma@sage-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@sage-analytics.com
Date: 2026-05-07T16:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Ramp & Pacing Playbook for Final Performance Eval — acceptance confirmation

Team — thanks for the quick turnaround. We've reviewed the annotated CSV and the conservative rate-limit run. The acceptance criteria are satisfied for the steady-state windows and the mitigations for the micro-burst head-of-line issue look reasonable. Sage will sign the MAP contingent on the following:

1) Redwood to provide final ramp_script.yaml and the two token-bucket param sets in a single doc.
2) A post-run summary with the final per-route p95/p99 TTFT and a short note on which workers were pinned and why.
3) A confirmation that autoscaler behavior (enabled in the 05/07 run) did not invalidate the steady-state gating.

If you can provide those by EOD 05/10 we'll finish procurement paperwork and target a production enablement on 06/01.

Emma

Emma Price
Director, Platform Engineering | Sage Analytics
[Attachment: procurement-req.docx (DOCX)]

messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 2 May 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Draft onsite plan for Catalyst Health workshop — presenter & travel asks

Hi team,

Attaching a first-pass agenda for the onsite workshop with Catalyst Health (attached: draft_agenda_v0.2.pdf). This is for the week of May 18; client confirmed preference for an arrival evening before and a full day of sessions. High-level goals: align on integration roadmap, kickoff exec alignment, and surface performance/cost tradeoffs for their prioritized flows.

Please review and confirm the following by EOD Tuesday (May 5):
- Presenter ownership for each session (see proposed owners inline in the agenda).
- Any slides or pre-reads we need to share in the pre-read package (deadline: May 10).
- Travel preferences for Redwood presenters (air class, arrival/departure windows) so I can seed travel bookings.

Preliminary presenter assignments (please confirm or suggest edits):
- Exec kickoff (30m): Lauren Bishop
- Architecture & integration deep-dive (60m): Rishi Malhotra
- Inference cost & optimization (45m): Dev Patel
- Breakouts / office-hours (90m): Priyom Das + rotating leads

Other items: AV needs (projector + AirPlay/Mac adapters), whiteboard + sticky notes, and a sign-in sheet. I'm also attaching a travel_form.xlsx for people to fill passenger info / frequent flyer if needed.

Action items:
- Everyone: confirm presenter ownership and list slides you plan to bring.
- Priyom: can you start a shared slide repo and confirm speaker bios?
- Dev: flag any content that needs a live demo and whether we should bring a laptop or use the client's network.

Thanks — coming together tight but should be straightforward once owners lock in.

Best,
Ava Chen
APM, Customer Field Programs
Redwood Inference

Attachments: draft_agenda_v0.2.pdf, travel_form.xlsx

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft onsite plan for Catalyst Health workshop — presenter & travel asks

Thanks Ava — looks good. Quick updates inline:

- Exec kickoff (30m): I'll own the deck. I'll pull together a 3-slide executive summary (OKRs, risks, ask) and would like a one-page ROI snapshot from Dev showing current cost per inference and projected savings with our recommended changes.
- Architecture session: Rishi, can you also include a short migration plan for their staging environment? 45 min may be tight; suggest 60 min.
- Integration breakout: I propose we split the 90-min block into 60 min workshop + 30 min Q&A; Priyom will coordinate the workshop exercises.

Pre-reads I want to include:
1) One-pager: "Redwood in Production" (ops + SLA summary)
2) Integration checklist (what we need from Catalyst to run the integration)
3) Sample post-migration runbook (short)

Please upload pre-reads to the Drive folder by May 10. Priyom — can you compile presenter bios (short, 2-3 lines) for the agenda? We should confirm AV: we'll need HDMI + two Mac adapters, one secondary mic, and a conference phone. Also collect dietary restrictions for travel meals.

On travel: prefer to travel the night before; if flights arrive after 8pm, we'll reimburse Uber to the hotel. I'll coordinate with legal on any last-minute expense approvals if needed.

Thanks — looping in the items I mentioned.

—Lauren
Director, Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

On Sat, May 2, 2026 at 09:15 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> (See message above with draft agenda + travel form)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emily Parker <emily.parker@catalysthealth.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 14:45:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed agenda & travel details for May 19 Catalyst onsite

Hi Emily — great to be partnering on this. Per our call last week, attaching an updated agenda (final_agenda_v1.pdf) and presenter bios (presenter_bios.docx). We have penciled May 19 as the full-day workshop with an arrival evening prior on May 18.

High-level ask from your side: please confirm final attendee list and any dietary restrictions by May 10 so we can finalize hotel and catering. We also wanted to check if adding a short "integration roadmap" breakout after lunch would be useful — we can add a 30-minute cross-team session dedicated to next-step owners.

Logistics notes:
- Recommended arrival: evening May 18. We'll share a hotel block link once attendees confirm.
- Travel reimbursement: Redwood will follow the standard travel policy (economy plus for flights under 4 hours; business for >4 hours with approvals).
- AV: we plan to bring a projector; otherwise we'll use the room's equipment and provide adapters.

Attachments: final_agenda_v1.pdf, presenter_bios.docx

Looking forward — let us know any edits and the final headcount.

Best,
Ava Chen
APM, Customer Field Programs
Redwood Inference

---
Attached: final_agenda_v1.pdf (agenda), presenter_bios.docx (short bios)

From: Emily Parker <emily.parker@catalysthealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Brandon Liu <brandon.liu@catalysthealth.com>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed agenda & travel details for May 19 Catalyst onsite

Ava — thanks for sending this over and for the team’s work. Confirming the following from Catalyst:

- Final attendee list (8): Emily Parker (Dir. Product), Brandon Liu (VP Eng), Sarah Gomez (Lead SRE), Tom Keene (Integration PM), Alicia Tran (Data Eng), plus three ICs from the model ops and platform teams.
- Dietary restrictions: Tom (vegetarian), Alicia (gluten-free).

Request: Can we add a 30-minute breakout focused on the integration roadmap directly after lunch? Brandon wants a short session to align on owners and timelines. If possible, make the breakout discussion capture next-step owners and deliverables.

Also, can you confirm whether you’ll need internet access for any live demos (we prefer everything to run on our staging to avoid adding network exceptions). For the exec kickoff, Brandon would like a short demo of the cost comparison (if possible) but understands if you need to run a recording instead.

Thanks — we’ll provide a final headcount by May 10 and confirm hotel preferences.

Best,
Emily Parker
Director, Product
Catalyst Health

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 6 May 2026 16:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Catalyst Health onsite — final agenda v1.1 + action tracker

Team — consolidating after Emily’s notes. Attached is the revised agenda (final_agenda_v1.1.pdf) that adds a 30-minute "Integration roadmap" breakout after lunch and calls out confirmed attendees. Key decisions and owners below:

Decisions:
- Add 30-min integration roadmap breakout after lunch (owner: Rishi Malhotra).
- Exec kickoff: Lauren to own 3-slide summary + coordinate a short cost slide from Dev.
- Travel: attendees will arrive evening May 18; Priyom to confirm hotel block and rooming list by May 10.

Action tracker:
- Priyom Das: create shared slide repo, upload bios and final pre-reads (due May 10).
- Dev Patel: one-pager on cost comparison for exec kickoff (due May 9).
- Rishi Malhotra: detailed 60-min architecture + 30-min integration breakout plan & runbook excerpt (due May 11).
- Lauren Bishop: compile exec deck and finalize opening remarks (due May 11).
- Ava Chen: finalize travel bookings once Priyom shares rooming list and Emily confirms hotel link.

AV / logistics notes: bring two Mac HDMI adapters, one wireless presenter, and a backup laptop with the recorded demo. Dev — if you need access to Catalyst staging, please coordinates with Brandon and Sarah ahead of the visit.

I’ll upload final_agenda_v1.1.pdf to the Drive and share the link in HubSpot. Please add any remaining items to the shared checklist.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
APM, Customer Field Programs
Redwood Inference

Attachments: final_agenda_v1.1.pdf

- Presenter ownership for each session (see proposed owners inline in the agenda).
- Any slides or pre-reads we need to share in the pre-read package (deadline: May 10).

- Architecture & integration deep-dive (60m): Rishi Malhotra
- Inference cost & optimization (45m): Dev Patel
- Breakouts / office-hours (90m): Priyom Das + rotating leads

Other items: AV needs (projector + AirPlay/Mac adapters), whiteboard + sticky notes, and a sign-in sheet. I'm also attaching a travel_form.xlsx for people to fill passenger info / frequent flyer if needed.

Action items:

- Priyom: can you start a shared slide repo and confirm speaker bios?
- Dev: flag any content that needs a live demo and whether we should bring a laptop or use the client's network.

Thanks — coming together tight but should be straightforward once owners lock in.

Best,

Redwood Inference

On travel: prefer to travel the night before; if flights arrive after 8pm, we'll reimburse Uber to the hotel. I'll coordinate with legal on any last-minute expense approvals if needed.

source.subject: Pilot credit allocation and trial extension request - NovaForge
messages:
From: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@foundrylabs.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the quick product demo last week — the team at NovaForge (we're an accelerator cohort company) is excited about Redwood's latency and cost controls. We're preparing a 6-week evaluation across two internal apps and would like to request: 1) a 60-day trial extension on the hosted plan, and 2) a $10,000 promotional credit to cover pilot usage and a small production-like load.

High level:
- Goal: validate inference cost at 2k TPS over short burst loads, evaluate model variants, and run our prompt-eval suite.
- Timeline: start in early May; run for ~6 weeks.
- Key success metrics: < 250ms median latency and < $0.02/token average cost for baseline prompts.

I've attached our pilot plan and a short usage sketch. Can you let me know what information you need from us and the typical approval cadence for programs like this? If helpful, Elena from Foundry Labs (copied) can vouch for the accelerator process and timing.

Thanks,
Samir Gupta
LaunchPad Ventures (portfolio rep for NovaForge)
samir@launchpad.co
+1 415-555-0123

Attachment: NovaForge-Pilot-Plan.pdf (PDF, 1.2 MB)

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Samir — really appreciate the clear plan and the context from Elena.

Short answer: we can usually support a pilot extension + credits for accelerator-backed evaluations. To scope it correctly, can you share an estimated token volume per day (or peak QPS) and whether NovaForge needs dedicated capacity or hosted API? Also: are any of the workloads PII-sensitive (if so we can route to a Private trial).

If you can fill this short form it will speed the review: https://redwood.app/pilot-request-form (1–2 mins).

Looping in Ben Carter on sales and Lauren Bishop from Customer Success to help land the offer and MOU. Ben — do you have a customary credit band and conditional terms you prefer for accelerator pilots?

Next steps I suggest:
- Samir completes the form + usage estimate (I attached a small spreadsheet we use)
- Ben/CS propose a conditional credit allocation and milestone success criteria
- We issue a pilot MOU (DocuSign) and enable the extension once signed

Thanks — I'll track this in HubSpot and mark as P1 for accelerator pipeline.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0199

Attachment: NovaForge-usage-estimate.xlsx (XLSX, stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Ava / Lauren —

I reviewed the pilot plan and usage sketch. Recommendation for a clean, low-friction handoff:
- Offer $8,000 in promotional credits (vs. $10k requested) tied to a 60-day extension on the hosted plan.
- Conditions: single-account usage, usage cap of 50M tokens total for the pilot, and sign the pilot MOU within 10 business days.
- Success Milestone: if NovaForge hits the latency and cost targets within the 6-week run, we convert to a Standard trial with optional Dedicated evaluation (SLA terms to be discussed).

Rationale: the requested $10k is fine but $8k keeps our average program burn reasonable for cohort-backed pilots while still covering robust evaluation. If Foundry wants to underwrite any overages we can do $10k and note sponsor contribution.

I'll create the HubSpot deal (DEAL-2026-0147) and draft the MOU. Lauren — can CS prepare a short onboarding checklist and a workspace for testing?

Ben Carter
Sales, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

Related: https://app.redwood.com/hubspot/deals/DEAL-2026-0147
Attachment: Pilot-MOU-Draft.pdf (PDF, stub)

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>, Elena Morales <elena@foundrylabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 13:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Samir / Elena —

Good news: we've approved a conditional promotional allocation for NovaForge. Summary below (officials in HubSpot and MOU to follow):

Approved allocation
- Promotional credits: $8,000 USD applied to NovaForge account
- Trial extension: 60 days from activation
- Usage cap: 50,000,000 tokens (pilot aggregate)
- Conditions: signed pilot MOU (DocuSign) within 10 business days; adherence to single-account usage; normal security review (no PII in prompts unless Private trial requested)

Next steps
1) Samir / NovaForge sign the MOU: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aPilotMOUlink (we'll also route via DocuSign)
2) Lauren will schedule a 30-min onboarding call to provision test keys and a workspace (Lauren: please propose times)
3) Ben will log the deal and mark the promotional credit schedule in HubSpot (DEAL-2026-0147)

If Foundry prefers to sponsor additional overage coverage, Ben can take that in the deal notes and we can top to $10k. Otherwise, we'll start with $8k to get them through the evaluation.

Samir — does this work for NovaForge? If yes, I'll send the DocuSign packet and activate the extension as soon as the signed MOU returns.

Thanks all — looking forward to getting them started.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com

Quoted message: > From: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
> Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 09:02:00 -0700
> Subject: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension
>
> (original message quoted)

Attachments referenced: NovaForge-Pilot-Plan.pdf, NovaForge-usage-estimate.xlsx, Pilot-MOU-Draft.pdf

- Offer $8,000 in promotional credits (vs. $10k requested) tied to a 60-day extension on the hosted plan.
- Conditions: single-account usage, usage cap of 50M tokens total for the pilot, and sign the pilot MOU within 10 business days.
- Success Milestone: if NovaForge hits the latency and cost targets within the 6-week run, we convert to a Standard trial with optional Dedicated evaluation (SLA terms to be discussed).

Rationale: the requested $10k is fine but $8k keeps our average program burn reasonable for cohort-backed pilots while still covering robust evaluation. If Foundry wants to underwrite any overages we can do $10k and note sponsor contribution.

I'll create the HubSpot deal (DEAL-2026-0147) and draft the MOU. Lauren — can CS prepare a short onboarding checklist and a workspace for testing?

Ben Carter
Sales, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

Related: https://app.redwood.com/hubspot/deals/DEAL-2026-0147
Attachment: Pilot-MOU-Draft.pdf (PDF, stub)

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>, Elena Morales <elena@foundrylabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 13:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Samir / Elena —

Good news: we've approved a conditional promotional allocation for NovaForge. Summary below (officials in HubSpot and MOU to follow):

Approved allocation
- Promotional credits: $8,000 USD applied to NovaForge account
- Trial extension: 60 days from activation
- Usage cap: 50,000,000 tokens (pilot aggregate)
- Conditions: signed pilot MOU (DocuSign) within 10 business days; adherence to single-account usage; normal security review (no PII in prompts unless Private trial requested)

Next steps
1) Samir / NovaForge sign the MOU: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aPilotMOUlink (we'll also route via DocuSign)
2) Lauren will schedule a 30-min onboarding call to provision test keys and a workspace (Lauren: please propose times)
3) Ben will log the deal and mark the promotional credit schedule in HubSpot (DEAL-2026-0147)

If Foundry prefers to sponsor additional overage coverage, Ben can take that in the deal notes and we can top to $10k. Otherwise, we'll start with $8k to get them through the evaluation.

Samir — does this work for NovaForge? If yes, I'll send the DocuSign packet and activate the extension as soon as the signed MOU returns.

Thanks all — looking forward to getting them started.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

source.subject: QuickChat: volume profile, blended rate example & model premium clarifications
messages:
From: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency and routing controls look promising. We're scoping an initial production deployment and want to validate economics before we lock a commit. High level: QuickChat expects ~50M generated tokens / month after ramp (month 4+), with the following approximate mix: 60% chat (mainly streaming completion), 30% embeddings (vector store writes + similarity queries), 10% misc (reranking, short calls).

Questions:
- Can you provide a blended per-token example for that mix using both (a) standard Redwood models and (b) a scenario where we use the premium chat model for 25% of chat traffic?
- How do you treat embeddings pricing — per-token or per-vector? Any differences between standard vs premium embedding models?
- What commit tiers/discounts do you recommend for 50M/month vs 100M/month?
- Any trial or onboarding credits for the first 2 months while we ramp?

Attaching our internal usage projection for reference. Procurement is cc'd on follow-ups (SARA).

Attachments: [Attachment: QC-usage-projections.xlsx (xlsx)]

Thanks,
Ellis
Ellis Donovan
Head of Platform, QuickChat AI
ellis@quickchat.ai
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Hi Ellis — great to hear the demo was useful. I ran your projection through our pricing matrix and put together two illustrative scenarios below. These are ballpark rates to help you model TCO; I can produce a line-item quote once you confirm final model mix and commit length.

Scenario A — No commit (pay-as-you-go guidance):
- Standard chat (redwood-chat-standard): $0.00060 per generated token
- Premium chat (redwood-chat-xl): +40% uplift => $0.00084 per token
- Standard embeddings (redwood-embed-standard): $0.00025 per token-equivalent (we price embeddings per token processed during encode)
- Premium embeddings uplift: +60% => $0.00040 per token-equivalent

Scenario B — Committed 12-month Tier (50M tokens/month baseline) — list discounts applied: 12% off base per-token for total committed usage; model uplifts applied before discount. Example blended math (your mix: 60% chat standard, 30% embeddings standard, 10% premium chat):
- Weighted (no discount): 0.6*$0.00060 + 0.3*$0.00025 + 0.1*$0.00084 = $0.000522
- With 12% commit discount: $0.000522 * 0.88 = $0.000459

Notes / answers to your bullets:
- Embeddings: we bill per token encoded (not per vector); for typical QuickChat vector sizes you should expect the token-equivalent rate above. If you route writes vs query-heavy workloads we can split billing lines (writes vs queries) for clarity.
- Overage: billed at committed rate + 15% (i.e., slightly higher than your committed unit) unless we agree to a burst buffer.
- Trial credits: we can offer $15k in onboarding credits (applied to first two months) for a 12-month commit.

I've attached a short SOW and a simple blended-rate worksheet. If you want, I can build a 3-year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) model factoring in model premium share and expected ramp.

Attachments: [Attachment: QuickChat-SOW-draft.pdf], [Attachment: QC-usage-projections.xlsx (updated)]

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
From: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Patel <sara.procurement@quickchat.ai>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Ava — thanks for the clear math, that helps a lot. A couple of clarifying items before we share with our finance team:

- Can you confirm the premium uplift caps? If we commit to 50M/month but end up shifting 40% of chat to premium over time, will the 40% uplift be applied across the whole tier or only to the premium token subset?
- For embeddings: the token-equivalent model billing is fine, but can you produce an example that separates encode (ingest) vs query costs? That matters to our cost projection.
- SOC 2 / compliance: procurement wants a copy of your SOC 2 or point them to your compliance portal.
- Payment terms / invoice cadence: do you accept net-45 for enterprise and will the onboarding credit be applied before invoice?

Cc'ing our procurement lead Sara — Sara, feel free to add procurement questions.

Ellis
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 08:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — internal negotiation notes

Internal notes (do not forward) — suggested approach for QuickChat negotiation:

- Offer: 12-month commit at 50M/month baseline, 12% off list as Ava suggested. Include 2 months of onboarding credits ($15k) applied up-front.
- Premium uplift: cap at +35% for premium chat models for this deal (instead of 40%) to be more competitive; billing will be per-token split (only premium tokens charged at uplift).
- Embeddings: separate encode vs query lines; price encode at $0.00025 and queries at $0.00022 for committed customers (we can absorb some cost on queries).
- Overage: allow a 20% burst buffer monthly before overage at committed rate + 10% (helps the customer not get surprise bills during ramp).
- Margin impact: Rishi, note this reduces blended ASP by ~6 points at 50M baseline — approve or push back? If we need more margin, request a 24-month commit or raise discount to 10% and keep uplift at 40%.

I'll let Ava incorporate and send a formal offer.

Marissa
VP, Sales Operations
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>, Sara Patel <sara.procurement@quickchat.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Ellis / Sara — thanks for the quick back-and-forth. Based on internal alignment, here is a formalized offer for QuickChat AI: 

Summary — 12-month committed agreement (Tier B):
- Baseline commit: 50M generated tokens / month (measured as generated tokens + embeddings token-equivalents)
- Discount: 12% off list per-token rates for committed baseline
- Premium model uplift: +35% applied only to premium tokens (we will meter premium model usage separately)
- Embeddings split: encode (ingest) billed at $0.00025/token; query billed at $0.00022/token for committed usage
- Overage: 20% burst buffer; beyond buffer billed at committed per-token rate + 10%
- Onboarding credits: $15,000 applied to invoice(s) in months 1-2
- Payment terms: Net-45 (approved for enterprise accounts)

Illustrative blended math (your stated mix: 60% chat standard, 30% embeddings (10% encode / 20% query), 10% premium chat):
- No discount weighted: 0.6*$0.00060 + 0.3*$0.00025 + 0.1*(0.00060*1.35) = 0.6*0.00060 + 0.3*0.00025 + 0.1*0.00081 = 0.00036 + 0.000075 + 0.000081 = $0.000516
- After 12% commit discount: $0.000516 * 0.88 = $0.000454
- Effective monthly spend at baseline (50M tokens): 50,000,000 * $0.000454 = $22,700 per month (first-order estimate before applying onboarding credits)

Next steps:
1) I can push an initial DocuSign SOW if you confirm the 50M baseline and the premium share assumptions. DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/QuickChat-SOW
2) I'll include SOC 2 link and our compliance pack in the next email for Sara.
3) If you want a 24-month commitment, we can consider tightening the discount to 14% or extending the onboarding credit.

Attachments: [Attachment: QuickChat-SOW-draft.pdf], [Attachment: redwood-invoice-template.pdf]

Let me know if you'd like me to join a call with finance/procurement to walk through the invoice cadence and metering model.

Best regards,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: QuickChat-SOW-draft.pdf]
[Attachment: redwood-invoice-template.pdf]
